Further killings will grant you higher favour in the King's honour. |KingQ>[**Reward: 500 Gold (Subject to terms.)**]] <== It seems straightforward to you, but you're not exactly cut out for combat. |Kami>["We should consider it, Master $name. 500 gold is nothing to turn your nose up at."] He's very correct. Perhaps you should. [[Take the Quest.->MQ2]] [[Leave it for now.->InnQuests]]What catches your eye is a small piece of parchment. You step closer to read the contents. =><= (css:"border: 1px solid black")[**Calling anyone who can lend a helping hand.** Hello. I am the Mayor and leader of the village, **Streamdale**. We are suffering from the wrath of a Dragon who lives in the mountains behind our village. We would greatly appreciate any assistance is ridding the town of this beast. He is burning our crops. **Reward: 100 Gold**] <== This seems like a relatively straightforward quest, though you're not much of a Dragonslayer. |Kami>["Perhaps there are other ways to make the Dragon leave, other than killing it."] [[Accept the Quest.->SQ11]] [[Decline.->InnQuests]]You spot a rather fancier-looking piece of parchment and you can only assume that whoever wrote it has money. You take a closer look. =><= (css:"border: 1px solid black")[**Calling experienced Adventurers.** I am the Merchant **Axedull**, a travelling salesman who goes between towns, but primarily resides in Holstein. The Trade Route to and from **Wrenville** in the north is being revaged by a pack of wild wolves. Out attempts to remove them ourselves have put a few people in danger. I would greatly appreciate if anyone could clear them out, and you will be paid handsomely for your work. **Reward: 200 Gold**] <== Wolves seem like thing that you might be able to deal with, especially with Kami at your side. [[Accept the Quest.->SQ21]] [[Decline.->InnQuests]]You decide to take the quest, and you know to travel to Scarhaven. (if:$innsc is 0)[You approach the counter and stare up at the Innkeeper, a rather tall white bear, with an apron around his front. He looks particularly toned. "Welcome. The name's Dinus. What can I do for you?"](else:)[You approach Dinus, and he spots you coming. A grin spreads across his face and he leans over the counter, looking down at you. "$name, good to see you. What can I do for you?"] You ask Dinus about transporation to Scarhaven, and his mood sullies a little bit, when he realises you're off adventuring and doing mercenary work. But, he can't really blame you, with the amount of money that's at stake. "Well...you can take a carriage, or you can walk it. The former is going to take you a couple of days, whilst the latter will take you more like 3 or 4." That was some good advice, so you thank him. The options are in front of you, but you're aware that a carriage will cost you money, whilst the other will be free, but take longer. "...Just be careful, kid. Okay?" Dinus warns you of the dangers ahead, and you appreciate it. You step out and decide what you want to do. It's currently $DN time. (75 Gold)")[(set:$coin to $coin - 75)(set:$MQ2c to 1)(goto:"MQ3")]] (link:"Walk the distance.")[(set:$MQ2c to 2)(goto:"MQ3")](if:$MQ2c is 1)[You decide to pay for a carriage to Scarhaven, and considering this was a royal decree, a number of mercenaries seem to be taking the same route. After paying up the fee for passage, you clamber up into the back of a spacious carriage with Kami, and the two of you sit amongst various othe mercenaries. "You don't look ready to fight." One of the mercenaries aptly pointed out your rather plain garments. He's right, you're not really suited for combat. Perhaps you can change that when you get there. You had the opportunity to sleep twice during your journey, and since neither you nor Kami trusted the mercenaries around you, you took turns sleeping. You slept first, and Kami watched, and then you watched whilst Kami slept, his head over your legs. Occasionally, someone steps out into the carriage's driver's seat, and another returns to sleep. Eventually, the carriage trundles to a halt, and you realise that you must be at your destination. A number of mercenaries head out from the tarp that covers the end of the carriage, stepping out into the small village. After a moment, you and Kami follow them. [[Continue.->MQ4]]](elseif:$MQ2c is 2)[You decide to conserve your funds and walk to Scarhaven. You just hope that you'll make it in time to help fulfill the quest. You have no idea how long that parchment has been up, but considering it was still there, you think you have time. After getting a few directions from Dinus, you make haste for Scarhaven on foot, with nothing but your tunic and underwear, and a small satchel of food that Dinus had been kind enough to lend you. Kami walked alongside you, protecting you and scouting ahead when needed. You had to rest at least 3 or 4 times on your journey. Walking all day certainly helped you strengthen your muscles, but it was incredibly tiring, and you slept for long periods of time in between resting. Kami had tried to help you and keep you motivated as best you can, which you appreciated. You met a number of people on the road, and spoke to a couple of them. One of them was kind enough to give you bread and 20 coins for your journey. (set:$coin to $coin + 20) After what feels like forever, Scarhaven is finally in sight. You can't quite make out anything until you get a little closer. [[Continue.->MQ4]]]You are now standing right in the middle of Scarhaven. Whatever way you had taken to get here, it's now day time. The village is quiet. You can see that there's not much but for at least 20 or 30 houses, presumably with 3 people to a house. There seems to be a smithy at the far end, and you could make out someone hard at work. Other than that, there isn't much here, but for the rolling fields to your left, acres of land owned by the local farmers. To your right is what you suspect to be the cause of the village's troubles: a rather dense-looking patch of forest that had to at least spread out for a mile or more, but obviously didn't cover very much, for it lacked a spooky name. Not too far from the forest's edge, just on the side of the main travelling route, was a rather luxurious tent. The outside was painted red and white, with an entrance wide enough for two people to enter through. A flag was stuck into the ground nearby, the insignia clearly representing the King of Reliquim. You can only assume this is where you'll get 'further instructions'. This place does seem nice though. Were it under different circumstances, you might stay for a while. You make a note to return at a later date. [[Head into the tent.->MQ5]]You step into the tent, with Kami in tow, and all hell breaks loose. Immediately, nearby soldiers and mercenaries are drawing their weapons, pointing them directly in your direction. Specifically, they point them right at Kami, who bows his head and lets out a small snarl. "Boy! Is this your mongrel?!" Someone from the back called out, and you profusely inform them that Kami is your companion. They hesitate for a moment, before they slowly sheath their weapons again, relaxing a little. Despite that, the tension in the air is thick, and you quietly make your way past the seething stares of mercenaries and towards a table at the far end of the tent. "My apologies," The knight standing on the other side of the table lifts his visor, revealing a rugged canine face. "Given the nature of our charge, you can understand we're a little wary of your typical forest animal." You can understand, to a degree, though you were a little bit angry. Kami is look decidedly furious, but he's reigning in his restraint particularly well. You blow a small sigh and look to the man, who shoots you an apologetic grin. Already, Mercenaries are filing their way out of the tent as more arrive. Presumably, you're going to be given a run down along with a few others. "I, uh...I must say, you don't look fit for battle." The knight awkwardly pointed out, but he could tell from the look on your face that you wouldn't be deterred. He cleared his throat. "Let's begin, shall we?" [[Continue.->MQ52]]"This is rather simple, ladies." The Guard called out rather loudly, and you take a step back to fall in line with the other mercenaries standing around, as well as some other guards. "The forest just behind this tent is full of **ravenous animals.** That means they're going attack **on sight**. Your job is to eliminate them. Preferably, kill them. We don't know why they're acting up, but we've tried our best to deter them and they keep returning." "500 gold is given to anyone who can kill at least one. As proof of your kill, we'll need you to bring something back. //The head.// Trust me, people have done //much// worse for money, and I'm sure plenty of you need 500 gold." The guard's eyes linger on you for a moment, and you shudder at the thought of having to cut off a poor animal's head just to get money. Kami looks decidedly nervous, and you wonder if it's a good idea to even bring him with you. Someone could mistake him for a wild animal and attack him, which wouldn't be good, for sure. "Bring the head to me, you'll get your gold. The more heads you bring, the more I'll tally. It's not a competition, but the King will be sure to consider you for any other missions required. Alternatively, if any of you plan to make an honest life for yourself, this is a prefect recommendation." Once again, his eyes linger to you. He's desperately hinting at you, but you know what's at stake. You don't consider yourself a mercenary, but perhaps he's right, and maybe you should make a life for yourself rather than going around doing odd jobs. "That's it. Roll out." He gestured, and the mercenaries began to file out. Soon enough, you follow them, Kami in tow. The forest lies ahead. [[Better get moving.->Maze9]](if:$MazeE is 1)[You're back at the entrance. If this was intentional, then at least you know where you're going. If it wasn't, then...//well.// You try to think of where to go next. No point in leaving now, of course. You still have a job to do, and you've come this far. [[Go North.->Maze5]] [[Go East.->Maze10]] [[Go South.->Maze13]]](else:)[You're at the entrance to the maze of trees. You can only guess on the directions you need to go, so you quickly plot out a North, South, East, and West, just so you don't get lost. It's good thinking. You wish you had a compass, however. |Kami>["I just hope we don't come across any trouble, $name."] You do too. You plot where you're going, but you lack a map. You'll just have to play it by ear.(set:$MazeE to 1) [[Go North.->Maze5]] [[Go East.->Maze10]] [[Go South.->Maze13]]]A corner. You're honestly not surprised. This place kind of seems like a large square. You wonder if it were purposefully designed that way by nature itself. There's nothing noteworthy here. [[Go North.->Maze9]] [[Go East.->Maze14]]You find yourself in what you can best describe as the 'corner' of the woods, though that's hard to really say. Nevertheless, from what you can see, the trees end and change direction as a sharp angle. It's the closest to a corner that you'll ever get. (if:$inv contains "Strange Mushroom")[You have already picked the Mushroom up. You stare sadly down at the withered patch of shrooms before you.](else:)[In front of you lies a patch of Mushrooms, and you could have swore that one of them sparkled for just a moment. Maybe it might be wise to pick it up.] (if:$inv contains "Strange Mushroom")[<!--Do Nothing--!>](else:)[|Mush>[Pick up the Mushroom.](clickreplace:?Mush)[You pick up the strange Mushroom. Immediately, all the nearby mushrooms wilt and wither, yet the one in your hand remains intact. Weird.(set:$inv to $inv + (a:"Strange Mushroom"))]] [[Go East.->Maze2]] [[Go South.->Maze5]]This clearing seems totally void of any and all life, which surprises you a little. Then again, there's also very little room to move around. The forest is so //dense...// [[Go North.->Maze1]] [[Go East.->Maze6]] [[Go South.->Maze9]]You make your way deeper into the forest. You can hear people fighting all around you now. You have a feeling you're getting closer to the source of the problem. The air is practically crackling with some sort of energy, and you suspect Magic is at play, perhaps from both sides. [[Go North.->Maze6]] [[Go East.->Maze11]] [[Go South.->Maze14]] [[Go West.->Maze9]]You feel the power growing and growing as you move, and you feel as if you're getting closer now. Suddenly, something catches your eye. It seems to be a figure in the distance, and it's large. It kind of scares you, but it also seems like it's the one thing that's causing the problems. At least, that's the hunch you get from looking at it. It's hard to make out it's features, though. [[Approach it.->MQ6]] [[Go North.->Maze7]] [[Go East.->Maze12]] [[Go South.->Maze15]] [[Go West.->Maze10]]This clearing is remarkably empty, much like some others. You're starting to think that you'll never find an animal head to take back at this rate, though you don't exactly have anything to help you cut it's head off. [[Go North.->Maze8]] [[Go South.->Maze16]] [[Go West.->Maze11]]You keep moving. In front of you lies the body of a fallen mercenary. He looks like some sort of tiger, with black stripes all over his primarily orange and yellow furred body. He has a rather obvious large cut in his abdomen, rakes in 3 lines. It's rather disturbing. [[Go North.->Maze2]] [[Go East.->Maze7]] [[Go South.->Maze10]] [[Go West.->Maze5]]You feel as if you're getting closer now. You can hear barking and roaring in the distance, and it sends a chill down your spine. Kami whimpers beside you. He's scared too. You put a hand to his head and gently stroke his fur, and he calms down a little. [[Go North.->Maze3]] [[Go East.->Maze8]] [[Go South.->Maze11]] [[Go West.->Maze7]]You wander into a rather bland looking area of the forest, and you hear a rustling from the left of you. (if:$M8D is 1)[(if:$inv contains "Speedband")[You're standing in the clearing where you met Wyatt. You hope to god that he's okay. If he's not, you don't know what you'd do. He seems like such a nice guy. [[Go North.->Maze4]] [[Go South.->Maze12]] [[Go West.->Maze7]]](else:)[You're standing in the clearing where you met that blu-ish gray canine. You hope he's okay. This place seems rather dangerous, after all. [[Go North.->Maze4]] [[Go South.->Maze12]] [[Go West.->Maze7]]]](else:)[(if:(history:) contains "WyattSSE")[You're surprised to see Wyatt wander from the side through a few trees, his armour covered in blood and his blade drawn and ready. His eyes widen when he sees you, and he wrinkles his nose, getting in close. "The hell are you doing here, $name? There's something here...something dangerous. I saw it. You need to get out whilst you still can." You didn't know what to say to him, but it looked as if your expression was enough. He sighed, his hand resting on your shoulder. "I care about you, lil' guy. And your friend. Just be careful, okay? Running isn't cowardice, it's smart." He reaches into his satchel, and retrieves something. He holds it out to you and you take it, surprised to see that it's some sort of headband. "This is for good luck. A Wizard once told me it makes you faster. I don't use it much anymore, but...you can." You take it. You doubt you'll use it, but Kami might.(set:$KGH to it + (a:"Speedband")) You thank him, and he straightens up. He gives you one last look, before he turns and trudges off deep into the forest. He must be looking to clear out some more before you get hurt.(set:$M8D to 1) [[Go North.->Maze4]] [[Go South.->Maze12]] [[Go West.->Maze7]]](else:)[A blu-ish gray canine appears from between a few trees, and his eyes center on you as he enters the small clearing. His armour, a mixture of metal and leather, is covered in blood, and his blade is drawn and ready. "...You shouldn't be here, kid. This is a dangerous place, for you and your friend. Trust me, there's...something not right." He looks a little scared, to be honest. If he's scared, then you should also have every reason to be. You shudder a little. "Just be careful, okay? Get out when you can." He warned. With that, he turned, trudging off to take out more of the animals. Perhaps he's right. Maybe you should leave, but part of you thinks there's more at stake.(set:$M8D to 1) [[Go North.->Maze4]] [[Go South.->Maze12]] [[Go West.->Maze7]]]]This patch is relatively clear. You can't see anything nice to acquire, and there doesn't seem to be any animals, either. You can hear the clamouring of mercenaries and barking in the distance. [[Go East.->Maze3]] [[Go South.->Maze6]] [[Go West.->Maze1]](if:$M3D is 1)[You find your way back to this part of the forest somehow. The body of the wolf that had attacked you lay on the ground. The Dragonoid from before didn't even clean it up. You can't bear to look at it for long before you get sick. The blood is everywhere. [[Go East.->Maze4]] [[Go South.->Maze7]] [[Go West.->Maze2]]](else:)[You wander through a set of trees, when you hear a snarling to your left. You turn and cover your head in surprise, trying to defend yourself. Kami doesn't even have time to react, and you have no idea what came over you. There's a sharp yelp and a whine, and the sickening splatter of blood. Nothing hits you, and after a moment, you move your hands away from your face. A Mercenary stands there, his sword bathed in blood, and the head of a dark-furred Wolf at your feet. You wrinkle your nose and take a step back, and the Mercenary turns to grab the head. You notice a number of heads hanging from his belt. How he moves with that much weight, you don't know. You look up to his face, and the grizzly, serious maw of a Dragonoid stares down at you. "This is no place for a child. Go home." He grunts, and turns, heading in the direction that you marked as South. Just who was that guy? Perhaps he's right. Maybe you shouldn't be here.(set:$M3D to 1) [[Go East.->Maze4]] [[Go South.->Maze7]] [[Go West.->Maze2]]]You're in what appears to be another 'corner' of the forest. It's as if the whole thing is a square, or something. It's odd. There looks to be nothing here. [[Go South.->Maze8]] [[Go West.->Maze3]]You keep moving. This place looks relatively clear, though you can spot a number of dead bodies in the distance. Animals, thankfully, not mercenaries, but you can't help but feel a pang of sympathy. It's like a bloodbath out here. [[Go North.->Maze10]] [[Go East.->Maze15]] [[Go West.->Maze13]]You keep going through the forest, depsite feeling like you need to rest for a while. There are trees for miles, but you're making sure to stay on track, following a single straight path, with the help of Kami. However, your trail has brought you to a rather deserted looking camp. It looks as if some mercenaries were here earlier. The more you look, the more it scares you. There are a number of swords lying strewn on the floor, caked in blood. The single tent in the camp has a giant claw mark on it, and you can swear that you see a hand poking out of the tent's entrance. It's time to leave. [[Go North.->Maze11]] [[Go East.->Maze16]] [[Go West.->Maze14]]You're at another corner of this forest. You have a feeling that this thing is going to end up being a full circle if you go around the entire outside. There doesn't seem to be anything noteworthy here. [[Go North.->Maze12]] [[Go West.->Maze15]](enchant: ?u, (text-style:'underline')) |u>[Strange Mushroom] A peculiar Mushroom that you picked in a forest near Scarhaven. You have a feeling is bears some magical propety, because you can catch it sparkling every so often. However, you have no idea what it's used for.<br/>You begin to approach the figure, moving steadily closer and closer with each passing second. You can feel the air crackling with power, and it makes you slightly nauseous. Kami begins whimpering beside you, and that's when you know that something is wrong. There's something about this figure you're approaching. Something...evil. As you draw ever nearer, you notice it's massive. Notably, it's quadruped, and you can only gather that is must be some very, //very// large wolf. You linger by a tree and watch the beast for a moment. It seems to be staring down at, well, something...or //someone.// Your eyes latch onto another figure, one that looks suspiciously like a human. Suddenly, the figure's head snaps towards you. It sees you. You freeze on the spot and stare, wide-eyed, and it's eyes glow green. As if on cue, the large creature beside it turns in your direction. Its eyes glow a pure, malevolent red. [[Uh oh.->MQ7]]You know better than to linger. You turn, and run. Your heart is pounding. A ear-splitting roar erupts through the forest and you press your hands to your (if:$species is "Reptile" or "Dragon" or "Avian")[earholes](else:)[ears] as you run, trying to block out the noise, but it's so damn //loud.// Kami is right behind you as you run, and you briefly turn your head to look back. You see the large creature bounding towards you, faster than you can run. It's quick, and it's at least 4 times your size. Of course it's going to get you. There's no escaping it. As you run, your ankle catches something. You trip on a root and fall flat on your face amongst the forest leaves. Fearful for your life, you roll onto your back and scrabble backwards, too scared to think to just get up and keep running. Kami senses your impending danger, and he turns to fight the source head on. Unfortunately, the beast isn't alone. A number of wolfs from the surrounding area have joined in on the hunt, with the intent to snuff you out. There has to be at least 5 or 6 of them, including the behemoth of a Wolf standing in front of you. It's 2 versus 7, and you don't like those odds. The adrenaline is coursing through your system. You feel as if your heart is about to explode. You can feel something inside you: a churning momentum that's only getting stronger and stronger. You feel as if you can...//reach it//, somehow. [[Tap into it.->MQ8]]You imagine tapping into his power, and all of a sudden, your body hums with a power you've never felt before. Your skin prickles, and your heartbeat quickens to something beyond normal. Every sense is heigtened. You can smell the air, smell the //fear// in your companion's very body language. Each snarl and pant falls on your (if:$species is "Canine" or "Feline" or "Fox")[ears](else:)[earholes] as if they were right up against them. You can practically taste the palpable tension in the air, as well as the nectar of something sweet: //magic.// You feel a renewed confidence. Slowly, you curl your hands into fists and get to your feet, and Kami turns his head towards you. He seems to freeze at the sight of you, presumably because you might look odd. You stare down at your hands, watching in awe as the faint whisps of green mist emanate from your fingertips, curling around your arms. |Kami>["Master. Feel that energy. Carve it into what you seek. Twist it to your will."] Kami coaches you through this powerful feeling inside of you. What is it you seek? What is it you //want?// [[For your enemies to retreat.->MQ9]]You push all your will into what you want, and you feel the power within you shaping to your goal. You want your enemies to back off and leave. Ultimately, you would like more, but it was imperative that you put yours, and Kami's life before those goals, just for a moment. |Kami>["Yes, yes, Master! That's it. Now, push that energy outwards. It needs to envolope your foes..."] You did as he suggested, and you watched as the green mist around your arms and fingers intensifies. You felt your hands trembling, your knees shaking. The power at your fingertips was a lot for your young body to handle all at once, but you did your best to remain standing. You could feel the power in your fingertips, and you felt an uncomfortable pressure in your fingers. You concentrated, and flicked your fingers out. The simple action pushed your will forwards, shoving the magic from your body. It fired off in small, green, mist-like bombs, which then expanded and enveloped the party of 7 creatures before you. You watch as the mist envolopes them, and you feel your strength beginning to leave you. Your visions returns to normal, and you take in a large gulp of breath. Eventually, your knees buckle, and you fall onto them, panting harshly. You feel as if you've just run a marathon, but you can only assume the spell you just performed was powerful. [[Continue.->MQ10]]You watch with bated breath, hoping to god that the spell you cast was enough to do what you needed to. The snarling wolves slowly stop, one by one, their teeth sliding back and their stance changing to something a little more docile. The 6 lesser wolves all eventually look calm, but the snarling, beast of a wolf in the middle is taking his time to eventually calm down. After what feels like a nail-biting 5 minutes or so, his teeth finally pull back and he slowly raises his head, staring down at you. His red eyes fade into something similar to a sharp green. The mist surrounding them slowly fades, leaving the 7 of them calm, and serene. The large behemoth raises his head, and lets out an ear-piercing howl. You cover your (if:$species is "Canine" or "Feline" or "Fox")[ears](else:)[earholes] and wince a little from the sound, before it finally subsides. The sounds of fighting around you suddenly stops. You can only assume that this large creature stopped all the fighting, somehow. The large wolf takes one look at you, before it bows it's head, like some sort of nod of respect. With that, he turns, padding his back the way he came, and the 6 wolves turn to follow him. Your eyes meet Kami's, and he blinks at you. Suddenly, your vision starts the blur. The adrenaline is wearing off, slowly, and you suddenly feel weak. Eventually, it's too much to bear. You collapse. [[Continue.->MQ11]]You slowly begin to come to, and the first sight you're greeted to is the bold colours of red and white. Your brain quickly recognises it to be the roof of the tent on the outskirts of the forest itself. You slowly begin to sit up, and wish you hadn't. You have a pounding headache, and god, it feels as if your head is about to explode. You wince, and hear a whine to the right of you. Kami is sitting beside you, and he's clearly quite worried for your safety. Luckily, it seems some people are nearby. "Glad to see you're up." Someone spoke, and you slowly turn your head towards a lean-looking Feline, clad in blood-stained armour. He crouches down beside you, his face serious. "I saw what you did. That...magic. You probably stopped the entire forest from killing us all. There were way too many of them as it was." He ran a hand over his head, before grinning down at you. "I'm in your debt, child. If you're ever in Scarhaven, come find me. Ask the Blacksmith in town where **Frederik** is. That's me, by the way." You remember his name, and tell him yours. "$name? Alright. Get some rest. The troops are packing up, but I told them you saved us all. This is for you." He sets a hefty sack of coins by your side, before getting to his feet. He lingers for a moment, grinning down at you, before he gives you a salute. "We'll make a soldier of you yet, $name." With that, he turns, and you stare at his cape as he wanders out. The large cape bears the insignia of the King, and you can only guess that this Frederik fellow is high up in the ranks. You appreciate the gold, however, and count there to be eactly 500. (set:$coin to $coin + 500) Counting those coins gave you a bit of time to recuperate. You decide that you should start making your way back to Holstein. You're definitely taking a carriage this time, and it looks like there's a free one heading back. [[Get going.->MQ12]]The events of the forest have worried you a great deal. You sit in the carriage with the other mercenaries, who seem too eager to talk about how much they had killed to be paying attention to you, or Kami, sitting in the corner of the carriage. You convey your fears to Kami, and ask if he saw what you saw. |Kami>["I did, $name. This is not good news. A Magister using his magic for evil purposes...it could spell doom for all of Orbath."] Was it really that bad? You press for further information, but Kami is happily ready to give it, if you ask the question. |Kami>["A magister wields a lot of power at their fingertips, and you would be surprised what one can do with creatures such as Dragons under their control."] You hadn't thought of it that way. If there was a Magister out there with the power of Dragons at his fingertips, willing to raid and destroy villages as he wished, then it could spell disaster. He could do anything, maybe even spark a war if he played it right. |Kami>["We should be wary. Should the Council hear about it, they might seek us out, as we are...of the same cloth, so to speak."] Perhaps he was right. If they were after another Magister, it would make sense to track his whereabouts with someone of a similar magical 'signature'. You're not sure how you knew that information. You decided to sleep away the rest of your fears, and Kami curled up beside you. You were glad to have him. Were it not for Kami, you might not even be here. You think that some of the people you know a little too fondly might be more receptive of you now.(set:$MQ1D to 1)(set:$Spells to it + (a:"Calm")) [[Complete the Quest.->Hub]]You decide to take the quest, and you know that you're bound for Streamdale. You have two choices ahead of you. Walk the distance, or take a carriage. Though, you have no idea how much it will cost to take a carriage, nor do you know far Streamdale is from the capital. With that in mind, you ask the Innkeeper. (if:$innsc is 0)[You approach the Innkeeper, a rather burly, muscular-built bear with an apron over his front. He smiles down at you as you approach. "Hey there, kid. Name's Dinus. What can I do for you?" You ask him, rather clearly, if he knows how far Streamdale is from the capital. "Streamdale? Uh, it's gotta be at least 3 days travel by carriage, more if you walk. You're not thinking of taking on that Dragon, are you? Kid, a Dragon will eat you for breakfast."](else:)[You wave over to Dinus, who gestures you closer, and he grins down at you as he leans over the counter. "$name! Good to see you. What's up?" You ask him if he knows how far Streamdale is from the capital. "Quite far. At least 3 days travel by carriage. It's near the mountains so it's bound to be...wait...are you going to take on that Dragon? Kid, that Dragon will eat you for breakfast."] You appreciate the sentiment, but you assure him you know what you're doing. He looks worried for you, but he doesn't press the matter. You thank him and retreat, heading out of the Inn and out into the square. Your choices are clear. (if:$coin is <75)[You can't afford a carriage](else:)[(link:"Take a Carriage. (75 gold.)")[(set:$coin to $coin - 75)(set:$SQ11c to 1)(goto:"SQ12")]] (link:"Walk the distance.")[(set:$SQ11c to 2)(goto:"SQ12")](if:$SQ11c is 1)[You take a carriage to Streamdale. The carriage is relatively empty, giving you ample time to figure out how you might deal with the Dragon. Naturally, you ask Kami for assistance. (if:$Spells contains "Calm")[|Kami>["Well, Master, since you know a basic calming spell, perhaps you can use that on the Dragon to calm him down, and then perhaps broker a deal."]](elseif:$inv contains "Magister's Dirk")[|Kami>["Since you have the Magister's Dirk, Master, perhaps you can use it to calm the Dragon down and then broker a deal with it."]](else:)[|Kami>["I honestly do not know master. Without the power needed, we have no way to successfully calm the Dragon down if you anger it. But...perhaps it will not be so violent towards you.]] You're honestly not sure what you're going to do, but you hope to god that you won't end up a Dragon's lunch due to your own cockiness. The carriage ride is long, and you sleep most of it off. After all, there's not much to do other than talk, and the other Mercenaries in the carriage aren't much of a talker. Eventually, Streamdale lies in the distance, and you blow a sigh of relief. [[Continue.->SQ13]]](elseif:$SQ11c is 2)[The walk is going to be painful, but consdering you didn't choose the carriage, you simply have to 'make your bed, and lie in it', as it were. Naturally, on your journey to Streamdale, you ask Kami's opinion of the quest. (if:$Spells contains "Calm")[|Kami>["Well, Master, since you know a basic calming spell, perhaps you can use that on the Dragon to calm him down, and then perhaps broker a deal."]](elseif:$inv contains "Magister's Dirk")[|Kami>["Since you have the Magister's Dirk, Master, perhaps you can use it to calm the Dragon down and then broker a deal with it."]](else:)[|Kami>["I honestly do not know master. Without the power needed, we have no way to successfully calm the Dragon down if you anger it. But...perhaps it will not be so violent towards you.]] He could be right. You're honestly not sure, and it looks as if you might have to play it by ear otherwise. With a sigh, you continue your journey. It took your 4 days to reach Steamdale, and it was an exhausting trip. [[Continue.->SQ13]]]You can easily see the damage that the Dragon has caused the minute you enter the town, and it doesn't look good. Smoke billows up from the fields to your right, and it's clear the Dragon has been at them. You can see the mountains in the distance, although you can't seem to find any kind of holes or cave entrances from here. As you and the other Mercenaries loiter around the middle of town, a rather overweight Ratman comes to greet you, dressed in lavish clothing. However, he does look mildly scared, and his clothing is slightly skewed. You can only guess that he's the mayor of the village, and you're soon confirmed of that fact. "Hello, everyone." The Ratman wheezed a little. "Thank you for coming. I'm the Mayor of the town, Poisant Uluémere." What a strange name. "The Dragon lies in the mountains behind you," He continued, hunching over a little before eventually straightening up. "I hope you can deal with it when even our own knights were unable to. We have a carriage to take you as close to the Dragon's cave as we can." You appreciate that, and you hear a murmur of gratitude from the other Mercenaries, too. [[Continue.->SQ14]]You, and a number of others, all bundle yourself into a carriage, with the intent to head up the mountain and face the Dragon head on. The carriage ride took a few hours, which is short in comparison to how long it took to get to Streamdale in the first place. The carriage makes its way up various steep inclines, with horses pulling the cart. Kami sits beside you, looking as calm as ever. You can notice a few serious stares from the other Mercenaries on the cart, and you can only guess that they're not taking you seriously. Perhaps they think you're too young for this sort of thing, or ill prepared. They'd be right: all you have is a tunic and underwear, and not much else. The carriage trundles to a halt in what feels like the middle of nowhere, and the carriage driver turns to address you all. "The cave is a little ways ahead. The path becomes too narrow from here." That makes sense. With the others, you disembark and begin on a walk to the Dragon's cave. You wind yourself through various narrow walkways and steep paths until you're standing at the foot of an increadibly large cave mouth. You have no idea how you couldn't see it from the village, and when you look down, you realise why: you're above the clouds. [[Continue.->SQ15]](enchant: ?Dragon, (text-style:'smear') + (text-style:'rumble') + (text-style:'bold') + (text-colour: #620000))One of the Mercenaries goes first, and you hover at the entrance to the cave mouth, watching him walk into the darkness. There's an eerie silence as you, and the other mercenaries, listen for any noise. Suddenly, you hear a blood-curdling cry, and the Mercenary is suddenly propelled out of the cave. He soars through the air and plummets off of the cliff, falling to his doom and dissappearing beneath the clouds. Your blood goes cold and you can the fear bubbling up in your throat. Much like you, the other Mercenaries are not so willing to just go ahead and enter the cave, but one is brave enough to fire a warning shot. He draws his bow and fires it into the darkness, and you hear a high-pitched, quiet yelp. It sounds like the cry of someone rather young. Then, you heard a deep, grumbling growl. Whatever he hit, it pissed off the Dragon, for sure. A large, red-scaled claw came out of the cave, at least the size of your entire body, and it whacked hard against the mercenary's side. He was sent sailing off the cliff like the others, and the claw came back around, missing you by inches and swiping against the Mercenary next to you. Your eyes widen as you stare at the large cuts in the man's body, and you cover your mouth in horror as his entire upper half slides off of his body. He's now in two pieces. You feel sick. That large, clawed hand reaches towards you, and you wince, thinking that you're going to die. The hand hovers over your body for a moment, as if ready to squish you at any moment, before there's a quiet rumbling sound. |Dragon>["..Magister..."] [[Continue.->SQ16]](enchant: ?Dragon, (text-style:'smear') + (text-style:'bold') + (text-colour: #620000))The Dragon knows you. Or rather, he knows //what you are//. That's mildly disturbing. You hesitate at the entrance to the cave. No other Mercenaries are with you. You'd be on your own, essentially. |Dragon>["Don't keep me waiting."] With a nervous gulp, you step forwards into the cave, and the darkness envelopes you. You're practicaly walking in darkness, unable to see. But then, something catches your vision and glows in the darkness: a pair of bright, yellow eyes, looming above you, staring into your soul. You feel afraid, and Kami snarls next to you. Suddenly, there is a puff of smoke, before a billow of flames erupts over the roof of the cave. You shield yourself, expecting to be scorched, but you're surprised to find no burns on your body and $skin. You look up to see that the entire roof of the cave has been illuminated by the flames. They must have caught onto something, because the entire roof is glowing with what looks to be jewels and rocks. When you finally lower your head, you can see what the problem is. [[Continue.->SQ17]](enchant: ?Dragon, (text-style:'smear') + (text-style:'bold') + (text-colour: #620000))There's not just one Dragon here, there's 4. Granted, only one of them is an adult. It doesn't take much to realise that the 3 much shorter Dragons are nothing but whelps, and they're clearly quite excited to meet you, albeit wary. The three whelps, which have to be at least 6 foot tall on all fours, nervously wiggle left and right next to each other, standing near the larger Dragon and tentatively sniffing the air. |Dragon>["I can see from the look on your face that you now realise why I refuse to leave. These are my children. Their mother left a long time ago. They need consistent care."] It makes sense to you. This Dragon is just looking after it's children, and having the village send mercenaries up to attack them is bound to ruffle a few feathers, so to speak. That doesn't explain why the crops are being torched, however, so you hesitantly ask. |Dragon>["They started it. The minute we settled down here, they sent a Knight to kill us. We risk everything by being here, I know, but still..."] Well... Now you can see why he'd be angry. This is quite the conundrum. |Dragon>["Magister. Like many before you, you value life. Do not be a monster."] This is a tough one. [[Offer some sort of deal.->SQ1SS1]] (link:"Try to persuade them to leave. (50% chance)")[(set:$SQ17c to 1)(goto:"SQ18")] (link:"Offer to talk to the village.")[(set:$SQ17c to 2)(goto:"SQ18")](enchant: ?Dragon, (text-style:'smear') + (text-style:'bold') + (text-colour: #620000))You have no idea why you suggested it, but you make an offer. A rather lewd one, in fact. You tell the large Dragon that you'll perform some sexual favours, in exchange for them leaving and finding somewhere else to live. The Dragon seems to consider it for a moment, before he blows out a rather large huff through his nostrils. Kami looks decided resigned to the idea, though he's clearly a little jealous. |Dragon>["My children are coming into the cusp of their adulthood, in more ways than one. If you can sate them, then perhaps I can consider relocation."] It's a fine deal, though you're a little nervous about 3 relatively large Dragon Whelps going to town on you. You have no idea what they'll do to you. Despite that, you and the Dragon seem to agree, and he makes a gesture to his offspring, letting them do as they wished. The first to come forward was the middle of the trio, and he seemed rather playful, nosing and sliding his snout into your side, circling around you so that he could see the extent of your body. After watching their sibling enjoying the new company, the other two eventually joined in on the fun, and the three of them began to circle around you like some sort of ritual, sniffing and nuzzling you all over, from your feet to your stomach, all the way up to your neck and face. You felt a little overwhelemed, to be honest. Then, their tact changed. One broke off from the group to greet the smaller canine, lowering itself down to Kami's level and bobbing it's head up and down in greeting. Kami looked as if he was caught off-guard for a moment, before inevitably greeting the Whelp, who seemed purely innocent. [[Continue.->SQ1SS2]](enchant: ?Dragon, (text-style:'smear') + (text-style:'bold') + (text-colour: #620000))(if:$SQ17c is 1)[(set:$DP1 to (random:1,2))(if:$DP1 is 1)[You make some very good points to the Dragon. You point out that despite what he does to try and win back the favour of the village, they will continue to send Mercenaries after them, and the continued stress and disruption would be harmful to his children. You insist that he'd be better off looking for a more quieter spot. |Dragon>["...Perhaps you are right, Magister. My stubbornness in staying in this cave only puts my children in danger. Maybe it's best if we find someplace else. Go to your village. Tell them I am going to leave, and give them this."] The Dragon reaches up with his massive hand and plucks a scale from his side, giving it to you. It's about as big as your torso, and weighs a ton, too. You thank him. |Dragon>["I hope you do not stray too far from the beaten path, Magister."] You have a feeling he knows more than he is letting on, but he waves a large hand in dismissal, a rumble escaping his throat. With that, you leave.(set:$SQ1E to 1) [[Continue.->SQ19]]](elseif:$DP1 is 2)[You try your best to make some saliant points to the Dragon, but he does not appear too pleased with what you're trying to convey. |Dragon>["I refuse. Go back to that village and tell them the error of their ways. I will not attack their fields if they leave my offspring alone."] It looks like you have no choice in the matter. You tried your best.(set:$SQ1E to 2) [[Continue.->SQ19]]]](elseif:$SQ17c is 2)[You make a surprising offer, suggesting that you go down to the Village itself, and tell them of how nice the Dragon is, and how imperative it is that he stay where he is, with his children. Perhaps the Village don't know he has offspring. The Dragon, somehow, finds this amusing. |Dragon>["Very well, Magister. Go down to those unruly creatures and tell them I will not harm them, or damage their propety, if they leave my children alone."] You agree. With that, you turn, leaving the cave in the hopes that the village might listen.(set:$SQ1E to 2) [[Coninue.->SQ19]]](if:$SQ1E is 1)[You make your way out of the cave, Scale in hand, and make you way back down the beaten path to the carriage that's waiting for you.](elseif:$SQ1E is 2)[You make your way out of the cave, dejected, and make you way around the beaten path back to the carriage.] The cart is waiting for you, as is the driver, having already turned the horses around. He cranes his neck towards you, and glances behind you, frowning. "...Did they not make it?" You shake your head, (if:$SQ1E is 1)[but you hold the scale up in your hands, showing it to the man. He grins at you in delight.](elseif:$SQ1E is 2)[and you hang your head, knowing there was nothing to be done. The man grimaces.] You hop aboard the cart and trundle your way back down to Streamdale, where the Mayor waits for you with bated breath in the middle of town. Thankfully, he didn't call the entire village to come and see, just in case you failed in your mission, as did the others. "Ah...I see the others didn't make it back," The Mayor grimaces and hangs his head, before looking at you with hope. "Good news, I hope?" (if:$SQ1E is 1)[(link:"Show the Mayor the scale.")[(set:$SQ1EE to 1)(goto:"SQ1E")]](else:)[<!--Do nothing--!>] (link:"Try to persuade the Mayor. (50% Chance)")[(set:$SQ1EE to 2)(goto:"SQ1E")] (link:"Tell the Mayor they won't leave.")[(set:$SQ1EE to 3)(goto:"SQ1E")]However, it was becoming clear that they had other intentions. You began to feel a snout sliding down against your backside, nuzzling against your $skin and biting against the waistline of your underwear. For whelps, they were surprisingly cunning and dexterous, and you felt your underwear sliding down past your knees. One of them circled to stand in front of you, and you watch in surprise as it's head bows down between your legs, tucking underneath the hem of your tunic and nuzzling against your $fcock. You lean forwards a little and let out a shaky exhale as you feel a large, wet tongue sliding against your $fcock.
