The whelp lets out heavy pants of pleasure, thrusting into your hand until he, too, is cumming, shooting ropes of cum over your front and splattering your face, as well as hanging from the other whelp's underside. [[Continue.->SQ1SSE]](enchant: ?Dragon, (text-style:'smear') + (text-style:'bold') + (text-colour: #620000))That was a rather interesting experience, to say the least, and you're stuck for the time being. Luckily for you, Dragons deflate a lot quicker than canines do, and after a couple of minutes of hanging from the whelp's cock, the knot slips out of you, leaving you slightly gaping and used. You're practically covered in cum, but the whelps seem particularly satisfied, and they curl up around you, nuzzling and cuddling into your body. |Dragon>["It seems they've taken a liking to you."] You forgot that the Dragon had been watching you the entire time. You're not sure what's worse: whoring yourself out for underage Dragon cubs, or doing so whilst the father watches you do it. Either way, it's mildly disturbing. As you try to find the strength to stand, a large hand lowers down to you. In between two claws is a shiny red scale, and you take it in your hands. It's about as big as your torso, and heavy, too. |Dragon>["Take this scale as proof that I will be leaving this place. Give me a couple of days, and I'll leave this village in peace."] You thank him, shakily getting to your feet with the Scale in hand, or rather, two hands. You begin to make your way out as the Dragons croon after you, hoping you might return, but you'll likely not see them again. Kami catches up to you. |Kami>["I don't think I'll walk right ever again."] (link:"Continue.")[(set:$SQ1E to 1)(goto:"SQ19")](if:$SQ1EE is 1)[You have good news. You hold up the Scale for the Mayor to see, and the Mayor stares down at the large, shiny scale with delight, taking it in his hands and hefting it closer towards him. You quickly tell him that the Dragons will leave within a couple of days and leave his village in peace. (if:(history:) contains "SQ1SSE")[The Mayor claps his hands in delight, but he looks down at your cum-stained clothes with a little bit of disgust.](else:)[The Mayor eagerly claps his hands in delight, looking utterly relieved.] "Thank you, brave Warrior. I'm sure it took a great deal to deal with the Dragon." The Mayor acted as if the Dragon was a menace, and you had a feeling that it might cause an argument if you explained that the Dragon was only seeking to protect it's children. "Thank you so, so much. Here, child. The gold, as promised. I'm sorry it's not much." The Mayor hands you a small pouch of coins, and you thank him, taking a quick bow. (set:$coin to $coin + 100) He shakes you hand feverently and takes a bow to you in return. "Now, I must let the village know. Ask the carriage driver on the side of town to take you home, he'll do it free of charge." That was rather generous of him, and you take him up on the offer, a wide smile on your face. |Kami>[I feel bad for those poor children, but at least the village will be safe, for a while.] [[Complete the Quest.->Hub]]](elseif:$SQ1EE is 2)[(set:$MP1 to (random:1,2))(if:$MP1 is 1)[You speak to the Mayor privately, making some rather clear points, and judgements, about the situation. You tell him outright that he started the war in the first place with the Dragon, and the Mayor grimaces at you as you accuse him. You rightly point out that the Dragon has offspring that require stability, and he is only protecting them. You finally insist that if he stops sending mercenaries up to kill the Dragon, then he won't face any difficulties from the Dragon in the future. The Mayor thinks on it for a moment, and you can tell he's conflicted. "...Alright, fine," The Mayor waves a hand and sighs. "We'll try to leave him be as best as we can. At least our crops will be saved for the next harvest." You nod, and thank him for being compassionate. He puffs out his chest at the compliment, before he looks mildly deflated. "The Town won't be happy, but I'll try. Thank you for your aid, young one. I suppose the reward is yours, since no-one else made it back. Ask the carriage rider on the edge of town to take you home."(set:$coin to $coin + 100) He hands you a small pouch of coins, and you shake his hand. You feel kind of bad for him, but sometimes you have to make sacrifices and compromises. You begin to make your way to the carriage, with Kami in tow. |Kami>["You did the right thing, Master."] [[Complete the Quest.->Hub]]](elseif:$MP1 is 2)[You try to reason with the Mayor and make very saliant points about the Dragon's predicament, as well as their own. You can see the Mayor is torn, but it's not enough. He runs a hand through his fur and sighs. "I'm sorry, child, but it cannot be done. It provides no comfort to the villagers. What happens when those Dragon offspring get older? They'll come out of there in a rebellious phase and destroy the village. That inevitable doom will make everyone go mad." "I'm sorry," He puts a hand to your shoulder, and you understand. "Obviously, no reward can be given because you did not solve our problem, but the carriage driver on the edge of town will take you home." It's generous, considering the circumstances, and you thank him. He walks off with a dejected demeanour, and you do the same, heading towards the cart. |Kami>["You did the best you could, Master."] [[Complete the Quest.->Hub]]]](elseif:$SQ1EE is 3)[You had put in a lot of effort, but it was regretfully all for nought. Knowing that you might not be able to successfully persuade the Mayor to leave the Dragons alone, you outright tell him that they won't leave, because there are children that need stable shelter. The Mayor looks a little annoyed for a moment, before he lets out a defeated sigh. "You did all you could, Child. Thanks anyway." He dejected steps away, presumably to gather the villagers and tell them it didn't work out as he planned. You wish you could have done more, but sometimes it just doesn't work out. |Kami>["You did the best you could, Master."] [[Complete the Quest.->Hub]]]First thing's first. You need to speak with the Innkeeper to find out the best way to get to Wrenville. You have a feeling you won't really need to go the entire distance, but it might be worth knowing how far it is regardless. (if:$innsc is 0)[You approach the Innkeeper, a rather well-built and muscular-looking Bear, with an apron covering his front. He smiles down at you as you approach, leaning over the counter. "Hey there. Name's Dinus. What I can do for you?"](else:)[You approach Dinus, the Innkeeper, and he beams down at the sight of you, leaning over the counter as you approach. "$name! What can I do for you today, huh?"] You ask him the best way to get to Wrenville. He scratches his head at the thought. "Carriage is your best bet. It's too far to walk. It's quite far north, so you're looking at at least 4 days ride, no less." That sounds rather daunting, but you thank him for the information. You //could// walk if you really wanted to, but maybe a Carriage is appropriate. (if:$coin is <75)[You can't afford a carriage.](else:)[(link:"Take a Carriage. (75 gold)")[(set:$SQ21c to 1)(set:$coin to coin - 75)(goto:"SQ22")]] (link:"Walk the distance.")[(set:$SQ21c to 2)(goto:"SQ22")](if:$SQ21c is 1)[You pay to take the carriage through into Wrenville, and it gives you and Kami some time to quickly discuss what you might have to do. After all, who's going to know wolves better than a wolf? |Kami>["Generally, when us wolves hunt, we hunt in packs, and it's usually because we're hungry. I can't honestly fathom why wolves would continue to keep attacking a trade route but for the food they carry to and from the towns. If they're not stopped, then someone could take drastic measures to wipe them out."] It's a very good point, and now you're slightly worried for the safety of those wolves. Perhaps that was the 'Magister' side of you worrying, or maybe you were just a big animal lover in the past. Much like Dinus has said, the carriage ride was brutally long, but it was only after a couple of days that you felt the carraige trundling to a halt. Since you were the only one taking the trip to Wrenville, you decide to disembark and see what the problem is. You're surprised to see the sight of a wreckage near the cart, and a number of people are standing around it. Curious, you get closer.](elseif:$SQ21c is 2)[You decide it'll be cheaper, though not necessarily easier, to walk the distance, much to Kami's enjoyment. He seems to like running around the fields on your walks together. It's going to take you a while, so you take the time to ask Kami about the situation. Who's going to know wolves better than a wolf? |Kami>["Generally, when us wolves hunt, we hunt in packs, and it's usually because we're hungry. I can't honestly fathom why wolves would continue to keep attacking a trade route but for the food they carry to and from the towns. If they're not stopped, then someone could take drastic measures to wipe them out."] Kami makes a very good point. The two of you travel for around 3 and a half days, before you find a strange wreckage on the side of the road, in broad daylight. This is the Trade route to Wrenville, after all, and you have a feeling this is the work of the Wolves. The wreckage, however, has a number of people standing around it, so you get closer.] [[Continue.->SQ23]]"Stand back, kid. This is dangerous work." You hover for a moment and look rather surprised when a Mercenary tells you to back off, and you peek around him to see the predicament. The cart is in tatters, and a number of bags of what would have been food lie strewn around the area. Meat and hunks of bread look to have been bitten into, along with various other fruits and vegatables, and you can only assume that the Wolves have been here. What surprises you, however, is that they still seem to be here. A large, grey-furred adult Wolf stands near a Mercenary, snarling and snapping it's teeth. The Mercenary has his axe drawn, ready to strike if it gets too close. You look a little closer, and your eyes widen when you notice 3 or 4 pups, considerably smaller than the adult, are also joining in on the fight, holding back near the adult Wolf and yapping madly in the mercenaries direction. "Yah!" The Mercenary bellows at the wolf, stamping on the ground and raising it's axe. It seems to spook the adult, who turns and darts off back towards the forest in the distance, and the pups join it. However, one of them stumbles and trips, and the Mercenary manages to grab it. "Hey, look, I caught one!" He guffaws, and the Mercenary that was holding you back finally leaves you be, joining the others as they talk amongst themselves. It seems this quest has already been solved, which is a shame. You turn to leave, wondering whether it's best to walk back or take the carriage. You pause, however, when Kami doesn't follow you, his eyes trained out towards the fleeing wolves. You ask him what's wrong. |Kami>["Aleida."] [[Continue.->SQ24]]Curious, you question Kami more on what he's talking about, and he turns to you, patting his front paws to the ground. He looks quite distressed. |Kami>["Master, we must retrieve that pup and release it into the wild."] He's speaking rather quickly, so you crouch down and put a hand to his head, trying to calm him down. You ask him to explain, in the loosest terms. He takes a deep breath. |Kami>["Before I was blessed by Pan, I had a wife, and 4 children. I thought I might never see them again, yet here they are. She was named Aleida, and they were named Vesper, Berion, Saige and Eike."] He turns his head towards the mercenaries, who jeer and chuckle whilst holding the pup by the scruff of it's neck. The Wolf pup is thrashing around, attempting to bite them so he can be released. The pup looked barely 5 months old. |Kami>["That's Eike. He's the youngest, and a clumsy boy. Please. We must help him, Master."] You'll do your best, of course. You can't deny Kami his children. Though, you have no idea how you're going to get the pup away from those mercenaries. You turn to watch as the Mercenaries begin to set up camp. Perhaps you can sneak in at night and take it, and Kami seems to have a similar view. You could either offer to camp with the mercenaries and take Eike when they're asleep, or wait until the cover of night and sneak in. (link:"Try to join the Mercenaries for the night. (50% chance)")[(set:$SQ24c to 1)(goto:"SQ25")] (link:"Wait until nightfall.")[(set:$SQ24c to 2)(goto:"SQ25")](if:$SQ24c is 1)[(set:$MeP1 to (random:1,2))(if:$MeP1 is 1)[You wander up to the mercenaries, and try to use your charms as a mostly unarmed, underage boy to try and convince them to let you stay with them for the night. Luckily for you, you appealed to the compassion of one of them, at least, and they let you stay, but they have no bed to give you. That's good enough. The Mercenaries, a group of 3, set up camp whilst you sit and watch, and day slowly turns to night. They place Eike inside a cage, and Kami tries his best to remain calm under the circumstances. You admire his resolve. He only has to hold out a little longer. You avoid the jeering leers of the Mercenaries, before you all eventually bed down for the night, and you lie down, staring up at the stars. You wait, patiently, for what feels like forever, until you can hear all three of them snoring. Now's your chance. You get to your feet, and you start to move. [[Continue.->SQ2E]]](elseif:$MeP1 is 2)[You try your best to persuade the mercenaries to let you stay with them until the morning, but you clearly didn't on put enough charm for them to believe you. They tell you to scram, and you'll have to begrudgingly wait until nightfall. The Mercenaries, specifically 3 of them in total, set up camp over the course of a couple of hours, whilst you watch them from behind a pile of rocks not too far from the road. You can see that they put Eike into a cage, and Kami can clearly barely contain his fury. You pat his head to soothe him. The time is nearing. Soon, Eike will be free. Day slowly turns to Night, and the Mercenaries spend a few hours drinking and chatting to each other, as well as goading the puppy stuck in the cage. Eventually, they bed down to sleep, the crackle of the fire keeping them well lit. It's time. You begin to move in. [[Continue.->SQ2E]]]](elseif:$SQ24c is 2)[You figure there's no use trying to persuade them, so you move away from the group and decide to wait until nightfall. The Mercenaries, specifically 3 of them in total, set up camp over the course of a couple of hours, whilst you watch them from behind a pile of rocks not too far from the road. You can see that they put Eike into a cage, and Kami can clearly barely contain his fury. You pat his head to soothe him. The time is nearing. Soon, Eike will be free. Day slowly turns to Night, and the Mercenaries spend a few hours drinking and chatting to each other, as well as goading the puppy stuck in the cage. Eventually, they bed down to sleep, the crackle of the fire keeping them well lit. It's time. You begin to move in. [[Continue.->SQ2E]]]You meander your way around the Mercenaries, tip-toeing and trying not to alert them to your presence. You know that if they catch you, it could end ugly. You're not ultimately sure if they'd just kill you or otherwise, so it's best to not wake them up at all. You slowly step over one of the Mercenaries and crouch by the cage. Eike begins to stir and wake, and starts patting his paws on the cage, on the cusp of barking. A single growl from Kami eliminates all noises from the pup, who hangs his head and looks decidedly told-off. These Mercenaries didn't even bother to put a lock on the cage, so it was very easy for you to ease open the latch and swing the cage door open, and it thankfully didn't squeak. The pup stepped out of the cage and you gestured for Kami to take the lead, tip-toeing you way around the mercenaries again until you were, finally, a considerable distance away. Your heart is pounding. Thank god you got out of there. You watch the tender moment with Kami and his son, and it's clear that they had both missed each other. Eike nuzzled into his fathers side and neck whilst Kami took in his scent, their tails wagging to and fro. In the dark, you make out the outline of a figure to your left, and you can see 3 smaller figures alongside. It's quite clearly Kami's old family, and Eike turns, bounding towards them. The wolf pup stops mid-way and turns back to look at the pair of you, before he runs to his mother. The group of 5 linger for a moment, before they turn and leave, heading towards the forest in the distance. You somehow feel lighter, and whilst Kami appears rather depressed now, you're certain he'll feel better. You crouch down and gently stroke across his head, suggesting you go home. He doesn't say a word, simply leaning his body into your side. [[Complete Quest.->Hub]]<img src="www.example.com/image.jpg"> //import images You glance down at Kami, and suggest changing equipment for the journey ahead, whilst you have the time. He agrees, and gets into a suitable position. |Kami>["Take your time in choosing, Master."] You look over Kami and his equipment. He currently has $KMH Maximum Health, $KA Attack, $KS Speed and $KR Armour. //Each piece of equipment has it's own stats. Health determines the extra maximum HP Kami acquires. Attack determines the strength of his swipes. Speed determines when he will attack, and if he will be able to dodge. Armour provides Kami with necessary damage mitigation.// (link:"Helm:")[(goto:"KEquipHelm")] $KHelm. (link:"Collar:")[(goto:"KEquipCollar")] $KCollar. (link:"Paws:")[(goto:"KEquipPaws")] $KPaws. (link:"Back:")[(goto:"KEquipBack")] $KBack. [[Return.->InnStayRoom]](set:$KCollar to "No Collar")(set:$KPaws to "No Guards")(set:$KBack to "No Armour")(set:$KHelm to "No Helm")(set: $KGH to (a:))(set: $KGC to (a:))(set: $KGP to (a:))(set: $KGB to (a:)) (set:$KGH to it + (a:"No Helm"))(set:$KGC to it + (a:"No Collar"))(set:$KGP to it + (a:"No Guards"))(set:$KGB to it + (a:"No Armour")) (set: $inv to (a:))(set:$inv to $inv + (a:"Coin Pouch"))(set:$coin to 500) (set:$innsc to 0)(set:$innvisited to false)(set:$DN to "day")(set:$DNO to "night")(set:$DNO2 to "day") (set:$FuckedKami to false)(set:$FuckedKamiTop to false)(set:$FuckedKamiBottom to false)(set:$FountV to false)(set:$WyattMet to false)(set:$RatV to false)(set:$RatD to false)(set:$Ratman to "The Ratman")(set:$MisfitF to false)(set:$MarketV to false) (set:$KGH to (a:))(set:$KGC to (a:))(set:$KGP to (a:))(set:$KGB to (a:)) (set:$MQ1D to 1)(set:$Carriage to 0)(set:$ScarStart to 0) (set:$HansF to false)(set:$location to "Holstein")(set:$SBSV to 0) (set:$SBSSpent to 0)(set:$LoveMeter to 0) (set:$FiendMet to false)(set:$FiendF to false) (set:$inv to it + (a:"Strange Mushroom")) (set:$Spells to (a:))(set:$Spells to it + (a:"Calm")) (set:$KamiExped to 0)(set:$KamiBonded to false) (set:$RalnMet to false)(set:$RalnTrain to 0)(set:$SQ3D to 0)(set:$SQ4D to 0)(set:$IoDQ1D to 0)(set:$IoDQ2D to 0)(set:$RowanMet to false)(set:$PellFreed to false) (set:$inv to it + (a:"Zexir's Amulet"))You look through the gear you currently own to see what you can equip Kami's head with. (if: $KGH's length > 0)[(print: $KGH.join(", ")).](else:)[You have no gear to equip.] <div class='img-container-inv'> (click:"No Helm")[(display:"No Helm")] (click:"Battle Helm")[(display:"Battle Helm")] (click:"Speedband")[(display:"Speedband")] (click:"Padded Helmet")[(display:"Padded Helmet")] </div> (link-goto: "Return", (history:)'s last) You look through the gear you currently own to see what you can equip Kami's neck with. (if: $KGC's length > 0)[(print: $KGC.join(", ")).](else:)[You have no gear to equip.] <div class='img-container-inv'> (click:"No Collar")[(display:"No Collar")] (click:"Spiked Collar")[(display:"Spiked Collar")] (click:"Magic Collar")[(display:"Magic Collar")] (click:"Wolf's Rebuke")[(display:"Wolf's Rebuke")] </div> (link-goto: "Return", (history:)'s last) You look through the gear you currently own to see what you can equip Kami's paws with. (if: $KGP's length > 0)[(print: $KGP.join(", ")).](else:)[You have no gear to equip.] <div class='img-container-inv'> (click:"No Guards")[(display:"No Guards")] (click:"Claw Guards")[(display:"Claw Guards")] (click:"Obsidian Guards")[(display:"Obsidian Guards")] </div> (link-goto: "Return", (history:)'s last) You look through the gear you currently own to see what you can equip Kami's back with. (if: $KGB's length > 0)[(print: $KGB.join(", ")).](else:)[You have no gear to equip.] <div class='img-container-inv'> (click:"No Armour")[(display:"No Armour")] (click:"Small Pack Saddle")[(display:"Small Pack Saddle")] (click:"Padded Armour")[(display:"Padded Armour")] </div> (link-goto: "Return", (history:)'s last) (enchant: ?u, (text-style:'underline')) |u>[Battle Helm] A gleaming steel helm made to fit a wolf like Kami. It should hopefully serve to provide his cranium with some protection in battle. Utilising this helm grants Kami 3 Maximum Health, -1 Attack, -2 Speed and 5 Armour. (if:$KHelm is "Battle Helm")[Kami already has this equipped.](else:)[|EBH>[Equip the Battle Helm.](clickreplace:?EBH)[You have equipped the Battle Helm to Kami. He looks ferocious.(set:$KHelm to "Battle Helm")]](enchant: ?u, (text-style:'underline')) |u>[Spiked Collar] A tough leather collar, fitted with sharp steel spikes all around it. It looks pretty vicious, and will no doubt make Kami look pretty tough. Utilising this collar grants Kami 2 Attack, 2 Speed and 1 Armour. (if:$KCollar is "Spiked Collar")[Kami already has this equipped.](else:)[|EBH>[Equip the Spiked Collar.](clickreplace:?EBH)[You have equipped the Spiked Collar to Kami.(set:$KCollar to "Spiked Collar")]](enchant: ?u, (text-style:'underline')) |u>[Claw Guards] A set of sharp iron guards that go over a canine's claws. These are fitted to Kami's front paws to allow his paw swipes to deal some extra damage. Utilising these claw guards grants Kami 2 Attack, and 2 Armour. (if:$KPaws is "Claw Guards")[Kami already has this equipped.](else:)[|EBH>[Equip the Claw Guards.](clickreplace:?EBH)[You have equipped the Claw Guards to Kami. He gives them an experimental swipe.(set:$KPaws to "Claw Guards")]](enchant: ?u, (text-style:'underline')) |u>[Small Pack Saddle] Turn Kami into a mule with this small saddle with packs set on either side. He's too small for you to actually ride, but it'll help alleviate some of the burden of your own inventory. Utilising this armour grants Kami 5 Maximum Health, -1 Attack, -1 Speed and a great deal of embarrassment. (if:$KBack is "Small Pack Saddle")[Kami already has this equipped.](else:)[|EBH>[Equip the Small Pack Saddle.](clickreplace:?EBH)[You have equipped the Small Pack Saddle to Kami. He shoots you a look.(set:$KBack to "Small Pack Saddle")]](if:$Carriage is 1)[You make your way to the edge of town, and find the line of Carriages. You know you can hire at least one from here to take you where you need to go, but you also know it's going to cost you. You step up to the closest cart and read the pricing on the parchment taped to the side. (if:$location is "Holstein")[|Locked>[You are already at Holstein.]](else:)[(if:$coin is >=30)[(link:"Go to Holstein. (30 gold.)")[(set:$coin to coin - 30)(set:$location to "Holstein")(goto:"HolC")]](elseif:$coin is <30)[|Locked>[You cannot afford the carriage to Holstein.]]] (if:$location is "Scarhaven")[You are already at Scarhaven.](else:)[(if:$MQ1D is 1)[(if:$coin is >=30)[(link:"Go to Scarhaven. (30 gold.)")[(set:$coin to coin - 30)(set:$location to "Scarhaven")(goto:"ScarC")]](elseif:$coin is <30)[|Locked>[You cannot afford the carriage to Scarhaven.]]](else:)[|Locked>[You don't know this route.]]] (link-goto: "Return", (history:)'s last)]](else:)[You make your way to the edge of town with Kami in tow. After that rather strange ordeal with the opponent Magister in Scarhaven, you wonder about taking up the Captain's offer and visiting the village once more. On the outskirts of town, you find a number of carriages sitting in a row in what is effectively a long, empty street, with enough room for them to trundle out single file towards the main route out of Holstein. You're not that surprised that they're so efficient. After all, there must be a lot of money to be had in this kind of business, provided you owned a cart and a horse. Some of them had tarps to shield you from the sun, others were left bare. You step up to one of the nearby carriages, and the drive tilts his head to stare down at you. He's a Porcine, primarily, and naturally rather overweight like the rest of his kin. This one, however, is far from slovenly, and looks to be dressed in reasonably lavish garments. "Where to today, fine fellow?" He inquired, reaching down a hand to tap on a parchment on the side of the cart, informing you of the prices to certain routes.(set:$Carriage to 1) (if:$location is "Holstein")[|Locked>[You are already at Holstein.]](else:)[(if:$coin is >=30)[(link:"Go to Holstein. (30 gold.)")[(set:$coin to coin - 30)(set:$location to "Holstein")(goto:"HolC")]](elseif:$coin is <30)[|Locked>[You cannot afford the carriage to Holstein.]]] (if:$location is "Scarhaven")[|Locked>[You are already at Scarhaven.]](else:)[(if:$MQ1D is 1)[(if:$coin is >=30)[(link:"Go to Scarhaven. (30 gold.)")[(set:$coin to coin - 30)(set:$location to "Scarhaven")(goto:"ScarC")]](elseif:$coin is <30)[|Locked>[You cannot afford the carriage to Scarhaven.]]](else:)[|Locked>[You don't know this route.]]] (link-goto: "Return", (history:)'s last)]You pay for the carriage to Scarhaven, and you and Kami climb aboard. This particular carriage was without a tarp over the top, but the idea of lounging in the sun and watching the clouds roll by, rather than having to sweat and swelter the walk to Scarhaven, seemed like a much more interesting prospect. You remember a phrase you've heard many people say: "Take what you're given." You do just that. The trip to Scarhaven took roughly 2 days or more, which wasn't so bad in the grand scheme of things. Horses didn't tend to move quickly unless scared or angry, and the leisurely pace they trotted along at was enough to lull you into a cosy sleep during the nights. The carriage seemed to be owned by two men, who took turns driving, alternating between day and night. You spoke to them occasionally, as well as a few of the other passengers you met in your travels. There wasn't much to tell aside from gossip. Eventually, Scarhaven was in sight, and you were pleased to see it. The carriage trundled towads the edge of town, and you slid off of the cart, thanking the driver and making your way into the town. [[Continue.->ScarHub]](if:$ScarStart is 0)[Scarhaven is a small sort of town, as you and Kami come to realise. The carriage slowly trundles it's way into town and takes a slow turn to the right, making it's way over towards the side of the main trade route leading towards the town's centre. With the carriage safely parked, you and Kami dismount and take a look around the place. The centre of town is similar to Holstein in that it, too, has a 'centrepiece'. However, rather than an impressive, massive fountain, this small town instead has a well, with a wooden roof and shelter surrounding it for a sturdy retrieval of water. You and Kami take a brief tour around the town, for what little there is of it, to see if there are any notable buildings or landmarks. There isn't much. The Forest lies just east of town, and it's mostly quiet now that the animals in the surrounding area have calmed down. Other than that, the town has a small Inn that might not have any rooms for sale, and a blacksmith on the far side of town. There is a large watch tower just by the entrance to the town, with a building connected to it. You guess that it's the watch barracks. You have a variety of options before you. It is currently $DN time. (if:$DN is "day")[The sun shines overhead, filling you with the sense of adventure.](elseif:$DN is "night")[The moonlight glitters above, lighting up the ground below with a rather unearthly glow, almost.](set:$ScarStart to 1)(set:$SBSV to 0) [[Visit the Blacksmith.->ScarBS]] [[Visit the Inn.->ScarInn]] [[Visit the Watch Barracks.->ScarBarracks]] [[Explore a little more.->ScarOther]] [[Get a Carriage.->Carriages]]](elseif:$ScarStart is 1)[You are standing in the centre of Scarhaven, with a variety of options before you. It's currently $DN time. (if:$DN is "day")[The sun shines overhead, filling you with the sense of adventure.](elseif:$DN is "night")[The moonlight glitters above, lighting up the ground below with a rather unearthly glow, almost.] [[Visit the Blacksmith.->ScarBS]] [[Visit the Inn.->ScarInn]] [[Visit the Watch Barracks.->ScarBarracks]] [[Explore a little more.->ScarOther]] [[Get a Carriage.->Carriages]]](if:$DN is "day")[(if:$SBSV is 0)[You know that there's a Blacksmith in town, and you decide it might be worth going and having a look. You're not one for combat gear and you imagine it to be quite expensive, but your inquisitive nature makes you curious about what there is. The first thing you notice as you approach is the large, smouldering coals, glowing dimly along the base of a circular pit. It was particularly wide and sat just a little bit above the ground. To the right of it is a chain linked to a set of billows which would, no doubt, coax the coals into life, should heat be needed. Adjacent to it was a narrow rectangular basin filled with murky water, and an anvil and sharpening wheel not too far away. You were drawn to the man working on a sharp implement at the anvil. He was...well, short. He was about 4 foot high and no taller, with small stubby legs and arms, but an unnaturally long body. He looked canine in posture and nature, with a mixture of white and cream fur covering his body in places, with what looked to be occasionnal flecks of a darker auburn. You remember this kind of canine being reffered to as a 'Corgi'. He's dressed in a dirty brown apron and a set of cloth pants, but his top remains bare, save for the apron itself. He didn't seem to see you approach at first, and when he turned his head to see you, he mildly jumped. A hand ran over his forehead and he straightened up a little, dumping his hammer on the bench nearby and dusting his hands together. "Good afternoon!" His voice was gruff, and his expression was cocky, but his height made it hard to take him seriously. He waddled his way over to you with a rather brisk swagger until he was standing not too far away from you. He snapped out his hand, and you awkwardly shook it. "The name's Hans," He greets you kindly and takes a step back again. "I run the Smithy. You need armor, I got it. Or weapons too. But, uh...you don't seem the type. Your wolf might work though." He shrugs at you, leaving you with the option of purchasing whatever you like, or even talking to him. He seems happy to drop what he's doing to cater to your needs.(set:$SBSV to 1) [[Purchase equipment for Kami.->SBSK]] [[Purchase equipment for yourself.->SBSC]] [[Get to know Hans.->SBSH]] [[Leave.->ScarHub]]] (elseif:$SBSV is 1)[Knowing that Hans, the Blacksmith, was in town, you make your way over, and Kami trotting along behind you and sniffing along the ground. (if:$HansF is true)[(if:$HansBU is true)[You head up the wooden steps with a feeling of dread. As your linger by the steps, you see Hans hunched over a sharpening wheel. He pauses for a moment and turns his head. Your eyes lock for a moment. Slowly, he gets to his feet and dusts himself off, hanging his head a little bit as he moves closer. "What can I do for you?" He asks quietly, and you realise you should make this encounter quick.](else:)[(if:$Relationship is "Hans")[You head up the wooden steps and find the Corgi bent over the forge, twisting a sowrd in the hot coals. He perks up when he heards someone nearby and turns, eyes wide at the sight of you. "Hey!" He beams and scoots closer, giving you a hug. "What's up?"](else:)[You head up the wooden steps and find the Corgi bent over the forge, twisting a sword around in the hot coals. He perks up when he hears your weight creaking the wooden steps, and turns his head to see you. His smile widens. "$name! Great to see you." He seems pleased, and his tiny tail wags behind him. He steps away for a moment, pulling you into a quick hug. "What can I do for you?"]]](elseif:$HansF is false)[You step up the wooden steps and glance over to the anvil and stove, and you spot Hans bent over the sharpening wheel. You can see sparks flinging off the blade, but they abruptly stop when Hans realises you're waiting for him to finish. He turns around towards you and he grins over at you. "Ah, it's you. Hey," He smiled over at you and placed the blade back down. "What can I do you for this time?"] (if:$Relationship is "Hans")[[[Spend some time with Hans.->HansRHub]]](else:)[(if:$HansF is true)[(if:$HansBU is true)[<!--Do Nothing--!>](else:)[[[Ask Hans something important.->HansR]]]](else:)[<!--Do Nothing--!>]] [[Purchase equipment for Kami.->SBSK]] [[Purchase equipment for yourself.->SBSC]] [[Get to know Hans.->SBSH]] [[Leave.->ScarHub]]]](elseif:$DN is "night")[It's the middle of the night, so you're not surprised to find the forge not very active when you get to the Blacksmith's building. The smithy is likely asleep, and maybe it's not best to wake him. It's not very nice, after all. |Kami>["Perhaps we should wait until the morning, Master."] The choice is yours, of course. [[Knock on the door.->SBSHn]] [[Leave.->ScarHub]]]Unlike Holstien, the Scarhaven Inn is a lot, lot quieter than you had expected. Though it seems to be a little more consistent in it's patrons. It's currently [$DN]time. (if:$DN is "day")[The Inn is packed with a variety of people eating food and drinking mead. You catch a few adventurers skulking about, but it's mostly quite empty bar from them. A number of tables are empty. The bartender sits up at the counter, and a bounty board sits at the far end. You also remember seeing some stables outside. An unknown, white-furred child sits in the far corner. [[Speak to the Innkeeper.->SIIK]] [[Check the Bounty Board.->SIB]] [[Check out the Stables outside.->SISt]] [[Speak to the unknown child.->Finn]] [[Leave.->ScarHub]]](elseif:$DN is "night")[The place is a lot busier than it is during the day. Whilst there were adventurers around during the day, they were also accompanied by a number of farmers who were spending some of their saved coins on a few beers of their own. A number of the people serving drinks seemed to be dressed rather lavishly, deliberately exposing their stomachs and wearing short shorts to expose their legs. It was no surprise most of them were female. The bartender stands at the counter serving drinks. A bounty board sits at the far end. You remember seeing some stables outside too. [[Speak to the Innkeeper.->SIIK]] [[Check the Bounty Board.->SIB]] [[Check out the Stables outside.->SISt]] (if:(history:) contains "AdpMerchant")[<!--Already met-->](else:)[A hooded figure sits alone in the far corner, nursing a drink. Their features are hidden, but the cut of their robes is foreign. [[Approach the hooded figure.->AdpMerchant]]] [[Leave.->ScarHub]]](if:(history:) contains "SBaF" or "SBaB")[You're back at the barracks. It's about as busy as you expected it to be, no matter what time of the day it is. There always seems to be people who aren't on shift, and people who are. Either way, you have your options before you. You can tell the guards are giving you some suspicious looks, but you can understand that. Very rarely would a child wander into a guardsmen's barracks unnanounced. But you're here for a reason, surely. Kami is waiting outside, so you shouldn't keep him waiting too long, you think.](else:)[The barracks. (if:(history:) contains "SBSHf")[That's where Hans said Frederick was.](else:)[<!--Do Nothing--!>] The building sits on the far outskirts of town, nestled adjascent to a large watchtower. As you approach, you peer up and see a man staring down at you and Kami, eyeing you up as you make your way towards the barracks. He looks decidedly wary. You can only assume this is because of Kami. Wolves aren't considered to normally be so docile and domesticated usually. You can understand why they are a little apprehensive. You step up to the barrack doors, which appear unguarded, and you swing open the door into the Barrack's interior. Kami decides it's likely better to wait outside whilst you have a look around. You're standing in what is effectively the 'lobby' of the Barrack themselves, which isn't much, to sya the least. There are a number of benches with stools either side of them, where a number of guards seem to sit on their break. There is a set of doors at the back, likely bathrooms, and another door which seems to be labelled. You recognise the name: *Captain Frederick Gaines.* You ponder whether it's a good idea to go in and see him or not.] [[Go to the Captain's Door.->SBaF]] [[Go to the bathroom.->SBaB]] (if:$DN is "night")[(if:(history:) contains "SBaF")[[[Frederick's light is still on.->FrederickN]]](else:)[<!--Do Nothing-->]](else:)[<!--Do Nothing-->] [[Leave.->ScarHub]](if:$DN is "day")[You decide to explore a little whilst the sun is still high in the sky. There isn't much for you to be exploring in such a small town, but you're admittedly a little curious. You make your way around the back of the house and stop, surprised at what you find. (if:(history:) contains "FMBc12" or "FMBc22")[You pause and are greeted to the sight of Dillan, a rather slimly built, cream and black furred Fox who you've seen, and molested. You don't know much about the boy, but he seems to urinate a lot. He's leant against the back of the house, peeing towards the ground once more. His gaze drifts up and his eyes widen when he sees you. You can see his cheeks growing flushed. "H-Hey." He grins awkwardly in response, and a wide grin spreads across your face. [[Molest him.->FBM1]] [[Leave.->ScarHub]]](else:)[A young Fox boy, with a peculiar coat of cream and black, is leaning up against the back of a house. Notably, his pants are around his ankles, collected around his feet, and his humanoid member is hanging out, pointed to the ground. A stream of urine is trickling from the end of his stretchy foreskin, splattering against the foundation of the house itself. His head snaps to meet your gaze, his blue eyes blinking. "Um...?" He doesn't quite know what to say, nor does he know why you're looking. (if:$age is >=13)[You're a little tantalised by the sight.](else:)[<!--Do Nothing--!>] (if:$age is >=13)[[[Molest him.->FBM1]]](else:)[<!--Do Nothing--!>] [[Leave.->ScarHub]]]](elseif:$DN is "night")[You explore a little in the dim light, and eventually find yourself around the back of a house. The place looks remarkably empty. (if:$RowanMet is false)[You explore in the dim light, making your way around the back of the houses. The town is quiet. Then you hear it: a low groan from the shadows. A figure is slumped against the wall. A young buck with tawny fur, lean and handsome despite the gash on his forehead. His travelling clothes are torn. Blood. &quot;Please...&quot; He raises a trembling hand. &quot;Animals... on the road. Controlled. Green eyes. They came out of nowhere.&quot; |Kami>[&quot;Green eyes, Master. Like the ones in the forest.&quot;] His name is Rowan. He was travelling from Seahome. [[Help tend his wounds.->ScarOtherN]] [[Head back.->ScarHub]]](else:)[The night is quiet. Not much to do out here. [[Head back.->ScarHub]]]](if:$HansBU is true)[Hans seems to take the brief request of work to coddle your companion, a little bit. He crouches down, and Kami trots forward, allowing himself to be displayed. Hans spends a great deal of time just petting Kami all over, and you shuffle awkwardly in place. After a few minutes, he gets up and grabs a selection of armour from a large chest, placing it on one of the nearby wooden tables. He wordlessly gestures to the pile for you to choose from.](else:)["Stuff for your dog, is it? No worries, take a look. I don't have a lot in at the moment, but give me a while and I can probably make some new things for you, in time." Hans takes a step to the side and digs around in a large chest, digging out a number of different items, setting them out on the bench for you to see. Kami trotted forwards and sniffed around them, having a look for himself.] (if:$KGH contains "Battle Helm")[You have already purchased the Battle Helm.](else:)[(link:"Purchase a Battle Helm (50 Gold)")[(if:$coin is <50)[You don't have the funds. Hans rubs the back of his head awkwardly.](elseif:$coin is >=50)[You have purchased the Battle Helm.(set: $coin to $coin - 50)(set:$KGH to $KGH + (a:"Battle Helm"))(set:$SBSSpent to $SBSSpent + 50)]]] (if:$KGC contains "Spiked Collar")[You have already purchased the Spiked Collar.](else:)[(link:"Purchase a Spiked Collar (30 Gold)")[(if:$coin is <30)[You don't have the funds. Clearly, talking about his work seems to give him some sort of vigor, because he gets to his feet and starts explaining. "My Daddy owned this smithy before me, and his daddy before that, and his before that. Generations and generations of my family have been working with metal." Hans definitely seemed to take pride in his work, and you could feel that passion from where you were standing. He placed his hands on his hips, and you try not to giggle at his childish his stance appears. "My father was an amazing metalsmith, but unfortunately, he passed a couple of years ago." Hans' confident demeanour faltered a little, before he quickly recuperated. "I didn't quite get to learn everything he knew, but I'm slowly beginning to learn myself. I supply the metalwork for the village, the barracks, and even some of the weapons used in the Empire's Militia up at Holstein." You're mildly impressed. [[Ask him about his personal life.->SBSHc2]] [[Ask him about his sexual life.->SBSHc3]] [[Ask him about his height.->SBSHc4]] [[Ask him about Frederick.->SBSHf]] [[Say goodbye.->ScarHub]]You ask the Corgi about his personal life, and Hans wrinkles his nose. Instead, you opt to resort to a more simple story: you are a farmer boy, and you've raised Kami since you were a pup, and you're leading a life of adventure with your trusty companion. "You, uh, don't look like much of a fighter, kid. If you need some gear for your dog, lemme know. I have some in stock." Hans suggested. "As for me..." He continued. "There ain't much to tell. I've worked here all my life. Parents have passed now-- plague got 'em-- but I get by. Since a lot of people need me to do metalwork, I tend to make quite a bit on the side, so I just use it to spruce up the home." It makes an awful lot of sense to you. Hans seems like a good guy. You find the small room to be a little cramped, but there is at least some decency within it. On the right hand side is a long counter with 3 sinks spaced on it's surface, set into the wood, for a man to was his hands. Opposite the sinks are wooden cubicles with a door on each. You take a step towards one of the open doors, and you quietly push it open. The door creaks as it swings ajar, and you're greeted to the sight of a small bench with a single wooden hole on it. Slowly, you sit up, rubbing your temple and crawling out of bed. The house is quiet. Kami begins to stir and get up when he hears you padding about, and he quickly joined you as you started to get dressed. Now that you've pulled on your tunic, you make your way outside. You turn the corner and see Hans working away on the forge, banging away on a sword by the anvil. He looks particularly stressed. You make your way up to him and he perks his head up as you approach. A wry smile spreads across his face. "Hey babe." He smirked a little bit, and he stepped away from the anvil. "I'm a lil' busy today hun. But, come by anytime. I'll always be happy to see you." He tugged you closer and leant closer, planting a kiss upon your lips. Your curl your arms around him and cradle him for a moment, before you step away and say farewell. [[Leave.->ScarHub]]As you step into Frederick's office, you turn to get a better look at him. You hadn't seen much of him the first time you'd met within the guardsmen tent on the outskirts of town, but you could easily get a better look at him now. He was more like a domestic cat than any predatory feline, and his rich hues of grey and blue made him look elegant and serene. Atop his head with a short collection of white fur, styled into some sort of 'stuck up' hair. You could tell it was fur, but it seemed to be dyed. "I'm glad you came. I wasn't sure if you'd ever come to Scarhaven. I take it you spoke to Hans?" You nod. (if:$HansF is true)[You decide you'd better not tell him what you and Hans got up to.](elseif:$HansF is false)[Hans was a nice man to talk to. Maybe you should get to know him more.] "Yeah. Hans is a good guy. Anyway, uh, the reason I called you here..." He took a seat behind his rich mahogany desk, placing his hands together and watching you with a suspicious gaze. "I'm not an idiot, of course. You have some sort of magic, something similar to that man in the woods. I saw him. I knew what he could do. Would you mind filling me in on the details?" You hesitate. You know what Kami would say in this situation. (link:"Tell him the truth.")[(set:$SBaFc to 1)(goto:"SBaF3")] (link:"Lie. (50% Chance.)")[(set:$SBaFc to 2)(goto:"SBaF3")](if:$SBaFc is 1)[You roughly explain to Frederick what happened. You tell him about your powers, how you inherited them, and how you were able to stop the animals from going out of control. You also mention the man you saw, and Frederick slowly nodded. "Yes...that man. He must have the same powers you do, but like everyone, he is an individual. I won't judge you based upon your abilities." He spoke calmly, and you're thankful he understands. "You might buy it, but I don'!" Neither you nor Frederick had uttered a word, and yet, someone had spoke. In a matter of seconds, Frederick's sword flew from his scabbard and turned in the air, the tip of the blade pointing directly to your throat. You tilt his head up nervously and your eyes widen in fright. You hadn't expected this. "Pren. Heel." The sword snapped back upon the command, twisting in on itself. The sword bended against it's hilt and curled into a tight ball, spinning wildly in the air. Within a matter of moments, it's surface began to change into something wholly different. It turned from a glossy, shiny grey to a rich, deep blue. Just like that, there was an audible *pop*, and the sword turned into a stumpy little creature. No taller than 2 foot, the strange beast was covered in bluish-red skin, bordering on purple, with a notable pot belly, a rounded head with a small nose and large horns and ears, thin arms and legs ending in rather large hands and feet. His tiny wings fluttered behind him, and his face construed into something similar to irritation and suspicion. [[Continue.->SBaF3c1]]](elseif:$SBaFc is 2)[(set:$SBaFc2 to (random:1,2))(if:$SBaFc2 is 1)[You tried to make up some sort of excuse, attempting to skirt around the issue of your magic. You didn't want to make anyone suspicious of you, and you thought this was the best way. Frederick listens to your words as you make up some sort of excuse for what happened. God knows how you managed it, but he seems to buy it, slowly nodding his head up and down. "I don't buy it!" Neither you nor Frederick had uttered a word, and yet, someone had spoke. In a matter of seconds, Frederick's sword flew from his scabbard and turned in the air, the tip of the blade pointing directly to your throat. You tilt his head up nervously and your eyes widen in fright. You hadn't expected this. "Pren. Heel." The sword snapped back upon the command, twisting in on itself. The sword bended against it's hilt and curled into a tight ball, spinning wildly in the air. Within a matter of moments, it's surface began to change into something wholly different. His tiny wings fluttered behind him, and his face construed into something similar to irritation and suspicion. Neither you nor Frederick had uttered a word, and yet, someone had spoke. In a matter of seconds, Frederick's sword flew from his scabbard and turned in the air, the tip of the blade pointing directly to your throat. You tilt his head up nervously and your eyes widen in fright. You hadn't expected this. "Pren. Heel." The sword snapped back upon the command, twisting in on itself. The sword bended against it's hilt and curled into a tight ball, spinning wildly in the air. Within a matter of moments, it's surface began to change into something wholly different. It turned from a glossy, shiny grey to a rich, deep blue. Just like that, there was an audible *pop*, and the sword turned into a stumpy little creature. No taller than 2 foot, the strange beast was covered in bluish-red skin, bordering on purple, with a notable pot belly, a rounded head with a small nose and large horns and ears, thin arms and legs ending in rather large hands and feet. His tiny wings fluttered behind him, and his face construed into something similar to irritation and suspicion. [[Continue.->SBaF3c3]]]]"Don't trust him, sire. That kid's a lying little shit." You feel your skin go cold. You realise now that you lied rather badly, and that Frederick and his creature had seen right through it. Both of them narrow their eyes at you, and your feel your heartbeat quicken. "Now's the time to tell the truth, $name. I understand you might have be scared for your wellbeing, but trust me, whatever secrets you hide are safe here." You blow a small sigh and give Frederick all the necessary details, from your powers and abilities, down to the strange man in the woods and how you stopped the creatures from destroying Scarhaven. Frederick nods and listens, occasionally glancing to thee creature, who nodded in turn. "I apologise for Pren's behaviour. Imps can be a little eccentric and often speak their mind, and he's still not used to listening to commands." Imp. Demon. That makes a little more sense to you. You apologise as well for lying, but Frederick doesn't seem to mind. "I can understand why you were anxious to tell me. Regardless, you did save out lives that day, and I require your aid again. Please come to me if you get any more information on this strange man. Otherwise, please come to me if you have any questions. I might be able to point you in the right direction." [[Thank him and leave.->ScarBarracks]]"Ignore Pren. He's always a little wary of strangers." Pren. That's the name of the tiny creature sitting before you, and he stares right into your soul with his dark eyes, the black irises staring into your own. You feel a little uncomfortable with his gaze. He doesn't stare for long, if only to do something entirely different. He leaps at you, and you let out a yelp of surprise, covering your face with your hands. His own large hands hook around your wrists and he flings himself around your body, his feet planting into your back as he straddles your frame, holding tightly onto your shoulders. You're not sure what he's doing at first, but then he takes a deep sniff on your hair. "...Alright, fine. He's a Magister, alright. That scent is particularly palpable." Pren remarked after a moment, and you blow a small sigh of relief. The strange creature lets go of you and scoots back towards Frederick, who looks a little apologetic. "Sorry. Pren is...a little unorthodox. Imps normally are." A demon. That, you didn't expect, but you didn't press for information, nor did you judge. Regardless, Frederick took a seat, looking grim. "Thank you for the information, $name. For now, I would appreciate if you could come to me with any other information you might have on this man in the future. Otherwise, feel free to come to me if you have any questions." [[Thank him and leave.->ScarBarracks]]"Trust me, Sire, this kid is a lying son o' a gun. He's got Magister written all over him." You feel your skin go cold, and Frederick frowned, staring you down, he snaps his gaze to Pren, the creature, who doesnt take his eyes off you. "I trust the child. He would not lie." Seems like Frederick bought your lie, which is good, but the creature seems a little infuriated about it. Frederick slaps the beast's head and Pren whimpers. "Ignore Pren. He can be a little eccentric. Most Imps are. Regardless, you seem to have helped us a great deal with the animal attack, so I want to employ your help once again, if you'll accept." You nod immediately, happy to help, and Frederick seems pleased, sinking into his seat. "Come to me if you garner any further information on this strange man in the woods. Otherwise, please come to me with any questions you may have. I might be able to point you towards any leads I might have." [[Thank him and leave.->ScarBarracks]]You ask Frederick about Scarhaven, and he tilts his head in surprise. "This ol' place? It's just a simple country town. We supply food to the capital as taxes and to some towns around us, but we mostly keep to ourselves. We're not like Seahome just Northeast of here: poor bastards are quite literally the bypass up to The Bridge, so they get a lot of traffic." "Just about the only thing to make out town a little more lively than usual is Hans, but even then, they only ever come once a month. There's honestly not much to tell." You're not surprised that there's little to say on the town. Part of you was just curious. [[Ask about Frederick and his personal life.->Frc2]] [[Ask about Pren.->Frc3]] [[Ask about any leads.->Frc4]] [[Say goodbye and leave.->ScarBarracks]]You realise it was likely a rather personal subject, but nonetheless, you ask Frederick about himself. The feline pulls a sheepish grin, looking a bit uncomfortable. "Well, I enlisted in the army when I was 14, and I've served the Empire of the Silver Spire ever since. I rose from a simple soldier to Luitenant, then up to Corporal and finally Captain. They must have recognised my achievements, I guess." "They knew that I originally heralded from Scarhaven, so they placed me here as Captain of the guard. Since a lot of the Milita come through here to collect weaponry from the smithy and deposit materials and money as payment, it seems only natural that they'd leave a Captain here to make sure no idiotic thieves try to take any of the goods. Plus, Hans and I are old friends." That made a little bit of sense. Out of curiosity, you ask a little about Frederick's relationship with Hans. "Been friends since we were kids. Granted, I was a lot older than him at the same, by roughly about 6 or 7 years, but...well, there aren't a lot of kids in the town, so we stuck together like glue." That's pretty cute. [[Ask about Scarhaven.->Frc1]] [[Ask about Pren.->Frc3]] [[Ask about any leads.->Frc4]] [[Say goodbye and leave.->ScarBarracks]]The minute the Imp's name left your lips, that sword slipped from Frederick's scabbard once again, and it twisted in the air. With yet another *pop*, the Imp appeared, settling himself on the desk and spreading the 4 claws toes on either foot. "Long story," Frederick groaned. "Pren is an Imp that I contracted not too long ago. When things were going south with the animals in the forest, this little Imp offered his aid in exchange for a contract and some food now and again. I accepted and he's been in a pain in my ass ever since." "Hey, c'mon, I'm not that bad." Pren grunted, leaning his head up and staring up at the Feline. Frederick glared. "He can shapeshift into a few objects, but mostly weapons. Sword, bow, dagger...occasionally a throwing knife," Frederick loosely explained. "I just have to endure his often...innappropriate outbursts." "Hey, why haven't you bent this kid over the desk already? Aren't you some sort of closet pedophile?" Pren remarked absently, and Frederick delivered a sharp slap to the Imp's head, making him yelp. You try to not look too put off. The Imp winks at you, and you have a feeling he's hinting at something. [[Ask about Scarhaven.->Frc1]] [[Ask about Frederick and his personal life.->Frc2]] [[Ask about any leads.->Frc4]] [[Say goodbye and leave.->ScarBarracks]]You ask Frederick if he has any leads on the strange man you saw in the Woods not too long ago. Frederick sighs and shakes his head. "Nothing yet, I'm afraid. Once I hear something, I'll let you know, but I haven't heard anything new." You understand. [[Ask about Scarhaven.->Frc1]] [[Ask about Frederick and his personal life.->Frc2]] [[Ask about Pren.->Frc3]] [[Say goodbye and leave.->ScarBarracks]]You kindly accept the Imp's offer, and he smirks, stepping to the side and letting you in. You step through into the small office and hear the clunk as the wooden door shuts behind you. As soon as you turn around, and Imp is upon you, climbing up your front and gripping at your clothes. You stagger a little from his weight and shift a little, leaning against the side of the desk. "You're a sexy little kid, y'know that? I love fucking 'em." Pren remarks with a happy, cheeky grin, and he slides off your body, scooting up onto the nearest chair and dragging it closer. His fingers dig underneath your knes and hoist them up, spreading your wide. "I bet you have a cute little ass." You gather that the Imp loves to talk, but you don't bother to tell him to be quiet. You feel his large, dexterous hands around your underwear, and you feel them slipping from your behind, the cool air of the office brushing across your $skin. Surprisingly, Pren didn't say a word, and you quickly realise why. His large hands squeezed your $sc $skin and spread your buttcheeks apart, his mouth descended down between your crack. It wears a rather baggy tunic around it's body, but its forearms are left exposed. Its hands are covered in a pair of gloves. You can't see much more below than that, but since you can't hear the gentle 'thunk' of boots on the floor, you guess that the child is also barefoot. Sitting right next to the strange cub is a rather hefty backpack filled with scrolls. "Oh, Howdy!" The boy snaps his head up and beams at you, displaying his rows of shiny white teeth. You have no idea what he's talking about. He doesn't elaborate at all, and reaches up, plucking a feather from his forehead. Just like that, he hands it out to you. "Hope you enjoy the rest of your time here." He happily adds, and you take the feather from him with an awkward thanks. Unlike the bounty board in Holstein, this one offers less in the way of quests, but each of them seem equally as difficult. Much like Holstein, they all follow a relatively similar format, listing the quest details and offering a gold amount as the reward. You squint and take a look over it. Above it all is a rather large piece of parchment that looks fresher and newer than the rest, with a royal insignia stamped onto the top. (if:(history:) contains "IoDHub")[There is an empty spot on the board. This quest must have already been completed.](else:)[[[King's Decree: Investigate the Rumblings in the Mountains. (Reward of 500 Gold)->MQu2]]] [[Scarhaven Royal Guard: Capture the Bandit Leader. (Reward of 200 Gold)->SQ3]] [[Von Aldersant: A Request. (Reward of 300 Gold)->SQ4]] [[Return.->ScarInn]](if:(history:) contains "SIIK")[You step up to the counter and greet Wes with a smile. He leans over the counter and rests his elbows on the wood, shooting you a wide and toothy grin as he sets the cloth and glass down. "Hey kid. What'll it be?" He asks with a tilt to his voice, a measure of softness that one would only take with a child.](else:)[You step up to the counter and look up at the Innkeeper. He doesn't seem to have a neck, his head simply stretching out into his shoulders and broad chest. His large, muscled arms work desxterously on polishing a glass. He doesn't seem to want to wear a top. "Welcome to Scarhaven, kid. The name's Wes. What can I do you for? The stables were obviously used for travellers and likely for any horses used by the guardsmen around Scarhaven itself. You step into the large stables, which is essentially a wide corridor with a roof overhead, and various small fenced areas on either side. You count that there are 10 pens, but only 5 of them are being used. The rest are empty. The place is eeriely quiet. The animals seem oddly calm around you, and you can guess why. There's not much you can do here. [[Leave.->ScarInn]]](elseif:$DN is "night")[You make your way out of the Inn in the dark and around towards the stables. They're used for traveller's horses as well as horses for the guards around Scarhaven. You gather is might be quite busy. Out of the 10 fanced stables, 8 of them are packed with horses. Most of them are quiet, but you can hear the whinnying and neighing of a horse at the back. You go to investigate. The Horse is looking particularly unruly and anxious, and is snorting and stamping his hooves. You feel bad for him: he must be having difficult adjusting to a cramped stall. A smirk dances across his lips, before he finally resigns to the idea. "Okay," He begins. "What do you wanna know about me, huh?" [[Ask about his childhood.->MisfitRI1]] [[Ask about his work.->MisfitRI2]] [[Ask about his sexual interests.->MisfitRI3]] [[Ask about his hobbies.->MisfitRI4]] [[Ask about his people.->MisfitRI5]] [[You have everything you need to know.->MisfitRHub]](if:(history:) contains "MisfitRI1")[The Ratman's nose wrinkles a little. "I already told you. Don't you remember?" He asked. Admittedly, you don't, so you shake your head. He sighs through his nose. "Okay, well...you know, I grew up in the sewer. I have 4 brothers, parents are...not that great at being parents. That's about it." You blink at him and you can tell he's a little peeved that you didn't remember what he'd already said and felt the need to ask again. You quickly apologise and see his lips curl into a small smile. "Yeah, well...don't worry about it," He muttered, reaching up to scratch the back of his neck. "S'not like there'd be much to tell otherwise anyway."](else:)[Misfit seemed rather surprised that you'd rather learn about the past than the present. At first, he looks a little uncomfortable. "Huh. Well, uh...you already know that I come from the sewers underneath the city. That's where most Ratmen are born, so I ain't no different." He explains loosely. You nod and follow along. "Uh, I have 4 brothers...we all have weird names. There's Freak, Megrim, Twist and Fiend. We all kind went into different professions...most of us became thieves or generally shady people." He shrugged. He looked a little nervous about continuing, but your encouraging stare gave him a little bit of pride, and he swallowed his nerves to keep going. "Our folks weren't exactly particularly parental-minded, but the sewers are safe enough for us, other than the occasional deep pit. We mostly avoid roaming around in the water: there's quite a few pathways here and there...and we snuck up to the surface a lot too." He realised he was babbling a little. You could tell he wasn't quite comfortable about talking about himself. "Well, uh...that's it, I guess. It wasn't eventful, nothing tremendous happened...I just have shitty parents and decent-enough brothers. Twist and Freak became thieves, Megrim went off the grid...we heard he became an assassin. Fiend was never very good at looking after himself so he still lingers around my place." Interesting. You grin at Misfit, and he blows a small sigh of relief. He looked like he was scared of your judgement, but looks glad now that you aren't bothered by his past. (set:$LoveMeter to it + 20)] [[Ask about his work.->MisfitRI2]] [[Ask about his sexual interests.->MisfitRI3]] [[Ask about his hobbies.->MisfitRI4]] [[Ask about his people.->MisfitRI5]] [[You have everything you need to know.->MisfitRHub]]You ask Misfit about his work, and he puffs out his chest, suddenly filled with pride. You know this is going to be good. "Oh, yeah. I'm an alchemist!" He exclaims, proclaiming it to the world. "Makin' potions and shit. Nah, I, uh, I've always been a little interested in potions, y'know? They kinda...//transcend// our normal ideas of the world. Kind of like magic." You can see this is a subject that really interests him, so you try to keep as equally interested, too. "In fact, //some// potions can be magic, but I'm not much of a magician. But, it's the closest to magic that I can get, and damn, it feels good. It's //fucking// incredible how a couple of different reagents in the right order can make a potion that can //grow back a fucking limb.//" He beams, and puts an arm around you. His smile fades for a moment. "Although..." He continues. "I can't get much for ingredients in the sewers. Occasionally I can find a merchant willing to trade some things, which is great, but it ain't often. I can't really experiment as much as I want to do, but at least some of my potions do some good, you know?" You understand, and you lean into him a little, taking in his scent. He puts his arm around you tighter, giving you an affectionate squeeze. "I tell ya $name, you and I are gonna do great work in my lab." (set:$LoveMeter to it + 20) [[Ask about his childhood.->MisfitRI1]] [[Ask about his sexual interests.->MisfitRI3]] [[Ask about his hobbies.->MisfitRI4]] [[Ask about his people.->MisfitRI5]] [[You have everything you need to know.->MisfitRHub]]Misfit purred and ran a hand across your stomach, stroking along your $skin and tracing his fingers up towards your chest, picking at your tunic top. "Wouldn't you like to know?" He grinned with a particularly wicked grin, and you know he has some things in mind. Perhaps you should (link:"indulge them.")[(goto:"MisfitRISS")] [[Ask about his childhood.->MisfitRI1]] [[Ask about his work.->MisfitRI2]] [[Ask about his hobbies.->MisfitRI4]] [[Ask about his people.->MisfitRI5]] [[You have everything you need to know.->MisfitRHub]]You ask Misfit about what hobbies he has, and the Rat has to think about it for a moment. "Honestly? Outside of doing alchemy, I don't really have anything. I don't like to go swimming, //fuck// that, nor do I write, paint, or anything else..." "Does that make me boring?" He grimaces, and you place an encouraging hand on his shoulder. Of course it doesn't! Misfit seems relieved and plants a sweet kiss on your forehead. "God, I could //fuck// you." He purrs. Misfit can be so vulgar when he's horny. (set:$LoveMeter to it + 10) [[Ask about his childhood.->MisfitRI1]] [[Ask about his work.->MisfitRI2]] [[Ask about his sexual interests.->MisfitRI3]] [[Ask about his people.->MisfitRI5]] [[You have everything you need to know.->MisfitRHub]]You weren't sure whether it was a sore subject or not, so you ask Misfit about his people, and what they're like. Misfit grimaces. "Most of us are horrible people. Some try to make their lives better, but the prejudice the capital enforces on us makes some of us think 'Hey, they think we're cunts, so let's be cunts!'. It's pretty sad." He sighs, and you almost feel back for asking. He ruffles your $hair and stares down into your $ec eyes. "This sounds contradictory, but I would not trust my people. Hell, maybe don't trust //me//. We're a naturally trecherous species, and to be fair, it's not really out fault. I bet if we lived above ground with you guys, things would be a lot different...but maybe in another time." You cuddle up to him, and he seems to appreciate it, his expression melancholy. [[Continue.->MisfitRISS2]]You ask Misfit if you can visit his house, and the Ratman looks momentarily surprised at the request. His expression, however, quickly turns to delight. He grabs you by the hand and drags you along, and you quickly glance to Kami, making sure he's following you. The two of you make your way around the city, nearing the outskirts with every step, until you take a sharp right turn and make your way down a simple paved slope. It looks like it heads down to the canal that runs through Holstein. From there, the three of you head west for some distance, before turning left into some strange corridor underneath the city's surface. Another turn down that corridor, and you're heading down a series of steps. Well, it's better than some sort of manhole. Soon enough, you find yourself being guided along a series of paths next to a long, watery line of sewage. You don't have to look to know it's probably rancid, and the smell assaults your nostrils. You can hear Kami whining his protest behind you. (if:$species is "Canine" or "Fox")[Much like him, you have a sensitive sense of smell, so you have to hold your breath occasionally.](else:)[You can't even begin to imagine how he feels.] Luckily, it wasn't for long. As the three of you walked, you eventually took a turn that strayed away from the main stream of sewage, deeper into the tunnels. You begin to notice torches set up on the walls, lit and flickering. You pass a number of what you can only assume to be chambers, before you finally stop at one. [[Continue.->MisfitHouse2]](if:(history:) contains "MisfitHouse2")[Misfit grins. He seems to like that you want to stop by. He leads you by the hand, since he always seems to know the way better than you. Kami follows along behind. The walk is short, thankfully, else it might be tiring. Then again, Misfit takes his walk every day! The smell of the sewers doesn't seem so bad this time, and even Kami doesn't complain. Eventually, you make your way down the corridors, and find yourself as Misfit's house once more. It looks the same as it always have, and he puts an arm around you. "What you got planned this time, babe?" (if:$FiendMet is true)[[[Ask if you can hang out with Fiend.->MisfitFiend1]]](elseif:$FiendMet is false)[<!--Do Nothing--!>] [[Something kinky.->MisfitHSS]] [[Ask to help in his lab.->MisfitLabGame]] [[Leave.->MisfitRHub]]](else:)["This is us." Misfit swings the door open, and you're quite stunned at what you find. The room isn't lavish, but it definitely screams of 'home'. The structure of the room itself is nothing but a giant cube, with sharp corners. You guess that it's about 20 foot by 20 foot, and must be one of the larger chambers, for such a structure couldn't exist normally without having some use. A number of pipes and tubes line the back wall, but Misfit has made them his own by hanging various decorations from them, in the form of both magical lights, and of strange ornaments fashioned from bone and fur. Underneath your feet is primarily stone, but a narrow grate runs right through the middle of the room, and you see a stream of clear water runnin through. "That's the drinking water for the city above. I got myself a nice place, you don't you think?" Misfit grinned, clearly proud of his accomplishment. You, too, are quite surprised. The rest of the room is quaint. Misfit obviously couldn't afford a good bed, but made his home amongst a collection of blankets on the far side of the room that sits atop a wooden frame. On the opposite wall in a long wooden desk, home to a series of glass tubes and vials. You guess this to be Misfit's lab. The smell that once lingered has since faded, and you've surprised at how cold you're not feeling. It doesn't feel that damp, either. Misfit sidles up to you and curls his hands around your hips, pulling you in. "You like it?" He asks, and you nod, beaming up at him. A grin spreads across his face. "Okay. What do you want to do?" [[Something kinky.->MisfitHSS]] [[Ask to help in his lab.->MisfitLabGame]] [[Leave.->MisfitRHub]]]At the mention of something sordid, Misfit's eyes widen and a grin spread across his face, wider than any you've seen before. He strides across the room to close the distance between you, and guides you over towards his bed. Your eyes cast over to Kami, who stands at the far corner of the room, tilting his head. He doesn't seem too bothered, but you feel bad for not including him. Misfit senses you hesitating, and he peers over towards Kami, who sits on the other side of the room by the entrance. He looks at the pair of you with an unreadable expression. "I mean...if you want." Misfit murmured quietly. [[Let Kami join in.->MisfitHSSc1]] [[Enjoy Misfit for yourself.->MisfitHSSc2]]You ask to help Misfit in his lab, and he seems pleased. "Alright then, lemme show you the ropes." He leads you over to the lab table and Kami settles himself amongst the blankets of the Ratman's bed, watching from afar. Misfit gestures across the table and you look down all the series of bottles and vials, distillers and separators. "Alright, so it's pretty simple. Each Potion has their own ingredients, and they have to go in order: the base, the two magic ingredients, and then final piece of magic. Luckily for us, I kind of have some magic, in the form of powder. Got it from a pretty special magician." You seem to understand, and he ruffles your $hair. "Get them all right, and I'll give you a little reward." He intices you with the promise of something special, and you grin. You look at the bottle, momentarily mesmerised by it's swimming colours. It's a liquid, but it glitters with a magical essence. You watch as Misfit pours it out into the vial and concocts something rather special. { (set:$potionn to (random:1,6)) (if:$potionn is 1)[(set:$potionname to "Fortifying")] (if:$potionn is 2)[(set:$potionname to "Bubbling")] (if:$potionn is 3)[(set:$potionname to "Staunching")] (if:$potionn is 4)[(set:$potionname to "Seething")] (if:$potionn is 5)[(set:$potionname to "Iron Skin")] (if:$potionn is 6)[(set:$potionname to "Divine")] } Misfit holds the potion up. (if:$potionname is "Fortifying")[You notice the liquid lookds decidedly firm, with a strange red sheen that makes it look almost metallic in nature. "This is a Fortifying Potion," Misfit explained loosely. "You use it to, well, fortifying yourself. It's useful for veteran fighters, as it makes them harder to be knocked off their feet. Some mages use it too. Sells for a pretty penny."](elseif:$potionname is "Bubbling")[The liquid itself seems to be eternally, perpetually bubbling in the flask, but Misfit holds it as if it's not hot to the touch. "This is a Bubbling Potion," Misfit explained. "Not used for that much, but makes for a peculiar herbal remedy. Works for, uh, some fevers and shit. I don't make it often."](elseif:$potionname is "Staunching")[The liquid inside it is so viscous that it varely moves, and you wonder if it's even a liquid at all. "This is a Staunching Potion," Misfit mused. "Doctors buy it off me a lot because you someone swigged this after their arm got chopped off, then they wouldn't bleed out. Super useful."](elseif:$potionname is "Seething")[The potion looks like it's on fire, and it gives off a small glove. Misfit looks to be holding it gingerly. "It looks dangerous, but this is a Seething Potion," Misfit explained. "Explorers will drink it in colder climates to keep themselves warm. Hell, I've seen barbarians wade into snow without any clothes on because they drank this."](elseif:$potionname is "Iron Skin")[You notice the liquid doesn't move an inch. Misfit caught your expression and turned it upside down, revealing it was, in fact, solid. "An Iron Skin Potion," He mused. "Makes your skin practically impenetrable, but it has to start from a source, which is your inevitable weak spot for like, 2 minutes."](elseif:$potionname is "Divine")[The liquid is glowing with an otherworldly aura that makes it seem almost holy in nature. "A Divine Poition," Misfit explained. "Isn't actually used for a whole lot, but it's hard to make, and rich people like to swim in it because it makes them look fancy." He looks down at you for a moment, lost in thought. Then, he hands you the potion. "Keep it, $name," He insists. "Just, you know, for fun." (set:$inv to $inv + (a:"Divine Potion"))] Misfit smiles down at you, and you look from him to the vial, and back again. (if:$MRightCounter is 4)["You did a good job, $name. You got 'em all right, too! Here's a little something." Misfit hands you a few coins for being such a good helper, and you can't help but grin from cheek to cheek. (set:$coin to it + 5)(set:$LoveMeter to it + 10)](elseif:$MRightCounter is <4)["You did okay, but you didn't get 'em all right. Try and get 'em all next time, okay?" Misfit doesn't look angry with you, but you feel a little sad that you didn't try your best. (set:$LoveMeter to it + 2)] [[Return.->MisfitRHub]](set:$inv to it - (a:"Divine Potion"))With an anxious nod, you accept his offer, unsure what he has in mind for you. You place the potion on the table and he reaches over to grab it, popping the cork and glugging the mixture town. You spot a thin trickle dripping from his lips and down his neck, but you don't say anything. He empties the entire vial and sighs with satisfaction, wiping his mouth. For a moment, you briefly see his red eyes glow. "Thanks! And now, your reward." He snaps his fingers, and you're suddenly blinded by a white, flashing light. When you vision finally returns to you, you find yourself in a different place entirely. You're in some sort of room, and you're laying down on what feels like the most luxurious bed you've ever experienced. You snap your gaze down to find yourself utterly naked, displaying your $body body and $sc $skin. Kami doesn't seem to be anywhere. Finally, you focus your attention on the white-furred boy, who's standing at the far side of the room, by the door, stark naked. You have no idea how he got you here, but you know something is amiss. As you try to move, you feel your arms disobeying you. The pup steps forward and climbs up onto the bed, crawling over you. "This is your reward, mortal. I suggest you savour it; not everyone has the luxury to bed a god, especially not one as willing as I." The boy's voice is laced with peculiar additional tones that give his voice and mannerisms an otherworldly aura. He promptly straddles your crotch and you take in his figure.
