[[Continue.->FinnSS5]]The cub panted, leaning back and relaxing a little. He looked down to admire the mess he'd made on your chest, before he shifted his legs forwards and spreading his feet out. His paws touch with your upper arms and he casually sits on your member, keeping himself steadily with his hands on the bed. "Whew. That was...you mortals sure are fun." He seems rather pleased with this turn of events. He looks down at you and you can't muster much of a smile. With a snap of his fingers, control of your limbs finally returns to you. Now able to move, you sit up, and place your hands on his hips. He watches you with a half-wary, half-amused expression as your touch his fluffy body, stroking against his stomach with the back of your hand. "Consider yourself lucky that you get to touch a god. You've been blessed, mortal." He pouts. You're fairly certain that what he said is true. "Well, this has been fun, but I must return to my duties," He sighs wistfully. "You shant remember this encounter, but...if you do have a Divine Potion, please bring it to me. I shall at least implant that idea into you." You're about to ask what he meant, but your vision is blinded again. When you regain control of your senses, you find yourself standing in the middle of Scarhaven's rather simply and empty town square. What the hell just happened? You can't remember, but your coin pouch feels a little heavier. 100 coins heavier, to be exact. (set: $coin to it + 100) [[Continue.->ScarHub]]You know Misfit well enough by now to pick up some of his mannerisms, and you are almost 100% certain that he's not a fan of living in the sewers, and likely doesn't plan to all his life. You're not much for making elaborate plans, but you want to do something nice for him. Hence, you suggest going somewhere out of the city. The concept alone seems to baffle Misfit, who stands there looking at you, wide-eyed. "Are you sure?" He asks nervously. "If anyone saw us..." You're confident that things are going to be fine. Without giving him much of a choice, you grab his hand and pull him towards the exit of the alleyway. Misfit makes a point of pulling his hood up and tucking his tail in before the two of you step out into the light. A quick, brisk walk through town takes you around the outskirts of the fountain, past various citizens and adventurers as you lead Misfit down the main trade route and out of the city. He starts to question you after a while, but you pleasant expression and earnest eyes quiten him down enough to humour you, at least for the time being. Your journey leads the pair of you across the rugged plains surrounding the city and towards the dense forestland that is barely a 5 minute walk away. Kami dutifully follows behind you with a knowing twinkle in his eyes, aware of your plans. You catch his gaze and cant help but grin childishly. You make your way through the outskirts of the forest, taking a methodical and intended route through the trees until you reach a beautiful clearing. [[Continue.->MisfitPicnic2]]Spotting another pamphlet on the board, you rip it off and give it a read. =><= (css:"border: 1px solid black")[**Requesting those skilled in combat.** This is a public message on behalf of the Guardsman and Mayor of **Scarhaven**. Bandits in the local area have been making it difficult to get imports and trade from the capital. Having set up shop after the rabid animals were driven out, they now seek to terrorise the villagers and steal their trade. We would greatly appreciate any assistance is ridding the town of the bandits. If you accept, please enquire at the guardhouse for more information. **Reward: 200 Gold**] <== (if:$SQ3D is >=1)[You have already completed this quest. [[Return->SIB]]](else:)[200 gold is a decent amount. Bandits shouldn't be too difficult, right? You look down at Kami, who sniffs the parchment. |Kami>["I can track them, Master. If we enquire at the guardhouse, we may get more information."] [[Accept the Quest.->SQ3Start]] [[Leave it for now.->SIB]]]You see a pamphlet on the board buried underneath some others, and you rip it from the board to read it. =><= (css:"border: 1px solid black")[**To whom it may concern:** I am the venerable **Yven Von Aldersant**. I have a matter that requires someone of a smaller persuasion. Specifically, I am seeking children to perform a rather delicate request. Those who are interested should come alone. You will be paid handsomely, and be provided accommodation and travel where appropriate. **Reward: 300 Gold**] <== (if:$SQ4D is >=1)[You have already completed this quest. [[Return->SIB]]](else:)[300 gold is nothing to scoff at, you know that much. Yet, you can't help feel that something about this 'request' isn't quite right. Kami sniffs the paper and his ears flatten. |Kami>["This does not feel right, Master. 'Seeking children'... I urge caution."] [[Accept the Quest.->SQ4Start]] [[Decline.->SIB]]](enchant: ?KingQ, (text-colour: yellow))You lean up to examine the uppermost flyer, which seems to be yet another decree from the Monarch of Holstein. You read it. (align:"=><=")[(css:"border: 1px solid black")[|KingQ>[**Calling any and all Adventurers.**] His Royal Highness, |KingQ>[**King Adalrico Dominus II**], Monarch of Holstein and Ruler of Reliquim, requests a **Investigation of the upmost importance** in the Hixborough Mountains to the North of the capital. Willing adventurers are encouraged to report to the Innkeeper, where they will recieve further instructions. Please be prepared to meet potential danger therein. The Kingdom is not responsible for your death if you depart unprepared. |KingQ>[**Reward: 500 Gold (Subject to terms.)**]]] The quest seems simple. A tug on your leg causes you to look down at Kami, who offers an expression of worry. |Kami>["This quest worries me, master. I feel a deep concern for what might happen if you go. I don't know how to describe it. I think your kind refer to it as a... 'gut feeling'."] That's the first time you've ever heard Kami so concerned. It's up to you whether you choose to go on this mission or not. [[Accept.->MQu21]] [[Decline.->SIB]]You decide that you might as well attempt the quest. The reward money is considerable, but what worries you more is Kami's fears about it. Perhaps there is more to the quest than meets the eye, and you feel it requires your attention. Taking the pamphlet, you make your way over to the counter and greet the innkeeper. The burly, muscular shark that stands behind the wooden counter takes the pamphlet from your hand and squints. "This is quite the nasty task," He remarks, observing your determined expression. "But if you insist..." "There has apparently been an attack on the Mountain outpost to the north, at Heaven's Reach. The king hasn't the strength of a suitable army to take up there, so he's asking for adventurers to deal with the problem." You ask him what kind of attack it was, and he shrugs. "I heard rumours of some sort of mage and even a dragon. I'd recommend not going up there without some sort of backup." You give him your thanks and turn to leave, Kami following no suit. The minute you stepped outside, you feel a tug on the hem of your tunic. You turn your head to see Kami looking up at you, a whine escaping his lips. |Kami>["This quest worries me. If a mage and a Dragon are in leagues with each other, then a Magister is likely involved."] You're inclined to agree. You're not exactly a hero, but you owe it to yourself to get to the bottom of this. Kami notices the glint in your eyes and bows his head in resignation. |Kami>["Then I will at least accompany you. If there are enemies, then I will defend you."] You bow your head in thanks. If he aims to protect you, then perhaps it's best get your equipment in order. [[Check Kami's Equipment.->MQu2Gear]] [[Don't check your gear, and set off.->MQu22]]Misfit is at a loss for words, and you can easily tell why. Spread out before you is a small clearing with some of the richest plantlife you've ever seen. Colourful flowers dot around the extensively grassy clearing, with vibrant fungi nestled comfortably against the trunks of the surrounding trees. At the back, hidden between two reasonably thick trees, sits a mesmerising waterfall and just enough of a pool to sit in and enjoy the refreshing feeling of running water. There, carefully draped across the grass in the most perfect spot, lay a quilt of red and orange hues, its edges frayed. Atop it's soft covers sat a small woven basket, its contents full of flasks of drink and pieces of food. You turn your head towards Misfit to find him gawking at you, his eyes wide. He hadn't expected such a heartfelt thing from you. His expression makes you grin and you grab him by the hand, tugging him towards the quilt. He nervously takes a seat on it, sitting cross-legged. You take the opportunity to slide in beside him, and Kami settles down nearby. "You did all this for me?" Misfit asked quietly, his voice barely above a whisper. You tell him you did, and his smile broadens. He wordlessly plants a kiss atop your head, and you feel his arms curl around your body and hold you tight. You can hear the hammering of his heart and warm, gentle breaths. You could stay like this forever. [[Continue.->MisfitPicnic3]]There are no shenanigans. No raunchy activities. The two of you are far too smitten and far too relaxed to even consider it. You spend the majority of the afternoon talking and sleeping under the warmth of the sun above and the heat of the forest around you. As it grows later, the two of you strip and take a relaxing dip in the lake. It's one of the few times you get to see Misfit naked, and he's ever bit as handsome as his form-fitting cloak seemed to imply: he has a shapely athletic body and a bristly amount of fur all over his body. He seems more than happy to show off, and you find yourself in a fit of giggles as his silly 'show'. The night soon begins to draw near, and as much as the two of you want to stay there for a little while longer, you know better than to stay out this late. Even if you wanted to, Kami seems intent on making you go, his whines cutting through the romantic atmosphere somewhat. You can see the apologetic glint in your companion's eye, however, so you don't scold him for it. You and Misfit return from the forest and back towards the city, once again moving through the now thinned crowd of people, with Misfit covering his head once again. You slip back into another alleyway, a known spot for Misfit to get to and from the sewers of the city without much difficulty. He pauses for a moment, standing in front of you, before he grasps you by the arms and pulls you in. "Thank you." He mumbles quietly, dipping his head to briefly kiss along your cheek. You gently carress his hips and he eventually pulls back. He casts you a cocky grin before wishing you a goodnight, for now. With a smile, you turn and leave, feeling all the more lighter. He seems faster.(set:$KHelm to "Speedband")]]Before heading out on your quest to Heaven's Reach, you decide it's best to make sure Kami is well prepared as well. You've not got the strength to fight for yourself, but at least Kami might be able to protect you with the right equipment. Kami currently has $KMH Maximum Health, $KA Attack, $KS Speed and $KR Armour. (link:"Helm:")[(goto:"KEquipHelm")] $KHelm. (link:"Collar:")[(goto:"KEquipCollar")] $KCollar. (link:"Paws:")[(goto:"KEquipPaws")] $KPaws. (link:"Back:")[(goto:"KEquipBack")] $KBack. When you're satisfied with Kami's equipment, you should head towards the carriage. [[Set off on your journey.->MQu22]] [[Return for now.->SIB]]Satisfied with Kami's equipment, you decide to head out. A carriage is likely necessary to make your way to the north, so you head towards the stables. Getting a carriage was easy enough. You thought it might cost you money, but a driver offered you transport for free, once he knew of your quest. Though, he did look a little worried that you were going without any armour or equipment. The journey took a couple of days, but that wasn't surprising. You had ample time to relax in the back of the man's carriage and sleep, with Kami draped across your knees, protecting you. By the time you reached the base of Heaven's Reach, it was nearing nightfall. Luckily for you, there lay a village at the base of the mountain, known as Extola. You paid for a night to rest before setting out the next day. What surprised you the most was the lack of other adventurers in the vicinity. You cannot find a single well-equipped explorer in sight. Perhaps they were too scared about the prospect of going up against a Dragon. You begin to doubt that this was a good idea. You decide to sleep on it. The bed is comfy enough to sleep on, and you manage to get a few hours of sleep, amidst your own nerves. When dawn finally breaks, you set off. Kami watches you from the side as you leave the village and move towards the base of the mountain, finding a suitable spot to ascend. There seems to be plenty of places to climb, and plenty of flat surfaces that seem to incline upwards. Climbing the mountain should be easy. |Kami>["Allow me to take the lead, master, in the event we are ambushed."] You nod and let him step ahead of you. With a deep breath, you begin climbing the mountain.(set:$KH to $KMH) [[Continue.->Gnoll]]You begin to make your way up the mountain, with Kami leading the way. The first section of the path leads you and your companion alone a fairly steep slope that takes you from the grassy knoll at the base of the mountain and up towards the rocky regions above. You don't find much on your way up. The mountain is lacking in any sort of herbal or medicinal ingredients, nor does it offer any reagents or riches. In fact, the majority of the place is pretty barren. One thing you do notice, however, is a series of scorch marks along the walls as you ascend. It seems that the rumors of Dragons might be true after all. As you ascend, you begin to heard something. A scuffling that makes you pause and grit your teeth. Slowly, you turn, and it seems like it was just time. Something launches itself towards you and you duck, dodging to the right just in time for it to fly over your head. Kami snarls at the sight of an enemy and turns to face it, just as you whirl to finally catch a climpse of what attacked you. It seems as if something followed you up the mountain. If you had to guess, it's a Gnoll, likely seeking treasure. You've known Gnolls to be burly brutes covered in brown fur and sharp teeth, but this one seems slender, and actually quite young. It turns to face you and grits its teeth, baring its fangs and brandishing a crude spear, it's tawny cream/brown fur a little dirty and dishevelled. Cloth and bandages cover it's extremities and protect his paws and hands. You ready yourself for a fight. You quickly make your way towards the village nearby, your muscles burning with the physical exertion, aching for rest. Eventually, you can take no more. You stagger and flop down near a tree, sliding towards the trunk and nestling yourself down. Kami curls up against you. |Kami>["Let's rest for now and try another time, Master."] You slowly nod, letting out a quiet sigh. You decide it's best to regroup and potentially acquire better equipment. You might have a better shot if you take your time. With a sharp snarl, Kami leaps forward and his teeth sink into the gnoll's arm. You hear the creature let out a wild yelp of pain. YA pang of guilt and compassion shoots through you, and you call out a command for Kami to back off. The canine immediately obeys you, relinquishing his grip and stepping backwards. You step a little closer to the gnoll, who snarls and tries to point his spear towards you. It takes very little effort for you to grab the weapon off of him, his weak grip making him unable to hold onto it. You toss it to the side, leaving him unarmed. There's no real need for you to do anything. The gnoll bares a series of claw and bite marks, but none of them are particularly deep. The teen will easily survive with just those few scapes. He bares his teeth at you, but the submission in his eyes makes it clear that he has no intention of fighting you now. It's up to you whether you decide to leave or potentially do something to him. One quick look at the gnoll's body tells you that he has little to no gold or treasures on him: the only thing he really has is the loose clothing on his body. A potentially lewd thought crosses your mind... (link:"Have your way with him.")[(set:$GnollFuck to true)(goto:"GnollWin2")] (link:"Leave him be and go.")[(set:$GnollFuck to false)(goto:"MountainPassage")]Kami lets out a quiet yelp as he jumps back, moving behind you. His back leg buckles and he crumples, flopping onto his side as you turn to face him, your eyes wide. For a moment, he doesn't move, and a stab of fear shoots up your spine and settles in his stomach. Then, he slowly raises his head, his piercing blue eyes staring up at you. A wave of relief washes over you for just a second, before he speaks. |Kami>["Master, watch out!"] His warning comes too late: before you turn around to face your attack once more, you feel the weight of something on your back. Caught by surprise, your legs buckle and you're forced to the floor. It didn't take much for you to realise that the gnoll teen was atop you. You feel its slender frame pressing against your clothed back, and the sudden cool edge of the spear points into your neck, warning you not to move. You suck in a breath and grit your teeth, knowing better than to move. His free hand runs down your body, across your clothed $body body and towards your hips. His slips a finger under your tunic to brush against your $skin. Your shudder and tense as his hand moves back down. A deep growl escapes his lips as you feel the warm, gentle pulsing of something against your behind, prodding against the cloth of your shorts. Once you realised his intentions, you began to squirm. He pushed the spear harder against your neck, enough to draw blood, and you feel a stab of pain that makes your freeze in place. Kami whimpers weakly near you, concerned for your safety. The horny grunts on the teen in your ear make it clear that you should stay still, if you value your life. [[Continue.->GnollLose2]]You order Kami to flee, and the wolf momentarily falters, if only for a moment. You gesture vigorously for Kami to take the lead as you back away, and the gnoll glares at you, snarling quietly under its breath. As you turn to run, the gnoll catches you by surprise. You feel the weight of something against your back and you're forced to the floor, a quiet groan escaping your lips as a little air leaves your lungs. You hear Kami bark and call your name, before it's quickly followed by a whine. You raise your head just in time to see Kami tumble to the floor. The gnoll must have kicked him when Kami came in close. You feel the chill of cold steel against your neck and you crane your head upwards, hissing through your teeth. A stab of fear shoots up your skin as you quickly realised that the gnoll teen has his speartip pointed at your neck. Knowing your life is potentially on the line, you keep yourself still. You feel his hand slide down your body and against your $skin, moving towards your hips. His fingers trail clumsily over your ass for just a moment, before they dip to the side. It feels as if he's going for your pouch. You feel the familiar jingle of coins in your pouch as his fingers toy with the small string that binds the top of the pouch together. (if:$coin is <= 30)[The gnoll feels the weight of your coin pouch and lets out a snarl of annoyance. You obviously don't have enough gold to satisfy him.](else:)[His hand plunges into your coin pouch and he tugs out a modest amount. You catch the glint of gold out the corner of your eye and guess that he took about 30 of your coins.(set:$coin to it - 30)] The gnoll heaves up off of you, and before you have time to whirl around and stop him, he takes off for the mountain ledge above. He ascends nimbly and dexterously, inevitably making his way up beyond your reach and out of sight. You wrinkle your nose and glance over at Kami, who is staggering to his feet. He seems okay, for the most part. With a sigh through your nose, you gesture him to follow you. The gnoll has left, so you might as well keep going. [[Continue.->MountainPassage]](if:$GnollFuck is true)[Having had your way with the gnoll from before, you make your way up the mountain path.](elseif:$GnollFuck is false)[Having left the gnoll be, you make your way up the mountain path.] You move around a sloping corner, the segment beyond seems to be a relatively linear path that moves upwards, with a number of dips along the path that you will have to jump over if you plan to keep ascending. You find very little in your way as you and Kami make your way along the path. You quickly come to the realisation that it might be worth getting into shape a little more if you plan to adventure on a regular basis: you struggle to jump large gaps and scale obstacles, when Kami makes the leaps effortlessly. It makes you sort of jealous, in a way. |Kami>["Master, over here."] Kami points you over towards a small dip in the wall that looks like a cave entrance from where you're standing. Sure enough, as you get closer you begin to see how deep it goes; it's a cave, alright, though not a big one. If anything, it's more of a tiny alcove with room at the back to shelter from storms. Given the bones and scraps of cloth you see scattered around, you'd hazard a guess that it used to be home to a den of animals, once upon a time. Kami is drawing your attention, however, to the backpack that sits at the far back of the cave. It looks a little bit in tatters, and a bony hand rests atop the pack, as if it were protecting it. You eye it warily, wondering if you should pick it up or not. [[Examine the backpack.->MountainPassage2]] (link:"Leave it be and move on.")[(set:$BackPackMP to false)(goto:"CliffFace")]The gnoll lets go of the spear, but he quickly places his hand on the top of your $species head, forcing your $nose into the dirt. His fingers curl around the $hl atop your head as he rolls his hips back, using his free hand to pluck at your shorts. You can feel the warmth of something between your cheeks and quickly put two and two together. His quiet groans in your ear tell you all you need to know. [[Continue.->GnollLose3]]The weight of the gnoll doesn't lift off of you, even though he's done with you. Instead, his hands move, running back down your body. You wonder if he's after a second round, but your viewpoint changes when you hear the familiar jingle of your coin pouch. The gnoll's fingers toy with the cloth string that holds the bag together, before he eventually loosens it and delves his hands inside. (if:$coin is <= 30)[The gnoll feels the weight of your coin pouch and lets out a snarl of annoyance. You obviously don't have enough gold to satisfy him.](else:)[His hand plunges into your coin pouch and he tugs out a modest amount. You catch the glint of gold out the corner of your eye and guess that he took about 30 of your coins.(set:$coin to it - 30)] Suddenly, his weight lifts from you, and as you whirl to face him, he backs off, bolting for the mountain's face. He climbs nimbly and expertly, his spear slung across his back. With surprisingly strength and agility, he ascends up to a plateau above and disappears from view. You quietly curse. You turn your attenion to Kami, crawling towards him and adjusting yourself. He is wounded, but not fatally: the gnoll did a number on him, but mostly just kicks and punches. Your cradle Kami's head in your hand and the wolf's tail wags, ever so slightly. |Kami>["I'm sorry..."] You shake your head, thankful that he's alive and can get better. You sit with him until he's able to walk, before the two of you depart the mountain. You vow to come back better equipped than before. [[Continue.->ScarHub]]You know it's wrong to do horrid things to a creature who's already batter and bruised by you own hand, yet you feel rather turned on at the thought of using his supple, teenage body. Though he seems unable to speak your language, he understands the intention of your glare well enough, and he narrows his eyes, uncurling his lips and keeping his mouth shut. You step a little closer as Kami eyes you from the corner of his eye, realising your intentions fairly quickly, having known you long enough to understand. He trots away, wanting no part in the lewd and forceful acts you're about to do. The gnoll doesn't move as you crouch down, staring into his eyes as you reach a hand out, sliding your hand under the scrap of cloth that covers his nethers. You push your hand up and your palm immediately meets with his swinging, hormonal nutsack. He immediately tenses under your touch, his eyes never leaving yours. You can see the mixture of fear and anger in his eyes, but when he recognises that you don't intend to harm his most precious instruments, he begins to relax. You roll them around in your fingers, feeling them. The gnoll had to be almost on the cusp of his manhood, given the size of his balls. It was either that, or the creature was larger than average. The gnoll's eyes lidded over slightly as your rolled the warm, fuzzy balls around your palm, curling your fingers and giving them the occasional, slight squeeze. He seems to enjoy the touching, for the most part. Slowly, you raise your $skined hand up and your fingers meet with the length of a cone-shaped member, which immediately twitches against your fingers. Your curl your digits around the length of the cock and the gnoll sighs harshly through his nose, settling himself back against his heels. He hesitates, before shifting his loincloth to the side, letting you get a closer look. It seems that, against its better judgement, the gnoll teen is willing to trust a complete stranger to satisfy his horny urges. You can understand to a degree, and dread to think what might have happened if Kami were to not prevail in his battle. It doesn't take long for the beast to begin whining and panting, his hips instinctively thrusting up against your hand like a wild animal. You're more surprised at how long it took him, more than anything; you'd barely been jerking him off for a couple of minutes. He might have been really pent up. The gnoll's breathing subsides and he closes his eyes, apparently relishing the feeling. [[Continue.->GnollWin4]]An awkward silence follows. The gnoll doesn't seem to know what to do, and you wordlessly pull your hand back, briefly admiring the seed on your fingers before wiping it into the rock beneath you. You have no intention of tasting it. Slowly, the gnoll scuffles back, eyeing you warily as his loincloth falls back into place of his slowly shrinking member. You made no motions to chase him as he slowly begins to rise, stepping away from you with each step. Eventually, he turns to grab his spear and darts towards the wall opposite you, leading up the cliff face. He takes one glance back before he begins to ascend as an impressive speed, digging his feet into small alcoves and pulling himself up through jutting out pieces of rock, until he reaches a plateau above. You watch him go and frown to yourself, wondering why you felt compelled to pleasure him. Maybe it was your Magister abilities tapping into him and sensing his need. Shrugging it off, you glance towards Kami, who trots towards you the minute you look in his direction. |Kami>["Feel better?"] He sounds almost sarcastic. You arch an eyebrow at him and question why you felt the need to do that aloud. He examines you for a moment. |Kami>["Even monsters as normal as those fall under your jurisdiction, Master. At least, I would assume so. Perhaps you connected with him somehow."] Kami confirmed your suspicions without so much as an afterthought. With a thoughtful nod, you gesture to him and the two of you continue your ascent. [[Continue.->MountainPassage]]You bite the bullet, as it were, and search through the backpack. You make sure to keep it at arm's length in the event something is hiding in wait, planning to ambush you. To your surprise, you're greeted to a pack of rations and hiking supplies. You remove the skeleton hand from the pack and drag it closer, loosening the rope tie around the top further and sticking your hand inside. You fingers meet with something dry and a little spongy, and you retrieve it. You recognise the thing in your hand as dried jerky; meat that had been left out to dry for some time and seared. Looking at it makes your head hurt a little, as if you're reminiscing about something you can't remember. You wince and pinch the brudge of your $nose for a moment, before taking another look at it. It seems edible enough, but who knows how long it's been here? You give it a tentative nibble and find that it tastes good. Turning to Kami, you split the jerky and give him some. If he was injured in the last battle, the food might help somewhat, though its certainly not as potent as standard medicine. (set:$KH to it + 4)(if:$KH is > $KMH)[(set:$KH to $KMH)] The rest of the pack offers pretty menial supplies that you already have with you, so its not worth taking. You notice a few coins at the bottom and take those with you. (set:$coin to it + 5) Satisfied, you straighten yourself up, and you and Kami set off further up the mountain. You feel a little better, having had some food. [[Continue.->CliffFace]]You and Kami get further up the mountain until you hit what seems to be an inevitable impasse. You're standing on a plateau that seems to sit just above where you fought the gnoll from before, though you can't really see him anywhere. You're a little high up, so you refrain from lingering near the edge. Two things stick out to you: the first is the complete lack of a path moving forward, but clear definitive platforms, almost man-made, that scale up a sheer cliff face in front of you, leading to another plateau above. You dread the thought of having to climb it. The second thing that sticks out to you is a mass sitting by the cliff itself, right at the bottom. You step closer to examine it and recoil when you realise it appears to be a mass of insects, roughly the size of Kami, all clumped neatly together. You shudder at the sight of them and your body tells you to run, but then you hear something: the sound of someone talking. From beyond the wall of insects, someone appears to be murmuring. The language is unfamiliar. |Kami>["I sense magic, Master. I think whoever is behind those insects are in trouble."] You'd be inclined to agree, and you want to help, but the thought of fighting the insects worries you somewhat. You have power, though you don't have a knowledgable grasp of it. You fear you might not be able to command the insects to move on your own, which might potentially mean you have a fight on your hands.(set:$Monster to 2) (if:$inv contains "Magister's Dirk")[(link:"Attempt to use the Magister's Dirk to dispel the insects. (50% Chance)")[(if:(either:0,1) is 0)[(goto:"CFBDirkWin")](else:)[(goto:"CFBDirkLose")]]](else:)[You are not confident enough in your ability to use your Magister powers.] [[Fight the insects.->Fight]] [[Flee.->FleePass]]You take out the Magister's Dirk and hold it in your grip. Like the other times you've held it, you immediately begin to feel fuzzy and your head begins to swim. Your vision blurs and the insects in front of you begin to glow, as if highlighted by your magic. You feel a rush of power and you grip the handle of your dirk firmly. You push out with your will, commanding the insects to move out of the way and disperse. In an instant, the bugs stop squirming, as if they've been caught in headlights. They scuttle and turn, before their backs open up to reveal beetle-like wings. They flap and oscillate as the bugs take to the air, forming a small swirling mass in the air. They hover there for a moment, before they make a beeline for you. It didn't work. |Kami>["Master!"] Kami leaps forward and immediately jumps on you, pushing you down into the rocks as the bugs soar mere inches from your face, swooping out over the plateau and circling around for another try. You scrabble to your feet and back away from them, quickly finding yourself pressed up against the wall. This isn't good. Suddenly, you hear a shout of a foreign language to your right. From the cave stepped a figure, but he was quickly obscured in a torrent of flame that swept across the sky, engulfing the bugs in a burst of flame. You wince at the swarm's ear-piercing shrieks, before they die down, the remnants of their singed bodies scattering to the wind. With the battle already over, you turn your attention to the figure, a bewildered expression crossing your face. [[Continue.->Akos]]You take out the Magister's Dirk and hold it in your grip. Like the other times you've held it, you immediately begin to feel fuzzy and your head begins to swim. Your vision blurs and the insects in front of you begin to glow, as if highlighted by your magic. You feel a rush of power and you grip the handle of your dirk firmly. You push out with your will, commanding the insects to move out of the way and disperse. The insects that surround the wall abruptly stop and turn slightly, as if looking at one-another, frozen by the power of your command. You twist the dagger in your hand and do your best to reinforce the command, guiding yourself by your instincts alone. The creatues remain frozen for just a moment longer, before they start to move. Scared by your command, the insects suddenly disperse, separating from one-another and rising up into the sky, where they take off in all manner of directions. You watch them go with wide eyes, a little surprised that the power at your fingertips actually managed to do some good. "Can't say I've ever seen that kind of magic before!" The figure from behind the wall, which you now realise to have been a magic-generated forcefield, steps out from a tiny alcove that he was hiding in and waves over at you. He makes his way towards you leisurely enough, examining the surroundings with a calculated eye. You're left a little confused. He could have easily dealt with the insects by himself, if he were able to use such a potent forcefield spell. Though, you're not sure how you know that. [[Continue.->Akos]]The swarm of insects prove to be too much for Kami to handle. They have a surprising amount of dexterity, and a combat power you weren't expecting. The minute that Kami begins to sway on his feet, you know it's over. One of the bugs separates from the group and swoops towards Kami, knocking him backwards. The wolf lands in a heap behind you and you turn, quickly running to him and crouching down beside him. He whimpers from pain, but he looks as if he'll be okay. His wounds are too severe. You turn your attention back to the bugs, who seem to be beginning to charge up for an attack. You wince at the thought of what might happen if they hit you, and you take a step back, holding up your fists. You're weak, but you'll beat them off if you have to. They swoop in to attack and your fear gets the better of you. You cover your head and hope they won't attack any vital points. You wonder if this might be it. Suddenly, you hear a voice, a foreign language that rings with power. Flames wrap around your body, but they never touch your skin, engulfing you in a peculiarly volatile warmth. The heat spreads outwards and swallows up the bugs whole. You wince at the sounds of their ear-piercing shrieks, covering your eyes from the blaze. The remnants of their bodies scatter to the four winds, leaving nothing behind. You look down at Kami who whimpers up at you, his eyes telling you he'll be fine. You let out a quiet, relieved sigh and instead turn your attention to the stranger who seemed to have saved your life. You feel admittedly bewildered: if he could use that level of magic, then why didn't he do so before? [[Continue.->Akos]]Slowly, over the course of the battle, the bugs began to drop, one by one. Kami's attacks, his bites and swipes, seemed to slowly be having an effect, as some of the insects began to fall, either dazed or bleeding out, until very few remained. They seemed axnious about fighting after a while, which made it even easier. It was inevitably over. With no more will left the right, the bugs flopped to the ground and remained there, dead or dazed. You breathed a quiet sigh of relief and wiped your forehead, crouching down to stroke along Kami's back, checking him over for injuries. Before you could speak, a wreath of flame suddenly swept across the plateau, and at first you thought it was aimed at you and your companion. You cried out in alarm, but to your surprise, the flames centered in on the pile of insects you had just defeated. You heard the quiet screeches of the still alive bugs burning alive in the flames. When they died down, there was nothing left but a pile of black ash. "Thanks for the assistance." You hear a voice and turn to see a figure stepping out of a small alcove on the cliff face, the very same spot where the insects were congregating around. Whatever the man had put up must have been an invisible barrier of sorts to keep them out. You frown and silently question why the man didn't just use the flames in the first place. He stepped forwards to greet you as you straightened up. [[Continue.->Akos]]Standing before you is a reptillian man, a stranger amongst the otherwise monster-infested mountains. He doesn't appear to be particularly chivalrous: he is lithe and lanky-frame, but he hunches over with the tell-tale signs of age. You'd guess he's around 50, perhaps even older, but its hard to tell. His scales are a rich mixture of black and brown that seem to blend in with one-another, giving his body an almost chocolate-y appearance. Clad in mage-like garbs that cover the majority of his body, the reptile looks you up and down, admiring your otherwise simple cloth clothing, the loose shorts and lack of shoes. He nods solemnly in your direction. "Hello there. Thank you for the assistance, though I could certainly handle them on my own." He remarked, his voice imposing and rich with wisdom. You remark aloud that he could have easily burned them from the start, and a chuckle escaped his lips. "I'll admit they frightened me a little, but that is the hidden ability of the Flying Timoris bugs. They have an aura of fear. I'm surprised you were able to hold your own, for the most part. I was able to fend them off when they got distracted." He steps back towards the alcove, where you notice a small pack sitting up against the wall, stuffed with all manner of supplies. (if:$KH is <10)[His eyes dart between you and Kami, noticing your companion's injuries. He retrieves a small bottle from his bag and offers it to you. It looks like a potion, so you feed it to Kami, restoring 8 health to him.(set:$KH to it + 8) He now has $kh HP.](else:)[He takes one look over and you and Kami and smiles wryly, slinging his pack over his shoulder.] "Are you heading to the top? I'd tell you to be careful, but you wouldn't have come here unless you knew what was in store for you...though I would have suggested better equipment for yourself first." [[Continue.->Akos2]]This man seems kind. You introduce yourself and ask for his name. You don't know whether knowing him in the future will prove useful, but he might be able to help you. His wedged snout grows into a grin at your introduction and he reaches up to idly scratch at the base of one of his small horns atop his head. "$name, is it? I'm Akos, Senior of the Arcpláge Academy. Pleasure to meet you." He holds out his hand and you give it a firm shake, offering him a small smile. He observes you for a moment before clearing his throat. (if:$species is "Reptile" or "Dragon")["I'm sure you have enough supplies to last you the rest of your journey, but I'm willing to offer more for a fee, of sorts." He clears his throat and you can gather the intention of his words. He seems to recognise that knowing glint in your eye and he looks a little embarrassed. "Let's just say an old lizard like me tends to grow some peculiar kinks over time, especially for those nearest to my own kind, like yourself. If you'll indulge me, I'll give you a little extra to help you along." You narrow your eyes at him and he shuffles on his feet for a moment. You glance down to Kami and notice the wolf looking away from you. He seems a little jealous, if anything. [[Accept.->AkosPaw1]] [[Decline.->AkosReject]]](else:)["I'm sure you have enough supplies to last you the rest of your trip, but in the event you need any more, I can offer you some...for a fee, of course." He clears his throat and his eyes hungrily run up and down your body. The knowing glint in your eye seems to catch him off-guard and he looks a little awkward for a moment. "Nothing too untoward, I can assure you...just something. I, uh...I can just go, too." You look down at Kami and notice the wolf looking away from you, almost as if he were jealous at you being propositioned. The man seems nice enough; he's surely not too much of a degenerate, right? [[Accept.->AkosSex1]] [[Decline.->AkosReject]]]You slowly nod, accepting his offer. You're not sure what the man has in mind, but you're more concerned with how you might get up the mountain without his aid, however little it may be. You'd rather take the risk if it means getting to the bottom of the quest, as well as making sure Kami is safe. The reptile beams and his eyes twinkle with delight at your acceptance of his proposal, and he gestures for you to shimmy into the little alcove he had been hiding in previously, presumably so the two of you can get some piece and quiet. You glance over to Kami, who nods at you knowingly. He acknowledges your commitment to the task at hand, and he doesn't plan to interfere. He doesn't plan to join in, either, trotting off towards the edge of the cliff to survey the land below. You step into the alcove and the old lizard puts his hands on you, manhandling you in a delicate and gentle fashion. He doesn't force you to do anything, but instead quietly guides you into a certain position. To your surprise, he guides you to the floor, urging you to sit. He looks over your $body $species body, lightly grazing his fingers along your $sc scales, grazing his digits along your hips and towards your inner thigh. He doesn't dip his fingers under your shorts: instead, he keeps moving his hands down, stroking along your relatively $body legs and towards your ankles. You quickly realise what his 'fetish' is: feet. Your eyes widen in surprise as his fingers drag along the tops of your feet, curling around to touch along the bare soles. Your paws are relatively clean; even the mountain rock and dirt cannot dirty them that much. His thumbs press into the muscle on your soles and he kneads them gently, making you awkwardly squirm. You can't say that you're that much into it, but he clearly likes it. You can see his flushed expression, his intense gaze... [[Continue.->AkosPaw2]]As much as you might want to, you feel it's potentially innapropriate to do such a thing with an utter stranger. You politely decline his request and his face falls. He looks genuinely disheartened, before clearing his throat and straightening up. "That's quite alright. I do understand, strangers and whatnot..." He looks away from you, and you can feel the awkward tension in the air. His gaze moves from the floor to the platforms above you on the cliff face and he offers a wry smile. "At the very least, then, let me help you ascend the mountain." You nod and give him your thanks, and he seems to brighten up, if only a little. He stands rather close to you for a moment and you feel fur brushing against your leg, drawing your attention to Kami, who has also quickly joined you. Sensing that something was about to happen, you tense. To your surprise, the earth beneath you begins to move. Rather, the layer of dirt atop it. To your surprise, you begin to lift into the air, aided by a platform made of pure concentrated magic and air. Akos uses a forcefield to lift you both up, carrying you above the platforms to the ridge above. In one fell swoop, Akos has managed to get you a substantial way up the mountain, to the point where there's not much left to go. You hop off the platform and turn to him, surprise to see him staying. "I must return back down the mountain...but I wish you the best of luck. Stay safe. Please." He smiles fondly at you and waves, the platform beginning to lower. You slowly wave back and watch him disappear from view. You glance to Kami, whose lips curl in something you'd guess to be a smile. You gesture forwards and he takes the lead, with you quickly following suit. Not much further to go now. [[Continue.->Pigorc]]Though a little reluctant, you agree to his request. His eyes light up with delight and desire, and a wide grin spreads across his face. He looks utterly overjoyed that you accepted his request. He gestures towards the alcove that he had hidden in before, considering it a particularly private spot for lewd activities. You glance down towards Kami and he nods in acknowledgement at you, understanding crossing his face. He knows that what you do is for an admirable goal. He gives you some privacy, stepping towards the cliff face and looking out towards the land below. [[Continue.->AkosFinish]]Satisfied with what you offered him, as it were, Akos rummages through his pack in an effort to find something that might help you on your journey up the mountains. He hands you some meats that seem to be treated with magic. He assures you they will never rot, and are good for a long journey. You thank him, though you're unsure if your lewd activities were really worth this. (set:$inv to it + (a:"Magically Treated Meats")) With that, he slings his pack over his shoulder and peers up the cliff face in front of you. He seems just as aware as you that it would be a hassle to climb up there by hand. Even if there are ample footholds and places to grip on to, you're unsure if your relatively weak body can handle it. "Well, that looks like a pain," The lizard remarks. "Let's fix that." You blink at him in confusion and stagger as the earth suddenly shifts beneath your feet. You glance over to Kami, who quickly bounds towards and steps rather close to your leg, the knowing glint in his eyes telling you that he's aware of what's going on. Suddenly, the ground beneath you lifts up-- no, it's not the //ground//, but the very air itself. Akos lifts the three of you up with a forcefield of sorts, using it as a platform and taking some of the grit and dirt with him. You wobble a little, caught momentarily off balance, before he lifts you higher and higher, until you reach the platforms above. In one fell swoop, Akos takes you up the mountain a considerable distance, to the point where this is very little left to go. "Hopefully this should do you well," He gestures for you to hope off, a smile spreading across his scaled face. "I'll be heading back down the mountain. Stay safe, little one." With a small wave, the platform begins to lower, and he fades from view. You rub the back of your head and nod to Kami, gesturing for him to keep moving. Kami shoots you something you'd best guess to be a smile and trots on ahead. [[Continue.->Pigorc]]Akos' hands touch with your legs and he moves them to the side, pressing them firmly together. With a sigh, he pulled his hips backwards and he shuffled away from you, fully aware that he made a considerable mess. He looked a little guilty and he offered an apologetic smile, grasping his robes and tugging them back up. "I'm sorry," He said, bashful. "It's been quite some time since I've...well, you know." You understand and quietly nod in his direction, and he slowly gets to his feet, clearing his throat and adjusting his robes until he looked as if nothing had happened. He took one look at you and then turned, his cheeks flushed. You quickly get dressed and cover yourself back up, aware of the stains on your $skin. It wasn't as bad as you expected it to be. [[Continue.->AkosFinish]](enchant: ?u, (text-style:'underline')) |u>[Magically Treated Meats] A set of meats-- more of a platter, really-- that have been magically treated to never rot. It's dry like jerky, but when you bite into it, it's rich with moisture. You acquired these from Akos when scaling the Hixborough Mountains.<br/>With Akos' help, you find yourself about 3/4 of the way up the mountain, with not much further to go. The path ahead is relatively plain, but it's strewn with various clumps of rock. It won't be smooth sailing to clamber over the various rocks to get ahead, but your have a certain degree of determination. Kami nimbly hops and jumps over the rocks as you follow along behind him, climbing along using a combination of your hands and feet. Your muscles begin to ache after a while, and it feel as if you've been scaling these mountains forever. |Kami>["Over here, Master."] Kami calls you attention to a thin slit between the rocks, and you clamber on over, peering down into the crevice beneath. Your attention is brought to what seems to be a ruined campsite, though you have no idea how someone might set up camp in such a cramped space. As you straighten up, you feel a mass on your back, forcing your down. You stumble and stop over the crevice, plummeting to the ruined camp below. Thankfully the drop isn't very far, but you feel a definitive stab of pain when your back connects with the rock. Kami leaps down to aid you, and as you srabble to your feet, another shape jumps down and lands on the rock, the ground shaking lightly from the impact. You gulp when you come face to face with a monstrous, bipedal creature: a Pigorc. Warthog-like anthropomorphic demi-humans with a penchant for violence and hulking muscles to help them do just that. Clad in nothing but thick shoulder pads and a pair of ripped, barely-holding-on pants, the Pigorc runs his eyes up and down your body, his sour expression and the lustful, almost murderous glint in his eyes making it clear he wasn't to be trifled with. Kami looks ready to defend you. You have a feeling your Magister powers won't help you this time.(set:$Monster to 3) [[Fight.->Fight]] (link:"Attempt to flee.")[(if:(either:0,1,2) is 1)[(goto:"FleePassPigorc")](else:)[(goto:"FleeFail")]]Kami lets your a sharp yelp as the Pigorc connects with his side again, the beast swiping his hand across your companion's flank and sending him flying into the wall. This time, it seems that Kami won't get up. You watch in dismay and worry as Kami whimpers on the floor, keeping himself laying down. Perhaps he knows better to stand up. Perhaps he's too weak to. A stab of guilt shoots through your body, followed by an inexplicable fear. You turn your attention to the Pigorc and quickly realise that he's looking right at you, his eyes filled with a mixture of lust and rage. His eyes hungrily look you up and down and you realise what he intends to do. You feel the fear bubble up into your throat and, althought you want to save Kami, you need to save yourself. You turn and run. You don't even make it a few metres before a mass connects with your lower back, sending you toppling fowards onto your face. Your chin connects with the rock and pain shoots up your jaw and along your spine, making you groan. You feel a hand around you ankle and you cry out as you're pulled along the ground and mercilessly flipped over onto your front. You come face to face with the lustful Pigorc, his snout sniffling and his nostrils flaring as he takes in your scent from above. A tongue lolls from his mouth and slurps along his thick tusks as his large, meaty hands run over your body, meeting with your shorts. He tugs and yanks with little regard for the wellbeing of your clothes, and you resist him initially, grasping at the waistline of your pants in order to keep them on. Sadly for you, his strength far surpasses yours: it takes him little effort to overpower you and tug your shorts from your body, exposing your $fcock. His eyes hungrily look over you for a moment, before he grips his hands to your legs and tucks them under your knees, forcing you to spread yourself. He settles in between your legs, and he keeps a firm hold on you as you try to scrabble back. [[Continue.->PigorcFLose2]]Kaim's attacks finally made an impact after a while. The Pigorc teetered and moved and inevitably staggered, leaning against the rock wall on either side of you. His legs gave out underneath him and he toppled, falling on his face. The bite and scratch marks over his body ooze blood onto the stone, dripping around him, but not enough to pool. You stand there and watch him, a rather sullied expression crossing your face. If he weren't such a brute, and perhaps didn't push you into the valley with an intent to harm you, you might have pitied him. He's not like the Gnoll from before: you can't muster the sympathy for a creature who doesn't care for life. You want nothing to do with it, so you let it fester in it's own blood. With a jerk of your head, you gesture for Kami to follow you as you turn, making your way down the small valley between the rocks. You find a series of footholds after a few minutes of walking, and you clamber you way up. When you emerge, you find yourself on the other side of where you were pushed in. You stretch your back, your muscles begging for rest, before you go back to clambering over rocks. It takes a minute or so, but the path inevitably smooths out, making ascending the mountain easier. |Kami>["Should we have left it there, Master?"] You don't see why not. It can cause no harm in that state. You assure Kami that it's fine, and the two of you keep moving. [[Continue.->Plateau]]You soon realise that you're not match for the monstrous Pigorc. You shout to Kami to retreat, and the canine turns his head towards you, giving you a form nod. You back up from the creature and then turn, bolting down the thin, narrow valley that you and Kami had fallen into. You ran, moving around sharp corners and jumping over rocks in an effort to get away. You can hear the loud thundering of feet behind you and quickly realise that the Pigorc is just as fast as you, if not faster. You turn your head just for a moment to look behind you. You see it, but it's too late. Your foot trips on a rock and you stumble, falling over and landing hard on your front. You wince from the pain and Kami turns to face you, making his way towards you. Before he can reach you, a hand swipes down above you, striking Kami across the side. Your companion is flung towards the wall, where he connects with it with a sharp yelp. You call out Kami's name just as you feel a hand curling around your waist, lifting you off the ground. You struggle in the Pigorc's grip, but the more that you squirm, the tighter its grip seems to get. You whimper and shift and you try your best to get away, but it is a futile effort. The Pigorc is considerably stronger than you. You fear for what it might do, but its intentions become clear rather quickly. You can feel its free hand gripping around your body, touching against your body, before his fat fingers meet with the waist of your shorts. He tugs harshly against your shorts and you try to resist him. Once you begin to feel the fabric tearing, you straighten your legs out. You don't want this to happen, but losing your clothes would be worse. You feel them slip past your hips and knees, falling from your feet and exposing your rear end. Kami whimpers for you, but there's nothing he can do. You feel the Pigorc's free hand gripping your upper leg, forcing you to spread yourself as you feel the heated, warm mass slipping between your cheeks. [[Continue.->FleeFailPigorc2]]You soon realise that you're not match for the monstrous Pigorc. You shout to Kami to retreat, and the canine turns his head towards you, giving you a form nod. You back up from the creature and then turn, bolting down the thin, narrow valley that you and Kami had fallen into. You ran, moving around sharp corners and jumping over rocks in an effort to get away. You can hear the loud thundering of feet behind you and quickly realise that the Pigorc is just as fast as you, if not faster. You turn your head just for a moment to look behind you. You see it gaining speed and you snap your head back in front of you. In front of you lies a series of footholds that you can use to get up, and Kami takes the lead, bounding up the same steps himself and glancing behind him with every jump, making sure you're following him. With a degree of physical exertion, you leap up to the first platform and manage to scoot yourself up just before the Pigorc grasps your ankle. You clamber up the next one before the creature has a chance to make his way up. It seems to be good at running, but not so much as climbing. You find yourself at the top, on the other side from where you were before you were pushed off. You quickly bolt along the path and clamber over rocks, feeling your hands scrape and cut against sharp, jagged edges. You care little for your injuries and just keep running, the blood pounding in your ears. After what feels like an eternity of climbing, the path smooths out and you quickly pick up speed as you sprint unhindered, Kami taking the lead. The two of you round the corner and wait, panting and gasping for air. You breathe a sigh of relief when you don't hear the harrowing footsteps of the Pigorc chasing you. For now, you're in the clear. You sink to the floor and rest for just a few minutes, attempting to calm your nerves, before moving forwards. [[Continue.->Plateau]]You're nearing the end now. After walking along the path a little more, you find yourself at yet another plateau, but this seems to be the highest one up. You stare out over the land of Orbath beyond and you're momentarily captivated by the sight of the lush foliage and plains in the afternoon sun. A smile plays across your lips, before you turn to face Kami, who's standing looking at the same thing. |Kami>["Can we rest here for a while?"] You don't think you can recall a time that Kami asked you for anything, so you're inclined to agree, perhaps a little too eagerily. You hate to admit it, but your muscles ache and beg for rest. Getting some time to relax might help the both of you. You step across the plateau, where a rocky overhang above you provides ample shading from the sun above. You scoot in underneath it and rest up against the wall. Kami trots over to you and he curls up against your side, resting his head on your lap. (if:$inv contains "Pain Leaves")[|Leaves>[You can feed Kami the Pain Leaves to heal him. He currently had $kh health. (10 HP.)](clickreplace:?Leaves)[You feed Kami the leaves and it heals him a bit.(set:$KH to it + 10)(set:$inv to it - (a:"Pain Leaves")) He now has $kh health.]](else:)[<!--Do Nothing--!>] It's kind of nice; you don't often get to relax with Kami like this. Well, you //could//, but you never really find the time. You run your hand over his head and his tail lightly wags from the touch. The softness of his fur and the warmth of his body makes you feel a little groggy after a while, until you eventually find your eyes drooping. Before you realise it, sleep has taken you, and you fall into a light slumber. Your dreams are haunted by a cloaked figure and long, neverending hallways. [[Continue.->PigorcFLose3]]It was a feeling like no other, mixed with an intense dread that made sure you'd never forget this moment, for as long as you lived. A searing pain spread throughout your loins that made you squirm and whimper, but the firm grip on your body and the Pigorc's rough fingers made it impossible to get away. The monster was ferocious and lustful, his wild bucks and thrusts driven by own extreme desire to breed and feel pleasure. His wild humps soon turn wet and audible, feeling the eerie quiet of the rocky valley with your own whimpers and the wet sound of the Pigorc's forced sex. The Pigorc leans forward and plants himself on his feet, plunging down into you with a renewed ferocity. His hands return to your legs and you are, effectively, supported by your shoulders alone. You feel the tears forming in your eyes as the newly-found angle the Pigorc has taken makes you feel more sore than before. The Pigorc's grunts grow more intense, his thrusts more irregular. You can tell he's close. [[Continue.->PigorcFLose4]]The Pigorc begins to grunt with more intensity, and his thrusts begin to slow. He plunges deeper than before, pushing down until he hilts with your behind, his veiny cock fulled embedded in you. Your innards instinctively squeeze around him and you feel your shoulders begin to ache from the awkward position. You're treated to nothing but the monster's sickening, slovenly face above you, its features clad in sweat, eyes wide and hungry. Then, suddenly, it orgasms: thick spurts of seed shoot into you, a warmth spreading from your stomach and up to your chest. You can see the bulging in your stomach, the way it twitches and throbs with each spurt. Your innards are splattered with gooey Pigorc seed and you grit your teeth, forced to bear and endure it. The monster snorts and oinks, thoroughly pleased with being able to get off, before he abruptly pulls back, withdrawing from you completely. Your lower end flops down as the Pigorc steps back, stepping away from you. Your ankles and ass hit the floor and you feel the gooey wetness of cum seeping from your hole, pooling around your behind. Your shorts lay in a heap nearby and the Pigorc looms over you, watching you for a moment. You feel a stab of fear swelling up but, to your surprise, the creature steps away. With that, it turns and skulks off, its hefty nuts and thick, dripping cock swinging between its legs. You watch it go with a mixture of bewilderment and relief, before it dawns on you. Kami. You scrabble over to him and he raises his head, albeit it only a little, to greet you. Bruises line his body underneath his white fur and you realise that he can't go on. He needs medical attention. |Kami>["I'm sorry, Master..."] You shake your head, glad that he's alive. You sit with him and stroke his head for a while, attempting to recuperate your own strength, before you haul him back down the mountain. Luckily for you, no-one gets in your way on the way back down. [[Continue.->ScarHub]]You awakre after a couple of hours to nothing but the warmth of a body in front of you. You blearily open your eyes, quickly realising that you're laying on your side. Kami is nestled down in front of you, the wolf in his own deep sleep. You press your snout into his back and curl your hands around his stomach, spooning him. You wish you could stay like this forever. The warmth of his body is intoxicating, and your nuzzle your head against him, taking in his scent. He smells like the mountain itself, but that's not surprising. It seems your touching has alerted Kami to you, and the canine begins to stir. A snort escapes his nose as his eyes blink open. He registers the sensation of your touch against his body and his tail begins to wag, slow at first, but quickly picking up speed. |Kami>["Master..."] You run your hand up and down, petting his belly and chest, and the canine lets out a sigh through his nose. |Kami>["It's nice like this."] You're inclined to agree with him, but you know that the two of you can't stay here forever. As much as you'd like to just enjoy the warmth of your companion's body, the rest of the mountain awaits you. You slowly sit up, and Kami does the same. The wolf rolls to his feet and you heave yourself up, feeling a little groggy. You're surprised to find that you're quite tired, but you haven't long to go. You get to your feet and set off, Kami following suit. [[Continue.->MQu2Finish]]There's a small path that you managed to make your way up to, it's incline steep and tiring. You clambered up, scrabbling and scraping with your hands and feet. The steep path eventually smooths out and grows less steep over time. The path narrows for a moment, before leading out to a relatively spacious area, almost like an arena. It seems to be the top of the mountain, though it's not exactly a peak: that's reserved for the point just behind this peculiar arena. You can see it in the distance. Maybe the mountains were reserved for military training a long time ago. Your attention is quickly drawn to a figure at the end arena of the area, a hooded mage of some sort, dressed in fine robes. Kami snarls in the direction of the figure and you know, for sure, that this person is bad news. You step closer and the figure turns towards you, its face obscured by its hood. You feel a chill run up your spine, as if its looking into your very soul. |DarkM>["A fellow Magister...how cumbersome."] The figure reaches its hand up towards the sky and the ground suddenly begins to rumble, knocking you off balance. You wobble on the spot for a moment and your eyes widen at the sight before you. From the back of the arena, near the pointed top, a terrifyingly large figure emerges, swathed in fearful energy. Its mouth opens and it unleashes a terrifying roar, making the earth quake. The beast arches its back and wings stretch from its back, covering the afternoon sky. You soon realise that its a Dragon, and a massive, evil one at that. You know you can't run from this one. You need to stop this beast before it unleashes havoc on the world. Fear bubbles in your throat and you grit your teeth, swallowing your nerves.(set:$Monster to 4) [[Fight.->Fight]]Your efforts are futile, as much as you tried. The Dragon is too powerful and too fast, and its hide is too thick. You attempts to do anything are in vain and you soon realise that you're not up to the task. The hooded figure raises his hand and casts it down, commanding the Dragon to attack. You leap to the right as a claw swipes down to attack you, its digits narrowly missing you. As you try to scrabble to your feet, the Dragon takes another shot at you. You know you're too slow to dodge his attack this time. A mass pushes at you from the side, knocking you away and onto the rocks. You hit the ground hard and turn just in time to see Kami, bounding through the air, his eyes filled with determination. He had knocked you to the side, leaving him in the trajectory of the Dragon's attack. Everything seemed to move in slow motion. You felt numb, watching with no way to help as the Dragon's fist and claws connected with Kami's side. You scream out his name and watch in sheer horror as the wolf was flung across the arena and right off the edge of the mountain. You had no idea what to say, your mouth open in a silent scream. You can already feel the tears forming in your eyes, but you have no time to shed them. Before you can even move, you feel yet another mass connecting with your other side. The Dragon's claws hit with your body and you're flung high into the air, careening off the side of the mountain and plummeting to the ground below. You see the sky above you, the wind howling in your ears. The last thing you see before your vision fades is the silhouette of beating wings. [[Continue.->IoDIntro]]Everything was dark for the longest time. For a while, you thought you were dead. You couldn't feel your hands, your feet, not even your whole body. Your mind felt as if it were sailing in a vast sea of emptiness. You felt like crying, like grieving. Kami was dead. Of that, you were sure of it. A wave of sadness washed over you and you don't know if you can go on. Maybe you won't have to. It doesn't really feel like you'll come back anyway. After that feels like an eternity, you feel a tingle, the first sign that your body is your own. It starts in your toes, running up your $skin and across your body, like a flame. From the tingle comes a deep-rooted pain that makes you want to scream, but it fades as quickly as it arrives. Your senses begin to return to you and you suck in a deep breath, suddenly able to breathe. Everything about your body begins to ache, and your fingers curl, feeling the softness of sheets against your fingertips. It takes a great deal of effort for you to open your eyes, but you eventually do it. You find yourself staring up at the cieling of a small room, the polished stone above you providing ample shade. Windows to your left allow the light to filter in, illuminating the room. The bed beneath you feels so soft, so inviting...you can't help but close your eyes for just a little longer. When you come to your senses again later on, you feel a touch against your cheek, the feeling of rough scales against your own $skin. You try to lift your hand and your body begs you to stop. "Rest, young Magister." The soothing words lull you into yet another slumber, before you have a chance to speak. [[Continue.->IoDIntro2]]You don't know how long it's been, but you're eventually able to come back to your senses. You're still in the same room, but you've no idea where that is. Your muscles don't ache as much as before: in fact, you feel sort of refreshed, weirdly enough. You slowly begin to sit up and take in the rest of the room. It sort of looks like an innkeeper's room, but fancier. A vanity table sits at the far end of the room, with drawers presumably filled with clothes. The windows to your left keep the place well lit, and on your right sits an ornate wooden door. The bed you're in is clearly an expensive one, the straw mattress weaved expertly, the covers soft. You swing yourself out of bed and slowly step over to the window, your walking wobbly. You guess that you must have been in bed for quite some time. When you reach the window, your jaw drops. You're in an island. That doesn't bother you so much, but what concerns you more is the sheer amount of Dragons that blanket the sky and the land below. It's a land in which the two species, the anthropomorphic animals and the Dragons, live in harmony. You see them talk to one-another and help each other as if it were the most normal thing in the world. It's a captivating and welcoming sight. You reach down and grasp the handles of the window, but you end up pausing. Your heart stops and you remember something very important. Kami. You feel the tears brimming in your eyes and you lower your head, filled with an inexplicable grief. The sun above is suddenly blocked by a mass, and you raise your head. The sight makes you jump. It's a dragon. [[Continue.->IoDIntro3]]The Dragon is huge. Well, at least, its head is. If you had the guess, the Dragon's head is almost size of your body. It jerks its head towards the large window, implying that you should unlock it. You twist the handle and swing it open. "Hello there," The Dragon speaks your language fluently, eyes twinkling with a surprisingly amount of friendliness. "When you're ready, please head out of your room and turn right. Walk all the way to the end and into the main council chamber. Oh, and put on some clothes." His eye blinks at you, sort of like a wink, and you feel the breeze against your body. You look down and realise you're stark naked, your $body $species body on show. You quickly snap your hands over your $skined and the Dragon in front of you chuckles. "Don't worry. We Dragons can't exactly wear clothes, you know." He tries to assure you it's fine, but that doesn't stop you feeling embarrassed. You open your mouth to speak, but the creature just shakes his head. "Whatever questions you have, I can't answer them. Wait until you get to the council chamber, okay?" His maw curls into a smile and, before you have time to answer him, he beats his wings and takes to the air, flying away. You wrinkle your nose and step away from the window, just in case people could see you. You're not sure what this 'council chamber' was all about, but if you wanted answers, then you'd have to go there. You reach for the drawers and tug the handles back, revealing clothes therein. You take out garments that you think best suit you, or rather, ones that you're comfortable with-- cloth shorts and a short-sleeved tunic. With that, you step out into the hallway. The long corridor stretches out before you and you follow the Dragon's advice, you turn right and head down the hall. [[Continue.->IoDIntro4]]The further you walk down the hall, the more you seem to hear. At first, it was a quiet murmuring, but it soon turns into a full-blown, normal conversation. You pick up snippets of what they're saying, but it's hard to make out some of it. "...I see. And the scouts have reported nothing else? That's a shame." You finally reach the end of the hallway, which seems to lead into an incredibly large chamber, bigger than any building you've ever seen. At the far right stands a pair of double doors, and on the left, a circle space with room around the back, three spots considerable depressed from the weight of something enormous. Above that circular arena, and along in a strip across the top of the building, sits clear shimmering glass that allows the light to seep in above. Your eyes are naturally attracted to the sight of a massive Dragon, behemoth in size, that sits near the circular area at the end in one of the depressed spots, right in the middle. If you had to guess, this must be why it was called the 'council chamber': a number of people seemed lined up near the entrance on the other side of the room, scrolls and parchments in hand. The large Dragon, its scales a mixture of turqoise, sea green and blue, nods down at a man who spoke to him before his eyes snap over to you. You feel the heat of his penetrating gaze, before he raises his head with a certain degree of authority. "Magister, do come over here. We have a great deal to discuss." You slowly nod and make your way over, aware of the stares that some of the people in the hall are giving you. You try to avoid them as you get closer to the Dragon, who towers over you like his very own building. He lowers his head enough to not intimidate you as much, and likely to hear you a little easier. [[Continue.->IoDIntro5]]"I'm glad to see that you're awake. I was worried for a time there that you might not wake up. I'm Zexir, and I was the one who brought you here." You feel a tremor run through your body, and the first words that come out of your mouth are a series of croaks. You clear your throat and a man suddenly appears next to you, offering you a glass of water. You realise now how parched you are, and you glug it down, politely handing it back to him. "I presume you were going to ask how long you've been unconscious. 2 weeks." You feel the colour drain from your face. You mouth opens to speak, before you close it again. Your hands clench into fists. You're surprised, but also scared. You ask about Kami, desperate to know if he's alright. "Your companion? He is well, and has been for some time. You needn't worry." You feel a huge weight lift off your shoulders and a sigh escapes your lips. Your knees feel like they're about to buckle as that paniced surge of energy in your body dissipate, replaced by a weary heaviness. He seems to notice you wavering a little and jerks his head towards one of the nearby servants, who rushes forwards to keep you steady. You feel your strength slowly return to you, but now your head is full of questions. [[Ask where you are.->IoDIntro5c1]] [[Ask who the Dragon is.->IoDIntro5c2]] [[Ask where Kami is.->IoDIntro5c3]] [[Ask how he knew you were a Magister.->IoDIntro5c4]] [[Say you don't have time for any questions.->IoDIntro6]]You want to know where you are. You can already tell that you're on an island, one filled with Dragons, but you haven't foggiest where that is. You didn't even know such a place existed. "You're a the Isle of Dragons, young one. A habitat for us winged creatures and servants of them alike." He doesn't elaborate further, so you ask: why in all of Orbath would people want to serve Dragons? You don't intend it to sound rude, but the Dragon's eyes narrow all the same. "I'll chalk your ignorance up to Orbath's propoganda, Magister, but Reliquim and Adplagam alike send up volunteers to serve under us. I can assure you they get appropriate compensation for their work, and they are here of their own free will." You cast a glance over to the nearby servant, and the man nods in your direction, as if confirming the Dragon's questions. Slowly, you nod back, rubbing the back of your head. You feel kind of bad for sounding so presumptuous. You curiously ask exactly how many Dragons are on the island, and how many people. "The island is quite small," The Dragon replies. "But we have a village for servants and tradesman that are currently at around 800. There are over 500 dragons here as well." That's a lot. [[Ask who the Dragon is.->IoDIntro5c2]] [[Ask where Kami is.->IoDIntro5c3]] [[Ask how he knew you were a Magister.->IoDIntro5c4]] [[Say you don't have time for any more questions.->IoDIntro6]]You have no idea who is before you, and how important he is. You decide it's likely best that you find out, and the Dragon bows his head, as if he's greeting you for the first time. "My name is Zexir, Magister. I am the head of accomodation and day-to-day management of the Isle of Dragons, as well as head of Education. You could say that I am, in essence, the leader of this isle." You didn't realise you were in the prescence of an effective monarch, and you quickly bow your head in an attempt to be respectful. "Rise, young one. You needn't be so formal; I ask for the simplest of respect when speaking to me, and nothing more. I am no King, just a Dragon trying to help his people." He acts so modest, but you can see an amused twinkle in his eye at your behaviour. You quietly wonder whether he's really a leader, or just a big kid. "I am the most powerful on the Isle, and as such, I was considered a leader for that very reason." Zexir remarks with yet another twinkle, and you have a feeling he just read your mind. The thought makes you gulp. You soon realise you should introduce yourself, so you do. "A pleasure to meet you too, $name, Orphan of the Forest." [[Ask where you are.->IoDIntro5c1]] [[Ask where Kami is.->IoDIntro5c3]] [[Ask how he knew you were a Magister.->IoDIntro5c4]] [[Say you don't have time for any more questions.->IoDIntro6]]There is one nagging thought on your mind: Kami. You want to know where he is, to know that he's safe. The thought of him hurt makes you throat close up. "Fear not, child. Your friend is safe," The Dragon assured you. "He is currently down at the village. He has been asked to stay there until your recovery, as we weren't sure what state you'd be in when you awoke." You're not sure what he means, so you ask him to elaborate. "Our patrol caught you falling from the mountain just as the Dragon attacked. We were lucky to catch you when we did, but your Magister powers unleashed and you became too difficult to carry. One of our patrol members had to knock you unconscious, and we worried you might have suffered a concussion. I am glad to see you are fine." You're not sure how he knows that for sure, but you take his word for it. Admittedly, you cannot even remember what happened after you fell off the mountain. You cannot even fathom the idea of unleashing your powers at all, considering the extent of what gifts you currently have. You wrinkle your nose at the thought. "When we are finished here, you can go and see him." The Dragon assured you.
