[[Ask where you are.->IoDIntro5c1]] [[Ask who the Dragon is.->IoDIntro5c2]] [[Ask how he knew you were a Magister.->IoDIntro5c4]] [[Say you don't have time for any more questions.->IoDIntro6]]You ask the Dragon how he knew you were a Magister, exactly. It's a well-kept secret-- well, as well-kept as you can make it-- yet he knew immediately what you were. "All magic in the world bears a unique signature," He begins, almost like a lecture. "And that signature can be tapped into and viewed by anyone with the magical knowledge and ability to do so. When our patrol found you, I cast out a net and caught the signature of your magic, which gave away what you were. That, and your little outburst as you fell off the mountain made it quite easy to tell." You fear that your role as a Magister might get in the way here, but the worry on your face seems to register with the Dragon, who offers you an apologetic smile, of sorts, and a quiet chuckle. "Magisters are a welcome blessing here, child, which is why you were personally seen to by my healer," He explained. "Magisters are mages who are, by design, friends to all creatures, relievers of suffering and hatred. They are also inexplicably rare: I wouldn't be so cruel as to cast one out, especially a child in need of aid." You feel sort of bashful, but also incredibly grateful. If his allies hadn't be there to help you, you might not be here now. [[Ask where you are.->IoDIntro5c1]] [[Ask who the Dragon is.->IoDIntro5c2]] [[Ask where Kami is.->IoDIntro5c3]] [[Say you don't have time for any more questions.->IoDIntro6]]Though he's told you Kami's alright, you feel as if you can't calm down until you see him. Your chest feels tight as you remember the way he soared off the mountain and out of view. Zexir seems to recognise your distress and he straightens up, not wanting to bombard you with questions. "We can talk a little more later," He began. "I'm sure you're anxious to see your friend. My servants will take you. Should you require rest whilst you're here, do come back. I'll have someone prepare a room for you." You bow your head in thanks and the large creature chuckles, somewhat amused by your politeness. Zexir gestures his head in his servant's direction and one of them steps forward, gently patting his hand on your back and leading you around. He leads you towards the other side of the chamber and out the large mahogany doors at the end. You stand at the entrance, looking down over the entire island. The council chambers seem to be built on a hill, where a path leads down into a small town below. From there, there appears to be a series of mountains and caves at the far end of the island, and an impressive arena just to your right, near the town itself. "It's down at the town," The servant spoke. "This way." It took you several minutes of walking to get to the town, and in that time, you saw a wide variety of peculiar things. You heard the scuffle and roars of Dragons fighting in the arena, the clamouring of dragons and non-dragons alike arguing at the fields and tilling the ground, harvesting crops all year round. The island was its own government, has its own economy, its own hierarchy. You enter the outskirts of the town and you're lead deeper therein. [[Continue.->IoDIntro7]]The servant leads you past various houses and shops and towards the centre of town, where a flat plaza sits in the center. You're captivated by the sight of non-dragons walking around, as if it were any old town. The dragons woop and soar overhead, but none of the townsfolk cower and whimper, none of them look up. Such sights, at least for them, are normal. You're lead towards a small building not far from the city center, where a sign sits atop an overhang. The words read 'animal doctor', and you'd guess that whoever works here also takes care of the Dragons, too. You glance to the servant, who gestures you inside. As he step in, the servant steps back and leaves. The waiting area of the room is pretty quiet, so you step up towards a small counter and ring the bell. From the back, past an open door, there was a scuffle of a chair scraping against the floor, followed by heavy bootprints. From the back emerged a figure, an Avian with pristine red and white features and a fluffy plumage. His feathered arms bore no kind of wings and his beak was rounded, like a parrot. His eyes widened at the sight of you and he straightened up, stepping quickly towards the counter. He doesn't say a word, so you inform him you're looking for Kami, your companion. "Oh, him? Sure." The Avian nodded and he turned his head, tweeting a little melody in the direction of the door. You hear the patter of pawpads against wood and your heart soars. Kami. [[Continue.->IoDIntro8]]Kami steps out from the doorway and your eyes meet instantly. Just like you, Kami freezes on the spot for a moment, captivated by your prescence. He bolts towards you, launching up and over the counter and right into your arms. You don't care that he's heavy, and you don't care that the weight of him makes you fell over. You're filled with an overwhelming happiness that knows no bounds. You fall to the floor with Kami atop you. You feel him lapping against your face, his tail thumping down against your leg, and you hook your arms around him. You sink yourself into his fur and breathe in his scent, a familiar smell that you've come to know as home. You feel tears well up in your eyes. You're glad he's alright. "That's sweet." The animal doctor sniffs and wipes his eyes with a feathered hand, apparently rather pleased to see the two of you reunited. You nuzzle into Kami's neck for a little longer before getting to your feet, your canine companion weaving himself around your legs happily. You felt empty without him here, but now, you feel complete. You bow your head and thank the animal doctor for helping your friend. Kami looks as normal as he ever does, free of scars and blemishes. "It's no trouble. If your little friend needs help again, you can come see me. The name's Erel." He bowed himself a little bit to you, introducing himself, and you give him your name in response. "A pleasure, $name. If you need to get back to Orbath, you can find a teleporter powered by Dragon Magic near the council halls. You can't miss it!" You thank him for his aid, and wish him farewell. You have a whole island to explore, and all the time in the world to spend it with your friend. [[Continue.->IoDHub]](if:(history:) contains "IoDHub")[You're standing back in the plaza of Icaria, town of the Isle of Dragons. There's many avenues to go here, but it's up to you where you decide to go. The wingbeats of Dragons overhead are already a welcome and normal sight to you, and the sight of them sort of makes you smile.](else:)[(if:$DN is "day")[You're standing in the middle of the busy down of Icaria, the only city on the Isle of Dragons. You're standing in a plaza with a mosaic pattern beneath you. The plaza has to be at least severeal metres wide and busy with people walking back and forth. There's plenty of places to explore. Shops line the center of the area and near the plaza, as well as a number of residential areas. You can see all the important places: a Blacksmith, Inn, and more. From here, you could move north towards the council chambers, wherein lies a teleporter, or towards the arena nearby. The sounds of Dragons roaring still reverberates across the island, but it seems like a normal thing to most people. You can also head south towards the residential area where the Dragons live. There isn't much else to do other than that, but that's till plenty for you to do.](elseif:$DN is "night")[You stand in the middle of Icaria, the town on the Island of the Dragons. It is currently night time, and although the streets are quiet, the dark silhouette of beating wings above you is still quite a common sight. Most of the buildings around you are quiet and still, their lights off. People are likely home, but they're also likely not going to answer if you knock. That would be rude, anyway.]] [[Visit Erel, the Animal Doctor->ErelHub]] [[Head to the Council Chambers.->CouncilChamber]] [[Head to the Arena.->IODArena]] [[Enter the Icaria Inn.->IODInn]] [[Go to the Dragons Residential Area.->IoDHouses]] [[Head to the Icaria Markets.->IODShop]] (if:(history:) contains "RalnChat3" or "IoDQ1")[[[Visit the Archive.->ValiaArchive]]](else:)[<!--Do Nothing-->](if:$DN is "day")[You decide to visit Erel, the avian animal doctor who helped to nurse Kami back to health. It seems as if his shop is open, so you step inside without a second thought. Kami follows in behind you. Much like the other times you've seen him, the counter is empty and the main waiting room is vacant. You patiently tap on the bell that sits on the counter and hear the scraping and tapping of talons against the floor. From the back room, the avian stepped out, his eyes widening at the sight of you. "Oh, $name! It's good to see you, and your little friend too. What can I do for you? He's not hurt, is he?" [[Get to know Erel.->ErelC]] [[Leave.->IoDHub]]](elseif:$DN is "night")[You decide to visit Erel, the avian animal doctor who helped to nurse Kami back to health. Unfortunately, it becomes readily apparent that now might not be the best time. Considering that it's the middle of the night, it seems that Erel is asleep. His door is shut tight, and you'd feel bad for knocking so abruptly and waking him. Perhaps it's best to return when his business is open, if you plan to talk to him. [[Return.->IoDHub]]](if:(history:) contains "CouncilChamber")[You return to the council chambers, having visited them once already. You don't go inside just yet, instead deciding what it is you want to do first. Should you require rest whilst you're here, someone is bound to give you a room, as per Zexir's promise.](else:)[You make your way up the hill past Icaria and towards the council chambers that preside over the rest of the isle, at more or less the highest point in the entire stretch of land, if you ignore the man-made mountains to the south. You had plenty of time to look at it before, so all that's left is to mentally map out the key areas. You quickly come to realise that the 'teleporter' that you've heard people speak out is situated adjacent to the chambers themselves, in a fancy little house with an appropirately illustrated sign. You can already see a line of people forming and, around the back of the building, sits a handful of different carts, filled with cargo. If you had to guess, the teleporter itself was advanced technology and they likely didn't get it for free, else everywhere else would have one! The only other thing you can do here is potentially visit Zexir himself. As head of the isle, he might be able to answer questions you have about Orbath. Considering your memory still hasn't returned, it might be beneficial to see if he can help you in some way.] (if:$DN is "day")[It is currently daytime, so the chambers are quite busy. It seems as if everyone is up and active, so you should have no trouble finding something to do, or someone to talk to. [[Go to your room.->IoDRoom]] [[Go to the Teleporter Chambers.->TeleporterC]] [[Go and see Zexir.->Zexir1D]] [[Return.->IoDHub]]](elseif:$DN is "night")[The chambers are quite and dark. It's the middle of the night, so you have a feeling you won't have much luck here. Perhaps Zexir might be awake. Otherwise, you're sure you can find someone to grant you access to your room. [[Go to your room.->IoDRoom]] [[Go and see Zexir.->Zexir1N]] [[Return.->IoDHub]]](if:$DN is "day")[(if:(history:) contains "IODArenaD2")[You return to the arena. The thundering of bodies crashing into dirt echoes across the hilltop. Raln is there, as always, barking orders at sparring Dragons. He catches sight of you and snorts. (if:$RalnTrain is 0)[&quot;You again. Ready to stop waddling and start fighting?&quot; [[Train with Raln.->RalnTrain1]]](elseif:$RalnTrain is 1)[&quot;Back for more? Good. You&#x27;re not hopeless.&quot; [[Continue training.->RalnTrain2]]](elseif:$RalnTrain is 2)[&quot;Magister. Let&#x27;s see if you&#x27;ve been practising.&quot; [[Advanced training.->RalnTrain3]]](elseif:$RalnTrain is >=3)[&quot;Magister.&quot; He nods at you with something resembling respect. [[Talk to Raln.->RalnChat]] (if:$DN is &quot;day&quot;)[[[Spar.->RalnTrain3]]](else:)[<!--Do Nothing-->]] [[Return.->IoDHub]]](else:)[You decide to take a look at the arena that sits not far from the council chambers, atop a hill. You climb up the steps and make your way there. Already, you can hear the thundering roar of Dragons fighting in the arena and the very ground shakes the closer you get. It seems as if the arena itself was built for Dragons: there are sizeable perches dotted around the walls that a number of Dragons, big and small, are already sitting on and waiting. You spot a great deal of them bearing some sort of armour and banner around their neck and back. You ask Kami what he thinks it means. |Kami>["It is their insignia, master. So you know who they work for."] You've always been surprised how knowledgeable Kami is. Maybe he's just observant. A small archway sits in front of you, leading towards the main arena. You step into the short tunnel and peer out from the other side. A mass shoots by you, crashing into the dirt and taking you by surprise. It's a good thing you were standing in the tunnel, or you might have been flattened. You peer out again and stare at the mass, quickly realising it to be not one, but two Dragons sparring, rolling around with each other. "Stop!" A voice roars, and the Dragons freeze mid-fight, clambering to their feet. A large figure ahead swoops down and lands nearby, striding closer. He's tall and imposing, almost as tall as Zexir, with a mixture of red and mottled brown. His eyes cast over the two younger, smaller Dragons with a withering gaze. "Are you trying to //fight// each other, or //mate// each other?" He snarled, before suddenly turning his head in your direction. "And you," He began. "This is a training ground! Do you want to die?" [[Continue.->IODArenaD2]]]](elseif:$DN is "night")[In the dark of night, you make your way up the hill towards the arena. It's strange to hear it so silent, considering how often the beasts fight from time to time, training themselves to protect the Isle and their own people. You step in through the archway into completely darkness, but the sounds of scuffling feet catch your attention. The moonlight just barely illuminates a dark figure, quadruped, who seems to be bounding and swiping the air. You can only guess it's a Dragon in training. You're admittedly a bit curious. [[Approach the figure.->Hran]] [[Leave.->IoDHub]]](if:$DN is "day")[(if:(history:) contains "IODShop")[You return to the market plaza to find it more or less as busy as ever. Nothing seems to have changed much from the last time you were here, but you can't tell for sure unless you get in for a closer look. You might as well browse, so you decide to make your way in further, slipping into the small crowd of people and scanning the stalls. [[Go to the Magic Vendor.->IoDMStall]] [[Go to the Herbal Vendor.->IoDVStall]] [[Go to the Equipment Vendor.->IoDBStall]] [[Leave.->IoDHub]]](else:)[You make a turn from Icaria's plaza and head down a fairly wide street, carts trundling along to the right of you as you hug the left hand side. The first thing to note is that Icaria isn't quite as busy as holstein: there are carts and peddlers, sure, but there isn't nearly as many pedestrians walking through the streets. You take in a deep breath and are greeted to rather crisp, fresh air. It's nice. Your journey takes you to what appears to be another plaza in and of itself. It's arguably the larger of the two, but that might be because of the sheer amount of stalls surrounding the out edges and sitting in the center. If you had to count them on your hands, you'd guess there was at least 20. You glance down at Kami, who sniffs and picks up the scent of food nearby. You cast your gaze back over the stalls, lost in thought. You probably have a spare bit of money, right? What's the harm in buying a few things? You head deeper into the market. [[Go to the Magic Vendor.->IoDMStall]] [[Go to the Herbal Vendor.->IoDVStall]] [[Go to the Equipment Vendor.->IoDBStall]] [[Leave.->IoDHub]]]](elseif:$DN is "night")[The plaza is quiet and silent. The stalls are still setup, or at least, the furniture is-- the surfaces are empty and void of any goods or services, but the wooden tables and chairs remain. If you had to guess, whoever set up shop in these parts had their own 'designated' space. Regardless, there seems to be nothing here, so it's probably best to leave. [[Return.->IoDHub]]]You head into the Inn in Icaria. Though you have no need to pay for lodgings, bounty boards for various adventures are still posted here. The Inn is quaint and seems to be rather busy, whether it's night or day, filled to the brim with a variety of adults of your species and more. The place is too small for Dragons to enter, but there is a large set of double doors at the back where some of the smaller Drakes, ones nearer to the size of your average adult, seem to be congregating. You catch the odd glimpse of waiter bringing them large kegs of bear, with sizeable bowls to pour it into. Your attention is drawn to the bounty board nearby, and you make your way over. The board is about the same size as others you've seen, but you do note that it's a little more scarce. It arguably doesn't surprise you: you reckon that most of the quests listed are isolated to just the island itself. You scan the board. Two postings catch your eye. (if:$IoDQ1D is >=1)[The Dark Dragon investigation quest has been completed.](else:)[[[Zexir&#x27;s Request: Investigate traces of the controlled Dragon. (Reward: Amulet or 200 Gold)->IoDQ1]]] (if:$IoDQ2D is >=1)[The hatchling quest has been completed.](else:)[[[Erel&#x27;s Request: Feral Hatchlings in the Eastern Caves. (Reward: 100 Gold)->IoDQ2]]] [[Return.->IoDHub]]You make your way south, moving along the path towards the houses at the very back of the island, south of Icaria. All around you are caves and mountains, none of them bearing any sort of paths leading up to them. Considering this is the living space for Dragons, the lack of paths for non-dragons doesn't surprise you in the slightest. You don't fancy attempting to bound up the rocks, but you were curious about what was around here. (if:$DN is "day")[It's the middle of the day, so its arguably very easy for you to make your way around the place. At least you can see your footing, at least, as you attempt to climb rocks and shimmy along paths. You soon realise that it's difficult to get anywhere on your own, but you can access a few of the houses by utilising a small valley that sits through the middle. You can't really get up to much around here, but there are a handful of caves nearby to choose from. You'd better be careful about 'breaking in' to a Dragon's house, though. (if:(history:) contains "HranSS3")[[[Visit Hran at his house.->Cave203]]](else:)[<!--Do Nothing--!>] (if:(history:) contains "Cave124")[[[Visit Cave 124 again.->Cave124Visit]]](else:)[<!--Do Nothing--!>] [[Visit the lower valley caves.->ValleyCaves]] [[Pick a random cave to enter.->IoDCaves]] [[Leave.->IoDHub]]](elseif:$DN is "night")[It's the middle of the night. As much as you'd love to explore, it's very difficult to really see where you're going. (if:$Spells contains "Volition Light")[Luckily for you, you have a light. You close your eyes and will a light to form in your hands. After some doing, it eventually sparks to life, lighting your way. Even with the light, it's hard to get anywhere. Rather than risk invading a Dragon's personal space, you decide to pick the ones where you can see lights. There are only two in view, each of them numbered. [[Go to Cave 102->Cave102]] [[Go to Cave 124->Cave124]] [[Leave.->IoDHub]]](else:)[You cannot go any further. It might be worth returning when you acquire a light. [[Return.->IoDHub]]]](enchant: ?u, (text-style:'underline')) |u>[Zexir's Amulet] A jewelled and ornate amulet imbued with Zexir's own personal magic. This was given to you because of your Magister title, and grants you the ability to teleport to and from the Isle of Dragons at will.<br/>You're not sure why you feel compelled to ask, but Erel seems like a nice guy, and you'd like to know more about him. When you mention this to him, he seems a little surprise and his beak tugs into a small smile. "Let's get comfortable then, shall we? Come through into the back room." With a gesture of his feathered hand, he leads you around the counter and into the back. The overpowering scent of various animals hits your nostrils, and you brush it off as he takes you into a small room. A table sits in the center, with various tools on a counter in the corner. Erel slips into one of the two seats on the left hand side of the room and you sit in the other one, Kami moving in to curl up around your leg. "Well, I can certainly answer whatever questions you may have. What did you want to know?" [[Ask how he came to be on the Island.->Erelc1]] [[Curiously ask him if he's single.->Erelc2]] [[Ask where he learned to heal animals.->Erelc3]] [[Ask if he knows any spells.->Erelc4]] [[Leave.->IoDHub]]You ask Erel how he came to be on the island in the first place. You hadn't even heard of it until you came here. The avian smiles and stares past you, as if reminiscing. "Well, I was in a group of people who cared a lot for animals...and they knew of the isle before I even did! They told me of its wonders and I wanted desperately to help, so they set me up here. Well...they at least gave me transportation. I told Zexir of my expertise and he stationed me here in the city." You remark it's an admirable goal to help Dragons. You've realised rather quickly from going around Orbath that not many people are fond of them. "Though they can talk and have conversation, they are still animals, like your friend there. I need to help them when necessary, and you'd be surprised how often that." A tweeting giggle escapes the Avian's beak and he looks momentarily embarrassed, covering the end of his beak with a feathered hand. //Cute.// [[Curiously ask him if he's single.->Erelc2]] [[Ask where he learn to heal animals.->Erelc3]] [[Ask if he knows any spells.->Erelc4]] [[Leave.->IoDHub]]If your adventures in Orbath have taught you anything, it's that people seem a little more lax about the idea of sex. Despite your potentially better judgement, you ask Erel if he's single, or currently seeing anyone. Erel's eyes widen at your question and he awkwardly rubs the back of his head, looking away from you. You feel as if you've asked him an uncomfortable question, but his beak opens to speak, regardless. "I, well...It would be silly of me not to realise the connotations of your question. Let's just say that I'm not really interested in...well, you. Or many people, for that matter." You wonder if it's your age, or something else. You see Erel glancing down at Kami every so often. You quickly put two and two together, and Erel seems to recognise the realisation on your face. His cheeks flush and he lowers his head, looking down at his hands, abashed. You cast a questionable glance down at Kami, who blinks up at you innocently enough. The wagging of his tail is a pretty clear indication that he finds the situation amusing, to a degree. [[Ask how he came to be on the Island.->Erelc1]] [[Ask where he learned to heal animals.->Erelc3]] [[Ask if he knows any spells.->Erelc4]] [[Leave.->IoDHub]]You ask where Erel got his knowledge of medicine from. You admit that you haven't actually seen him heal anything in person, but considering the job he did on Kami, you suspect that he must be very good at his work. Asking about his knowledge seemed to make Erel look a little smug, the pride twinkling in his eyes. "I studied at a small academy in Adplagam, a long time ago," He explained in almost a hushed whisper. "Though don't tell anyone else that." He winked and you make a motion with your hand, gesturing sealing your own lips. He giggles at your admittedly childish response. "It was an off-the-books sort of thing...they taught me some basic healing magic and showed me how to brew my own potions, but I know it takes more than that to heal someone, so I studied anatomy in my own time." The more you speak to Erel, the more you're impressed by him. [[Ask how he came to be on the Island.->Erelc1]] [[Curiously ask him if he's single.->Erelc2]] [[Ask if he knows any spells.->Erelc4]] [[Leave.->IoDHub]]Erel smiles fondly when you ask him what kind of spells he knows, and he holds out his hands. He murmurs under his breath and, to your surprise, light blooms in his hands. It flickers and struggles to stay alight, before it blooms event further and grows brighter. "I know some basic spells...though I can't say I'm an exprt. Some of these are quite difficult to do, especially the healing ones." You curiously ask how it works, and the avian puts a hand to his chin, furrowing his feathered brow. "When you use healing magic, its sort of like pushing magic into someone else's body. You have to feel the injury and try to figure out how to heal it. You have to feel every single part of it, and will it to fix. You need a skilled knowledge of anatomy and how muscles and bones work to understand it." Impressive work. You nod understandingly and offer your respects to someone with impressive skill. Erel's cheek flush and he bows his head in acknowledgement. [[Ask how he came to be on the Island.->Erelc1]] [[Curiously ask him if he's single.->Erelc2]] [[Ask where he learned to heal animals.->Erelc3]] [[Leave.->IoDHub]](if:$DN is "day")[You step into the halls of the council chambers in broad daylight, glancing around for someone who might be able to help you. You spot a formally dressed, clipped man nearby and you step forward, awkwardly stating that you require a room. He seems to recognise you and bows his head, gesturing you to follow him. He leads you to the left past the main chamber, where you recognise the divets of where the elder Dragons typically sit. He takes you into a hidden hallway of small doors, each of them home to a lavish room. He stops right at the end and unlocks the door, allowing you access. With that, he bows and leaves. You step into the comfort of a private room and stretch, preparing yourself to sleep.](elseif:$DN is "night")[In the dark of night, you enter the council chamber's halls. You scout around in the darkness and are quickly startled by someone standing right at the entrance, dressed in formal attire with a straight face and dull eyes. He nods to you and you clear your throat. Trying not to speak too loud, you request a room for the night. He gestures you forwards and he leads you across the chamber. From the main chamber, he takes you to the left into the rooms hidden around the back, each of them small but lavish. He walks you down to the end of the hall and then stops outside one of the doors. He unlocks it with a bundle of keys and lets you inside. In the comfort of a small room that seems reasonably familiar, you prepare yourself to rest. Kami is already getting settled on the bed.] (link:"Rest for the $DN.")[(set:$DN to $DNO)(set:$DNO to $DNO2)(set:$DNO2 to $DN)(goto:"IoDStaySleep")] [[Ask Kami for sex.->KamiSS1]] [[Change Kami's Equipment.->IoDKEquip]] (if:$pk is >=1)[(if:$KamiExped is 0)[[[Experiment with your powers on Kami.->KamiExp1]]](elseif:$KamiExped is 1)[[[Experiment with your powers on Kami again.->KamiExp2]]](elseif:$KamiExped is >=2)[[[Use your powers on Kami.->KamiExp3]]]](else:)[<!--Do Nothing--!>] (if:$inv contains "Zexir's Amulet")[(link:"Use Zexir's Amulet to go to Holstein.")[(set:$AmmyTP to 1)(goto:"IoDAmulet")]](else:)[<!--Do Nothing--!>] (if:$inv contains "Zexir's Amulet")[(link:"Use Zexir's Amulet to go to Scarhaven.")[(set:$AmmyTP to 2)(goto:"IoDAmulet")]](else:)[<!--Do Nothing--!>](if:$inv contains "Zexir's Amulet")[You thought about attending the house with the teleporter that sits beside the Council Chambers, but you quickly remember that you own Zexir's amulet. With no need to go there, you ultimately decide against it and turn your attention to other matters. |Kami>["If you wish to go to Orbath, master, I would recommend using the amulet in a private area, like the room we are assigned. The same would go for when we are in Orbath, too.] Good idea.](else:)[You enter the teleporter house that sits next to the Council Chambers-- or rather, you end up standing in line for what feels like hours until you're eventually seen to. Inside the small hut is a designated area which a marked sigil on the floor, with roped banisters keeping the section clear. A dragon sits nearby, his head rested down and his eyes closed. His horns seem to be glowing with magic. A counter sits nearby and you step forwards to greet the person behind it-- a robed female magician, with a feline face and inspecting eyes. She looks you up and down quizzically, and you quickly inform her that you request transport to Orbath. Her gaze flicks towards a set of parchments on the table and she quietly goes through them whilst you awkwardly stand there. She pauses on a page and then straightens up, readjusting the parchments. "I've been approved by Lord Zexir to grant you this." She digs under a lip of the counter, where you can't see, to retrieve an amulet. A thick, midnight black cabachon sits in silver finery, with a thick chain, implied it should be hung around the neck. She places it in your hand and you feel the warm hum of magic against your $skin. You quietly ask her what it is. "An artifact powered by Zexir's magic himself, under his orders," She explains. "It is, in essence, a portable teleporter, which will include you and your friend, if he is close. Clasp it firmly in your hands and think about where you want to go, though our Master has highly suggested that you only use it to travel to and from the island. The risk of teleporting to the wrong place could prove dire." You bow your head in thanks and she does the same, offering a small smile. You turn and leave, for you've no use for the teleporter now. (set:$inv to it + (a:"Zexir's Amulet"))] [[Return.->CouncilChamber]]You glance down at Kami, and suggest changing equipment for the journey ahead, whilst you have the time. He agrees, and gets into a suitable position. |Kami>["Take your time in choosing, Master."] You look over Kami and his equipment. Kami currently has $KMH Maximum Health, $KA Attack, $KS Speed and $KR Armour. //Each piece of equipment has it's own stats. Health determines the extra maximum HP Kami acquires. Attack determines the strength of his swipes. Speed determines when he will attack, and if he will be able to dodge. Armour provides Kami with necessary damage mitigation.// (link:"Helm:")[(goto:"KEquipHelm")] $KHelm. (link:"Collar:")[(goto:"KEquipCollar")] $KCollar. (link:"Paws:")[(goto:"KEquipPaws")] $KPaws. (link:"Back:")[(goto:"KEquipBack")] $KBack. [[Return.->IoDRoom]]You crawl into bed and roll onto your back, pressing your head against the soft pillows. The matress is soft and inviting, and Kami snuggles up to you. The combination of cosiness and warmth lulls you into a deep sleep rather quickly. (set:$IoDDreams to (random:1,3)) (if:$IoDDreams is 1)[Your dreams are, for once, nice. You dream of warm summer evenings, laying on a bed of what feels like incredibly fluffy fur. You feel as though you could sink into it forever, and if you had your way, you just might. The fur seems to be breathing and curling around you, demanding more and more attention as time goes on, until you're practically buried in it. Yet, it's not trapping you-- instead, you can breathe normally and even feel elated. You awake from a restful slumber to find yourself spooning Kami. You sigh through your parted lips and curl into him, and you hear the heavy beat of his tail thumping against the bed. You know you have to get up soon, but...just a little longer is fine, right?](elseif:$IoDDreams is 2)[You dream of yourself falling, over and over. The wind whips around you and you can't hear a thing. You can't see anything above or below you. It feels like an eternity that you've been falling but then, suddenly, a shadow figure encompasses your vision and swallows you whole. You wake up with your mouth agape in an empty scream. Your body shudders from the horrid dream, but you forget it mere moments after you wake. With a sigh, you lay back down in bed and rest a bit longer, before inevitably getting up.](elseif:$IoDDreams is 3)[Your dreams are dark and silent. You dream of an endless void, an expanse of nothingness that float in, almost numb to everything around you. You shudder at the mere feeling of chill setting into your bones before you hear something in your ear: a whisper of a voice that makes your very soul churn with fright. It says only one word: 'Magister'. You awakre in a cold sweat, your body heavy from a restless sleep. Kami whines next to you and nuzzles into your side, trying to do his best to comfort your nightmares. You idly stroke his head and sweep out of bed in a sleepy stupor. Walking will wake you up, and help to forget the haunting feeling in your very bones.] It is now [$DN]time. [[Continue.->CouncilChamber]]You decide to visit Zexir. You have a feeling he'd be happy to talk to you about whatever you want to know, and perhaps what you want to learn. You step into the council chambers and speak to one of the servants nearby. You request to see Zexir, and they don't tell you 'no', explicitly; if you had to guess, they were likely informed of your status upon your arrival some weeks ago. They ask you to wait and you idly stand around with Kami, who sniffs the air and pads around nearby. Eventually, a servant comes to collect you and you're taken to the right hand side of the chambers, the opposite of where you stayed originally. You're taken down the hall until you reach a rather small door. Immediately, you wonder how Zexir might be in the room beyond when the entrance is so tiny, but you're soon to find out. As you step into the room, you're greeted to a chamber about the size of the actual council's own. You might only be guessing, but you presume that the size of the circular chamber where the council dragons reside and Zexir's own room is about the same. Sure enough, Zexir is sitting opposite you on what appears to be a gigantic cushion, laying on his front with his hind legs tucked in like a dog, his front legs folded over each other. He bows his head down to greet you and you offer him an awkward wave. "Magister. It's a pleasure," He smiles courteously. "Tell me, what brings you to my Chambers?" [[Ask if he can teach you about the world.->Zexir1DWorld]] [[Ask if he can teach you how to fight.->Zexir1DFight]] (if:$Spells contains "Volition Light")[[[Ask if he can teach you more magic.->Zexir1DMagic3]]](else:)[[[Ask if he can teach you Magic.->Zexir1DMagic]]] [[Ask if he can teach you about the Isle.->Zexir1DIsle]] [[Take your leave.->IoDHub]](set:$ZexNight to (random:1,3))(if:$ZexNight is 1)[(goto:"ZexirAltSS1")](elseif:$ZexNight is 2)[(goto:"ZexirAltSS3")](else:)[It's probably not wise, but you decide to visit Zexir at this time of the night. You step into the council chambers and note a number of willing and able volunteers nearby, but they don't notice you in the dark, even with their torches. You move past them towards the end of the hall and turn right, hoping that it's the right way to go. Your journey leads you towards a small door at the end of a hallway, and Kami moves in beside you, keeping himself low to the ground. You approach the door and listen intently, hearing nothing beyond. Slowly, you grasp the handle and open the door, sneaking inside, with Kami weaving in from between your legs. The room is massive, almost the size of the council chamber. You soon realise you're not alone when you hear the heavy sounds of breathing, and you turn your head to the far end of the room. You catch the outline of a massive figure in the moonlight and quickly guess that Zexir is asleep. Just as you're about to leave, the door swings shut in front of you, trapping you within the room. The shifting of fabric behind you alerts you to Zexir's prescence and you turn, wide-eyed, to see him slowly raising his head. His eyes glow menacingly, basking the room in an etheral glow. Then, suddenly, he stops, letting out a long yawn. "Magister. Why are you are as such ungodly hours of the night? Dragons also sleep, you know..." He sounds a little irritated, but patient. His glowing eyes flicker off and leave you in the dark once more, momentarily blinded. You'd better think of an excuse, and fast. [[Say that you wanted to hang out.->Zexir1N2]] [[Say you took a wrong turn.->Zexir1N1]]](if:$AmmyTP is 1)[You hold Zexir's Amulet in your hands and concentrate, thinking of Holstein. More than anything else, you think of where you first came into the big city of Holstein, it's vibrant city streets and welcoming people. You think of those you met and how you want to see them. You feel a tug against your very soul, and its as if you're flying through the air. You can't feel a thing, neither your own weight or the touch of anything against your $skin. The thought frightens you and you keep your eyes squeezed shut, scared of what might be in front of you. A sudden clamouring rings in your ears, and you open your eyes to find yourself standing the middle of Holstein's square. People are looking at your awkwardly and Kami nudges your leg, trying to get you to move. You clear your throat and deposit the amulet in your back pocket, nodding to your companion as you make your way out the square.(set:$Location to "Holstein") [[Continue.->Hub]]](elseif:$AmmyTP is 2)[You hold Zexir's Amulet in your hands and concentrate, thinking of Scarhaven. You think of the blacksmith and the smell of his burning coals and metal. You think of the rich countryside and the thick forest that sits at the back of the village. You feel a tug against your very soul, and its as if you're flying through the air. You can't feel a thing, neither your own weight or the touch of anything against your $skin. The thought frightens you and you keep your eyes squeezed shut, scared of what might be in front of you. Suddenly, you hear nothing but the twittering of birds and the thick, metal clanging of steel against steel. You open your eyes and find yourself standing in the middle of Scarhaven in all its glory. You breathe a sigh and tuck the amulet back into your pocket.(set:$Location to "Scarhaven") [[Continue.->ScarHub]]]You clench Zexir's amulet in your hands, feeling the warm pulse of the jewel against your palm, its magic seeping through you. You close your eyes and cast your thoughts to the Isle of Dragons and the inhabitants of Icaria. You think of Erel, the animal doctor, and Zexir, the kind and warm leader. You feel a tugging on your soul and its as if you feel nothing, neither your weight nor a touch against your $skin. You've experienced this sensation before and it frightens you. You keep your eyes squeezed shut and feel a sudden rush of wind around your face, followed by your feet hitting grass. You open your eyes to find yourself standing on the bank overlooking Icaria, just in front of the council chambers. You breathe a quiet sigh of relief and stow the amulet away for now, heading down the hill with Kami in tow.(set:$Location to "IoD") [[Continue.->IoDHub]]You step up to the magic vendor's stall. A wizened old raccoon sits behind the stall's counter, looking as if he's half asleep. He perks up-- albeit only a little-- when he sees you getting closer, and a cheeky grin spreads across his lips. "Want to learn magic, boy? I've got a few oddities that might help you...I can even provide some one-on-one tutoring if you like." He cackles quietly under his breath and you get distinctive creepy vibes from him. You decide not to say anything and, thankfully, he doesn't press for more information. (if:$inv contains "Dog Collar")[You have a Dog Collar that Kami refuses to wear, so you ask the vendor what might be wrong with it. He licks his lips as he takes it from you. "Mmh. It's cursed, boy. I can dispel the curse, for a price. How does 50 gold sound?" His lips curl into a smile, and you have a feeling you might be being ripped off. |Curse1>[Ask him to dispel the curse.](if:$coin is >=50)[(clickreplace:?Curse1)[You ask him to dispel the curse. He places it on the table and chants for a moment, and your eyes widen as you see a black vapour roll off the collar and dissipate into the air. After a minute or so, he pushes the collar in your direction, looking a little more tired then before. You give him the gold and thank him. You recieved a (text-colour:#82ecff)[Wolf's Rebuke] for Kami.(set:$coin to it -50)(set:$inv to it - (a:"Dog Collar"))(set:$KGC to it + (a:"Wolf's Rebuke"))]](elseif:$coin is <50)[(clickreplace:?Curse1)[You haven't the coin to dispel the curse. The wizard snorts.]]](else:)[The merchant remarks that can dispel curses as well, for a price. You don't think you have any cursed items in your possession, however.] The Raccoon offers a wide selection of magical items, and he makes a note that he can buy some from you, too. You can't see much that you want to buy, though. (if:$inv contains "Ruby Necklace")[|Sell1>[Sell the Ruby Necklace. (60 Gold)](clickreplace:?Sell1)[You sell the Ruby Necklace to the man for 60 gold. He stows it away and hands you your coins.(set:$coin to it + 60)(set:$inv to it - (a:"Ruby Necklace"))]](else:)[<!--Do Nothing--!>] (if:$inv contains "Silver Orb")[|Sell2>[Sell the Silver Orb. (30 Gold)](clickreplace:?Sell2)[You sell the Silver Orb to the man for 30 gold. He stows it away and hands you your coins.(set:$coin to it + 30)(set:$inv to it - (a:"Silver Orb"))]](else:)[<!--Do Nothing--!>] (if:$inv contains "Heart Ring")[You have already purchased the Heart Ring.](else:)[|Buy1>[Purchase the Heart Ring. (50 Gold)](if:$coin is >=50)[(clickreplace:?Buy1)[You purchased the Heart Ring. The raccoon's lips curl into a slovenly grin. (set:$inv to it + (a:"Heart Ring"))(set:$coin to it - 50)]](elseif:$coin is <50)[(clickreplace:?Buy1)[You don't have the money for this. The raccoon grunts.]]] [[Leave.->IODShop]]You spot a small stand with a variety of herbs and alchemical reagants sitting atop it, and you wonder if any of them might be useful. You have a few yourself that haven't seen much use. You step up to the counter and a brash, short looking mouse woman greets you, bowing her head in respect, even though her eyes twinkle and flicker with some hidden contempt. You're not sure why, but at least she isn't being deliberately rude. "What can I do for you?" She speaks with an edge and gestures to her wares. You look down and wonder what to buy. You can't find much, though. { (if:$inv contains "Strange Mushroom")[You remember the mushroom you acquire sometime ago, and you hold out the mushroom for the woman to look at. She squints, before her eyes widen in surprise. She remarks that it is a rare mushroom found only in magical forests. She quickly suggests you sell it to her for 40 gold.<br/> <br/> |Sell1>[Sell her the mushroom.](clickreplace:?Sell1)[You sell her the mushroom. She practically snatches it out of your hands in glee.(set:$coin to it + 40)(set:$inv to it - (a:"Strange Mushroom"))]]<br/> } (if:$inv contains "Empty Bottle")[You have already purchased the empty bottle.](else:)[|Buy1>[Purchase the Empty Bottle. (10 Gold)](if:$coin is >= 10)[(clickreplace:?Buy1)[You purchase the empty bottle. The woman smiles and takes your money.(set:$coin to it - 10)(set:$inv to it + (a:"Empty Bottle"))]](elseif:$coin is <10)[(clickreplace:?Buy1)[You cannot afford the bottle. The woman scoffs.]]] [[Leave.->IODShop]]You approach the blacksmithing stall, curious on what goods they provide. The man behind the stall, a large and intimiating bear, gestures for you to browse his goods. "Can't say I have much in your size, kid, but you're welcome to gander." You appreciate him being patient with you as you look over the goods. His eyes glance towards your wolven companion in a mixture of intrigue and amusement, before looking back to you. "If you're wanting armour for your pet, I might have a thing or two." Kami doesn't like to be referred to as a pet, but he seems to keep himself quiet about it, for the time being. You kindly ask the bear to show you what he has. He heaves it up from under the stall and places it on the wooden surface. (if:$KGH contains "Padded Helmet")[You have already purchased the Padded Helmet.](else:)[|Buy1>[Purchase the Padded Helmet. (20 Gold)](if:$coin is >=20)[(clickreplace:?Buy1)[You have purchased the Padded Helmet. The bear offers you a smile.(set:$KGH to it + (a:"Padded Helmet"))(set:$coin to it - 20)]](elseif:$coin is <20)[(clickreplace:?Buy1)[You haven't the gold to purchase this. The bear offers his condolensces.]]] (if:$KGC contains "Magic Collar")[You have already purchased the Magic Collar.](else:)[|Buy2>[Purchase the Magic Collar. (40 Gold)](if:$coin is >=40)[(clickreplace:?Buy2)[You purchase the Magic Collar. The bear smiles fondly at you.(set:$KGC to it + (a:"Magic Collar"))(set:$coin to it - 40)]](elseif:$coin is <40)[(clickreplace:?Buy2)[You haven't the gold to buy this. The bear offers his condolensces.]]] (if:$KGP contains "Obsidian Guards")[You have already purchased the Obsidian Guards.](else:)[|Buy3>[Purchase the Obsidian Guards. (80 Gold)](if:$coin is >=80)[(clickreplace:?Buy3)[You purchase the Obsidian Guards. You've just made the bear's day.(set:$KGP to it + (a:"Obsidian Guards"))(set:$coin to it - 80)]](elseif:$coin is <80)[(clickreplace:?Buy3)[You haven't the gold for this. The bear grimaces, but understands.]]] (if:$KGB contains "Padded Armour")[You have already purchased the Padded Armour.](else:)[|Buy4>[Purchase the Padded Armour. (20 Gold)](if:$coin is >=20)[(clickreplace:?Buy4)[You purchase the Padded Armour. The bear seems pleased.(set:$KGB to it + (a:"Padded Armour"))(set:$coin to it - 20)]](elseif:$coin is <20)[(clickreplace:?Buy4)[You haven't the gold for this. The bear understands.]]] [[Leave.->IODShop]](enchant: ?u, (text-style:'underline')) |u>[Padded Armour] A set of back armour for Kami that provides ample defense without sacrificing too much speed. Utilising this armour grants Kami 5 Maximum Health, -2 Speed and 3 Armour. (if:$KHelm is "Padded Armour")[Kami already has this equipped.](else:)[|EBH>[Equip the Padded Armour.](clickreplace:?EBH)[You have equipped the Padded Armour to Kami. He seems sturdier.(set:$KBack to "Padded Armour")]](enchant: ?u, (text-style:'underline')) |u>[Obsidian Guards] A set of durable guards made from the near-unbreakable material known as Obsidian. They provide immeasurable defense at the cost of a lot of speed. Utilising these claw guards grants Kami 1 Maximum Health, -4 Speed and 10 Armour. (if:$KHelm is "Obsidian Guards")[Kami already has this equipped.](else:)[|EBH>[Equip the Obsidian Guards.](clickreplace:?EBH)[You have equipped the Obsidian Guards to Kami. He seems sturdier.(set:$KPaws to "Obsidian Guards")]](enchant: ?u, (text-style:'underline')) |u>[Magic Collar] A Mysterious Collar that boosts Kami's vitality, but offers little in defense. Utilising this collar grants Kami 15 Maximum Health. (if:$KHelm is "Magic Collar")[Kami already has this equipped.](else:)[|EBH>[Equip the Magic Collar.](clickreplace:?EBH)[You have equipped the Magic Collar to Kami. He seems healthier.(set:$KCollar to "Magic Collar")]](enchant: ?u, (text-style:'underline')) |u>[Padded Helmet] A slender, form-fitting helmet for Kami that boasts decent armour without sacrificing that much speed. Utilising this helm grants Kami 2 Maximum Health, -1 Speed and 4 Armour. (if:$KHelm is "Padded Helmet")[Kami already has this equipped.](else:)[|EBH>[Equip the Padded Helmet.](clickreplace:?EBH)[You have equipped the Padded Helmet to Kami. You breathe a sigh of relief.(set:$KHelm to "Padded Helmet")]](enchant: ?u, (text-style:'underline')) |u>[Wolf's Rebuke] With the curse dispelled, you feel as if this collar holds an immense power. It almost hums in your hands. Kami might be able to use it. Utilising this collar grants Kami 7 Maximum Health, 5 Attack, 5 Speed and 1 Armour. (if:$KCollar is "Wolf's Rebuke")[Kami already has this equipped.](else:)[|EBH>[Equip the Wolf's Rebuke.](clickreplace:?EBH)[You have equipped the Wolf's Rebuke to Kami.(set:$KCollar to "Wolf's Rebuke")]](enchant: ?u, (text-style:'underline')) |u>[Empty Bottle] A pretty strangard empty bottle. You won't find much use out of it other than for storing liquids.<br/>You quickly bow your head and offer the Dragon a fervent apology. The Dragon looks you up and down and snorts, turning his head from you and gazing off into the distance. "So you're that blasted Magister that Zexir speaks so highly of?" He remarked with a grunt. "I didn't expect you to be just a child." (if:$age is >15)[You point out that you're definitely just a kid, and the dragon bows his head, glaring at you with an angry gleam in his eye. His head is slightly bigger than your torso, and you've no doubt he could swallow you whole without chewing once. "You don't even have a weapon, boy. You're certainly no man. In fact, you're practically a baby, waddling around and craving mother's milk." His sharp remarks make you quietly seethe.](elseif:$age is <=15)[You hang your head and feel a pang of shame and guilt, and the Dragon sighes, regarding you from a distance, measuring your stature, perhaps even your resolve.] "Magisters are something of a rarity, what with the Wizarding Council gathering you all up," He began. "They are well respected amongst us Dragons, back in the olden days. Something of a mediator between us and those blasted politicians." You sent a small hint of compassion in his voice as he glares down at you, claws digging into the dirt. "My name is Raln. It would do you well not to die, little one. Return here at another time, and I will help you learn to fight." You bow your head in respect and he grunts, turning from you. He barks orders at the sparring Dragons, who begin their fight anew. [[Return.->IoDHub]]You approach the figure in the dark, getting closer and closer. (if:(history:) contains "Hran4")[You soon realise it's Hran, doing his usual nightly training, it seems. You call over to him and he turns to face you, bounding closer. "Hey, $name." He beams and dips his head, a wicked grin spreading across his lips. The two of you idly chat for a time, the usual small talk, before Hran inevitably stops beating around the bush. He looks bashful as he paws at the dirt, wiggling left and right. "Do you maybe, wanna...you know?" He grins sheepishly. [[Have sex with him.->HranSS2]] [[Take your leave.->IoDHub]]](else:)[The figure, which you can now rightfully assume to be a Dragon, bounds and leaps with surprisingly agility. Yet, they also seem remarkably clumsy, as you soon find out. The figure leaps forwards and attempts to turn 360 degrees on nothing but their front paws, but their feet get caught on the dirt and they flounder, falling onto their side. For a moment, you're worried, for they just lay there. Then, suddenly, they start to thrash, thumping their tail against the dirt. "Damnit!" You hear it cry, and it lets out a long, frustrated sigh. Your feet scrape against the dirt and it suddenly sits up as it hears the sound, caught by surprise. It manages to make you out in the moonlight, and Kami provides an ample 'beacon' with his pristine fur. He sighs again when he realises that you're just a non-dragon and clambers to his feet. "N-Non-dragons aren't allowed on the premises." He speaks with his best attempt at meaningful authority, but when you stand there awkwardly and say nothing, he deflates. "O-Okay, fine, just...keep outta my way, okay? I gotta practice." [[Ask him his name.->Hran1]] [[Leave.->IoDHub]]]The Dragon intrigues you. When he's standing on all fours, he's about up to your shoulder. His body is stocky and his limbs are thick and muscular, but he lacks the wings that other Dragons bear. His horns are jagged and angular, but his face is a little rounded and soft. You could imagine riding him, and the thought amuses you a little bit. You ask him his name, if only because you're interested in him. He awkwardly glanced at you with an inquisitive eye. "Hran," He introduces himself. "...Though I can't say I know you." You greet him with your name, as well as giving him Kami's name. He looks at the pair of you back and forth, before he nods, as if he were memorising your faces. "$name..." He mumbles your name, before clearing his throat. "Sorry. I have to get back to it." You curiously ask what he's doing, causing him to pause again. He sighs and sags his head, suddenly overcome with a sense of sadness. "I'm training," He explains grimly. "I'm the weakest of the newest recruits and Master Raln wants me to be in top fighting condition...but I feel falling over my blasted hind legs." He angrily scuffs his hind paws into the dirt, toes digging and scooping up grit. You can empathise with his plight. "Maybe I need a break..." He sighs, sitting back on his haunches and observing you from a distance. "Why are you here? I mean, you know, not in a rude way..." You explain that you had been brought here after being rescued, and the Dragons eyes suddenly go wide. "Oh! You're that Magister!" [[Continue.->Hran2]]Hran suddenly bows his head, as if he thinks you deserve a great deal of respect. You can't help but chuckle and wave at him, gesturing him to stop: you're certainly not that kind of person...right? He raises his head and offers you an apologetic smile, looking a little shy. "I can't say I've ever met a Magister before...I remember the days of being taught the way of the world as just a hatchling: they said all Magisters were friends to Dragons." You curiously ask him to tell you more, and he nods. "It was some 30 years ago I was taught. Time moves differently for Dragons, you see: I'm 45 years of age now, but that's more like...15 for your folk. When I was young, I was taught the war between Dragons and the monarchs of Orbath." "They said that the Dragons were unstoppable, but they didn't want to eradicate the Nomads-- that's what they called those 'Non-Dragons'-- but they wanted to live in harmony with them. Nomads didn't like that. But they eventually came to an agreement: Nomads would give 'em this island, if they stayed off the main continent. It seems quite the horrid deal, doesn't it? Well, our ancestors took it anyway." You can't think why, and Hran notices your confused gaze. He shrugs his muscular shoulders. "Don't look at me, $name. I can't tell you the answer...that question still puzzles me to this day. But what we learnt was that Dragons ne'er spoke to the monarchs: it was the Magisters who did the talking. They loved all animals, and animals loved them. They were like druids, but with less nature involved." You nod your head thoughtfully. What an interesting tale. [[Continue.->Hran3]]Hran straightens up a little and offers a quiet sigh, glancing down at you. "So that's why Dragons give you respect, $name. You're a Magister. You're sort of, like...the spokesperson of our species. You also happen to give a mean--" He catches himself before he finishes the sentence and chuckles, quickly averting his gaze. "That, uh, was just something my uncle says. He is a bit of a rowdy one." He awkwardly explained, though he wouldn't say what he intended to, leaving you a little confused. "So, uh, is it true?" Hran questioned, a coy smile spreading across his maw. "Your power comes with a price? Like...some sort of mating curse?" You think you get what he means, and you shuffle awkwardly on the spot, feeling a little self-conscious. You admit it's true, and the Dragon giggles like a maid fawning over a handsome man. "Kinda freaky..." He remarks quietly. "But also kinda good, right? ...I mean, you're a Magister. You probably get this kinda thing a lot, huh?" He stares at you rather intently, and you think you get the gist. He's hinting at something, and you have a feeling you know what he wants to hear. [[Say that you have no problems with fucking Dragons.->HranSS1]] [[Say that you have to go.->Hran4]]You make your intentions clear: You have no problem having sex with Dragons. A sly smile spreads across Hran's muzzle and he suddenly gets to his feet, padding over towards you. He runs his snout along your cheek and down towards your neck, taking in the scent of your 'Nomad' body, according to what he tells you. You can feel the warmth of his hot breath against your $skin, the feeling of his smooth scales grazing your flesh. You raise a tentative hand up to stroke along the side of his neck, feeling the controus and ridges of his hard scales under your fingertips. He suddenly lifts himself up and you feel a large forepaw on your back, pushing you down against the dirt. You land hard on your hands and knees and he lets out a little hiss of worry. You hear a whisper of his apology and roll your eyes begrudingly. Hran clearly doesn't realise his own strength. You find yourself underneath him, his underbelly just above you. He cocks up a hind leg and displays himself rather lewdly, and you gaze upwards to see the outline of a slit, barely illuminated by the light. You reach out and trace a finger along the line of his slit and he visibly shudders from the touch, his tail wagging furiously. Your fingers toy with the slit, slipping your finger up and down the sensitive flesh and lightly probing your fingers, gauging its tightness. He quivers from the sensation. The more that you touch, the more he seems to enjoy it, until you begin to feel a warm, wet mass pulsing against your fingers. It glides down and probes against your palm, twitching and pulsing to life against the warmth of your hand. You hear Hran quietly sigh in bliss and see the silhouette of his toes curling under the moonlight. You quickly realise that this twitching mass is his member, and you enclose your $skined hand around it, dragging from the tip and up towards the base. Hran lets out a quiet gasp and his hips instinctively begin to thrust down against your hand. You feel the wetness of something against your cheek and deduce that he's an eager little Dragon. [[Continue.->HranSS2]]Hran's eyes widen and he drops his head, looking a little awkward. "I get it. You're busy, right? Well, I, uh...hope to see you around sometime." You pat his shoulder in apology and turn to leave. You kinda feel bad for just going like that, but you're sure he'll be just fine. Kami follows along behind you dutifully, and the pair of you leave Hran to his 'training'. [[Return.->IoDHub]]The entire length of his cock is gooey and slick, and as you stroke it up and down, you can hear the audible sounds of your lewd handjob. His hips buck here and there into your hand, and you can hear his heavy panting, the sheer enthusiasm and need to nut emanating off of him like a thick fog. You sort of wish he had a pair of ample nuts for you to toy. The crease between your thumb and finger runs along the underside of his cock You raise your head up towards the tip of his member and fumble around for a bit trying to find it, before your $nose inevitably meets with the wedge end of his member. He lets out a quiet groan of utter pleasure as he pushes his hips down, sliding his cock just a little bit further into your mouth. You feel the sticky wetness of pre-cum on your tongue and quickly swallow it down. Your glide your hand up to the base of his member and feel the bump of a knot forming. Your fingers slip over the smoothness of that inflated muscle and it swells a little under your touch. You return your hand to the member and begin to glide your hand a little more rigorously, pumping mostly the underside of the member along your palm, keeping your mouth firmly around the tip to give it a few encouraging suckles. It seemed to be all the Dragon needed. Hran thrust down against your mouth, pumping his hips back and forth and beginning to pant and squirm with a greater intensity as time went on. He eventually could take no more, and his half-moan, half-pant noises made it clear he was close. You feel it before you taste it: the thick, violent and irregular pulsing against your palm warns you of the impending seed, and you taste it moments later as it arcs against the back of your throat, forcing you to swallow. You catch the silhouette of the Dragon's splayed toes, the moonlight visible between them as thick, plentiful dollops of cum ooze and splatter into your mouth. It inevitably becomes too much and you're forced to part your lips, letting it dribble from your mouth and down your chin. [[Continue.->HranSS3]]The Dragon pants wildly, clearly overcome with the pleasure of his orgasm, and you pull your lips back, feeling the last remnents of load his shooting against your $nose. Your close your mouth and do your best to swallow, wiping your chin. "Oof. You Nomads..." Hran muttered quietly, trotting to the side and moving off from over the top of you, not wanting to flatten you. His cock hands limply between his legs, but it slowly begins to shrink as you get to your feet. "Sorry that I, um, pushed you onto the floor..." He awkwardly shuffled. "I'm a flightless Dragon so I have a lot of muscle, but I can be a bit clumsy...sometimes I don't even know my own strength." You figured as much, and you assure him that it's fine. You glance over to Kami, who was sitting on his haunches nearby, his tongue lolled out. You can only assume he enjoyed the show, however short it was. "You'll come back, right?" The Dragon beamed, and you nod. He gives a sigh of relief. "Oh, uh, I might be at home during the day, sometimes. Just look for Cave 203, 'kay?" He tells you, and you make sure to jot it down. With that, you leave him be. You don't want to intrude on his training any more than necessary, and he seems to think the same thing. [[Return.->IoDHub]]You admit to Zexir that you suffer from a little bit of amnesia since you became a Magister, and you know very little about the world. You ask if he can give you a quick rundown, or whatever he feels is necessary. He nods sagely and gestures you to take a seat. "It would be silly to start at the beginning...so let's start with the gritty parts. The war," Zexir solemnly began as you slip onto a small chair nearby. "Long ago, some 500 years or so, Orbath itself was on the brink of war between the Non-Dragons, whom we referred to as 'Nomads', and our own kind, the Dragons." "Dragons, back then, were an unstoppable species, but we were also quite varied, both in size, stature, magical prowess and opinion. Like any civilisation, there were rebel factions who did not like the idea or war, and some who preferred it. It might surprise you to realise that the majority of Dragons didn't want to fight. We were born from Magic and believed it to be an absolute and just law; petty quarrels such as war didn't concern the most of us. But it was the ignorance of a select few that sparks the Nomads to fight us, and we refused to give up our cultures, our homes." "All wars end in tragesty, child, especially this one. Many of our kind died, in particularly the younger, smaller ones. I was only a young adult when I saw my family burned and torn to shreds." He hangs his head for a moment and you almost feel bad for asking, but he continues nonetheless. "Eventually, the monarchy of Orbath, both in Reliquim and Adplagam, came to a decision: bloodshed was not necessary. They believed themselves to have the upper hand. Our kind simply didn't want to lose anymore children. So, we came to a treaty." "The treaty was, in essence, the start of everything you see here now. The treaty outlined that we would be placed upon this small Isle with the last of our kind, and paid a monthly tribute, in return for leaving Orbath in peace. We agreed to the terms, though not all of us wanted to abide by them." [[Continue.->Zexir1DWorld2]]Zexir blinks at you for a moment, before he begins to chuckle. "I'm flattered you'd come to me for such things, $name, but you'd be better off speaking to Raln. He is much more discipline and much more experienced in teaching Dragons and Nomads how to fight." You remark aloud that you find it surprising for a Dragon to teach non-dragons how to fight, and Zexir blinks at you in amusement, maw curling into a smile. "He might seem brash and rude, but Raln is a very kind-hearted creature. Make sure you give him the respect he deserves." You bow your head and assure him you will treat him with kindness. Zexir looks pleased. "Was there anything else?" [[Ask if he can teach you about the world.->Zexir1DWorld]] (if:$Spells contains "Volition Light")[[[Ask if he can teach you more magic.->Zexir1DMagic3]]](else:)[[[Ask if he can teach you Magic.->Zexir1DMagic]]] [[Ask if he can teach you about the Isle.->Zexir1DIsle]] [[Take your leave.->IoDHub]]You know you're not strong. You know you're not particularly good with magic, either. Perhaps Zexir can teach you. So, you swallow any pride you may have and you ask him. "I can, child," Zexir bowed his head. "But the process is long and gruelling. Are you sure you're up to the task?" You nod fervently, filled with a sudden sense of determination. He smiles fondly at you and he moves his head back, amused. "Let's start right now, then. Hold out your palms." You're surprised to be starting so early, but he must be confident that he can teach you. You quickly straighten up and clear your throat. "Now, close your eyes." He calmly commands, and you don't defy him. You close your eyes and stare at the darkness in front of you, feeling anxiety nestling into the pit of your stomach. "Imagine what it feels like to have the sun on your face," His voice is calm and rumbling, reverberating into your very soul. "Imagine the feeling of staring up at the sun, of how your eyes burn to stare at it. Capture that moment, that feeling." You feel a rush of something in your very body and it makes you shudder. Your eyes water with a weird sensation. "That's it," He soothes. "Push your will towards your palms. Feel the tug of that power inside you and let it pool at your fingertips. When you're ready, try to mold that power into a ball." [[Continue.->Zexir1DMagic2]]You ask Zexir if he could explain to you how the Isle works and what the roles are here. You like this place, but you still feel like a stranger. Zexir nods and he flicks his head towards a bookcase. There, a small parchment flutters out and settles on the table nearby. You step on over and watch as it opens on its own, revealing what looks to be a map of the island. You take in its shape: that of an irregular oval, sitting vertically. It seems as if the map itself is alive, its terrain moulding and changing to match the current climate of Icaria and the Isle itself. Mountains sit on the south of the island and Icaria stands in the middle. You're a little surprised at how small it is compared to the rest of the island. What surprises you more is the sizeable space north of the council chambers that appears untouched. "You know all of the crucial landmarks, I'm sure." Zexir began, reaching out a clawed hand, hovering a single digit over the map to point at the right places. "Icaria is in the middle of the island, and the residency for most of our Dragon inhabitants sits at the far back, in those mountain areas. We had to terraform those ourselves, and it took many years. Those with gifted magical potential live here, in the Council Chamber, and are taught by me exlusively." You ask him what lies to the north, and you see his eyes twinkling his mischief. "Nothing much." He says, but doesn't elaborate any further. What a peculiar mystery... "Now, onto the hierarchy." [[Continue.->Zexir1DIsle2]](if:(history:) contains "SQ1E")[You remark aloud that you remember seeing a Dragon in the mountains around a village called Streamdale. A Dragon was apparently making life difficult for the townsfolk, and you go on to explain that the Dragon in question had offspring it was trying to protect. "Ah...I believe I know who you mean. How did it go?" Zexir wonders. (if:$SQ1EE is 1)[You tell him that you were forced to get the Dragon to leave with his children, though you don't know to where. Zexir grimaces. "Sometimes, a difficult decision needs to be made," Zexir nods. "Don't feel ashamed for your decision: trying to find a balance between Dragons and Nomads is a difficult duty."](elseif:$SQ1EE is 2)[You tell him that you stood up for the Dragons with the mayor, though you can't remember what happened after that. Zexir bows his head out of respect and seems pleased that you would stand up for his kind.](elseif:$SQ1EE is 3)[You regrettably inform him that you weren't able to persuade the Dragon to get along with the villagers, and you fear that the worst might happen in the future. Zexir sighs, but he seems to understand. You have a feeling he knows the Dragon you met.]](else:)[Though you find it wrong to think that Dragons wouldn't want to live in peace, you can understand the plight of those who might want to have their old homes back. Such a dilemma leaves you torn. "I can see the worry and thought in your eyes, child, and it warms me that you take such stock in our history, and in our past." Zexir bows his head in respect of you.] "In any case, let us continue with the story of Orbath's history." "The joining 400 year gap between the war and the state we are in now doesn't particularly matter, for the most part. If anything, the gravest nature beyond the war is the increased sightings of Demons over the past several hundred years, but that hasn't quite reached a climax yet." "Shortly after the war, a council of Wizards was formed to preside over the entirety of Orbath, save for our little Isle. They effectively police Magic on the main continent to stop difficult magicians from performing wrongdoings. From what I hear, they also train magicians in the magical arts. I'll admit that it confuses me how you've alluded them thus far." [[Continue.->Zexir1DWorld3]]"I shant overload you with information, child, so I will go on to explain the world around you for now." Zexir bows his head and his eyes flash. From the side, a slip of parchment emerges from a bookcase and floats over to the table, where it unfolds and spreads out. Before you sits a map of a ring-like continent, with its edges fatter than the top and bottom, almost like two wide bananas joined at the tips. "This, young Magister, is Orbath," Zexir explains. "On the left sits Reliquim, and its capital Holstein. On the right sits Adplagam. Reliquim is a country filled with lush greenery and plenty of crops and livestock, whilst Adplagam is a warm, almost uninhabitable desert biome. The only reason Adplagam has survived this long was the discovery on long since forgotten Magical technology, which has kept them afloat for many years. They've been unable to terrform the terrain thus far, but they've managed to keep themselves alive, at least." "Reliquim is run by the Dominus family, though I'm sure you know that by now. I can't tell you much about how it feels to live in Reliquim, but from what I've heard, the Nomads tend to segregate depending on species. I have heard grave murmurings of slavery for some of what they consider to be the 'lesser' species. Reliquim is also home to a great deal of Demi-humans and monsters, such as Orcs, Goblins, Kobolds and Gnolls, to know a few." "Adplagam, on the other hand, is almost barren, run by the Desert Queen Nylia. You won't find much out there bar from the odd sand monster or two. Its capital, Uile, is mostly underground beneath an abandoned temple, brimming with ancient Magic. I've never seen it myself, but I've heard its a marvel to behold. They are incredibly wary of outsiders, and hate anyone from Reliquim." "Between them is the Eternal Bridges, to the north and south. In the war, long ago, the Northern bridge was destroyed and they have spent many years attempting to repair it. The southern bridge remains intact, for now, and trade between the countries happens there." This is a lot to take in. [[Continue.->Zexir1DWorld4]]With that, Zexir withdraws from the map and sighs through his nose. "There isn't much else you need to know about this world, Magister, but let me offer you some advice, at the very least." He explains, and you nod, listening intently. "Trust no-one," He speaks darkly for a moment, before his maw curls into a wry smile. "At least, no-one you don't truly know. Spies from Adplagam are just about everywhere. Be careful where you go and who you speak to, in the event they might try to decieve you. It sadly happens too often for me to count. I've met many a volunteer on this Isle who had been wronged by someone." "You might have noticed that Orbath is rather...lax with certain laws," He continues. "Specifically, the age of manhood. They consider someone who is 16 to be a man, and anyone younger is still a child, but that doesn't make them useless. Daughters of royalty are wedded off as soon as they turn 12, usually, and if it weren't obvious from your journey thus far, you might have realised that mating and sex is not a rare occurence." You're inclined to agree: it seems to be everywhere nowadays, but you can't understand why. Trying to think about it hurts your head. "Some have argued that a curse was placed upon the world many eons ago, one that gives Nomads an insatiable lust. In truth, it's likely their lax laws on sex and their lack of any age of consent that makes it so common. If that's your thing, then I applaud you for embracing it...though do be careful, Magister. I don't want to see you put in harms way through sheer lustful acts." He sighs, and it appears as if the talk is over. "Was there anything else you needed to know?" [[Ask if he can teach you how to fight.->Zexir1DFight]] (if:$Spells contains "Volition Light")[[[Ask if he can teach you more magic.->Zexir1DMagic3]]](else:)[[[Ask if he can teach you Magic.->Zexir1DMagic]]] [[Ask if he can teach you about the Isle.->Zexir1DIsle]] [[Take your leave.->IoDHub]]"There are 3 'heads' of the island, much like a hydra. Though, please don't 'cut off' any of them." "The first is me. I am the head of Magic and by extension the leader of the entire island. It is up to me to make the final decisions, but by no means do I run a dictatorship: I get all my advice from my two closest friends: Raln and Valia." "You might have met Raln already, but you'd be surprised to know that he's one of the council members. He operates the 'military' for the island, as it were, training young Dragons to a point where they can defend themselves, and the island, in a pinch. They perform regular cloaked patrols with one of my own students to keep track of goings-on in Orbath, and he trains the wingless Drakes to monitor activity on the island. It gives some of the Dragons purpose, which is key to keeping some of them complacent." "Then there's Valia, head of accommodation and agriculture. Between Icaria and the caves to the south is a series of plantations that is overseen and monitored by Valia herself, with noteworthy volunteers doing most of the groundwork. She uses her own special brand of magic to fertilise the crops so that we always get a bountiful harvest every year. She's also the head doctor for the island and sees through many a hatching, when the time comes. You'll want to get on her good side if you want to stay here for any length period of time, but much like Raln, she is kind." "The island is yours to explore, $name. There isn't anything here that is expressly off-limits, apart from my Magic lessons. That's not because you're not worthy, of course-- but untrained Dragons can cause quite the havoc." "Is there anything else you needed to know?" [[Ask if he can teach you about the world.->Zexir1DWorld]] [[Ask if he can teach you how to fight.->Zexir1DFight]] (if:$Spells contains "Volition Light")[[[Ask if he can teach you more magic.->Zexir1DMagic3]]](else:)[[[Ask if he can teach you Magic.->Zexir1DMagic]]] [[Take your leave.->IoDHub]](enchant: ?u, (text-style:'underline')) |u>[Calm] A basic level Magister spell that allows you to calm a creature into a docile state. It doesn't work on all creatures, especially those that are strong. Has a nasty side effect of making them horny for you.<br/><h2>Spells</h2> (if: $Spells's length > 0)[Your spell book contains a (print: $Spells.join(", ")).](else:)[Your spell book is empty.] <div class='img-container-inv'>{ (click:"Calm")[(display:"Calm")] (click:"Volition Light")[(display:"Volition Light")] (click:"Volition Fireball")[(display:"Volition Fireball")] }</div> (link-goto: "Return", (history:)'s last) You tilt your head and frown, attempting to push the magic from your fingers and into your hands. You feel the mass of something against your fingers and you instinctively curl your hands inwards, pushing your palms together. You feel a solid object between your hands, pulsing warmly. You push your magic against it a little more and feel it pulse and swell in response, growing large until your fingertips are barely able to touch. In a panic, you ask it to stop and it suddenly halts, not expanding anymore. "Open your eyes, $name." You do as he asks and stare down at your palms. Sitting between your hands is what looks like a miniature sun, but it's not hot to the touch, nor does it smoulder with flames. It is, simply put, an orb of bright white light, almost blinding to look at. You feel momentarily mesmerised by it before looking up at Zexir, eyes wide. He smirks. "You have a natural knack for Magic, I believe. This particular spell is a very natural form of Magic known as 'Volition', the Magic of free choice and force of will. The manipulation of Magic through your own willpower is not an easy task, but it will prove useful in a pinch. You've just learnt to create a ball of light through sheer will alone. I hope you will practice and find it useful." You don't know what to say, but you are overwhelmer. You passionately bow to him and you hear Zexir chuckle fondly in amusement.(set:$Spells to it + (a:"Volition Light")) "I can teach you some more spells another time. Was there anything else you needed to know?" [[Ask if he can teach you about the world.->Zexir1DWorld]] [[Ask if he can teach you how to fight.->Zexir1DFight]] (if:$Spells contains "Volition Light")[[[Ask if he can teach you more magic.->Zexir1DMagic3]]](else:)[[[Ask if he can teach you Magic.->Zexir1DMagic]]] [[Ask if he can teach you about the Isle.->Zexir1DIsle]] [[Take your leave.->IoDHub]](enchant: ?u, (text-style:'underline')) |u>[Volition Light] An orb of light you can create with the natural magic known as Volition. By imagining the feeling of staring into the sun and forcing that feeling into a ball through sheer will, you can illuminate even the darkest of places.<br/>(if:$Spells contains "Volition Fireball")[Zexir smiles, but this time he doesn't let you have your way. He bows his head down to meet you at eye level. "I think I've taught you all I should at the moment. Learning magic comes with a great deal of study and dedication, and Volition is an easy stopgap for you on your journey. When you've grown more mature, and when your journey is looking grim, I will help you again. Until then, master what I have taught you." You're ever so slightly disappointed, but you understand.
