You reach out a hand and place it on Zexir's nose, lightly stroking the scales. He lets out a peculiar sound, similar to that of a croon. "I can help you with anything else, however." [[Ask if he can teach you about the world.->Zexir1DWorld]] [[Ask if he can teach you how to fight.->Zexir1DFight]] [[Ask if he can teach you about the Isle.->Zexir1DIsle]] [[Take your leave.->IoDHub]]](else:)[You ask Zexir if there's anything else he can teach you in that moment, and Zexir sighs, shaking his head. His lips are curled in some form of amusement, though. "Very well. Only 1 more thing. I cannot afford to teach you any more without a little more rigorous training. You'll want to stand up for this one." You nod and make sure you're in a suitable spot. Kami makes sure to step back so that he doesn't end up getting in the way. "You need to defend yourself, so let's teach you a basic attack spell. We'll use Volition again for this one, though it might be a little tricky. Close your eyes." You do as he says, flexing your fingers. You're already pushing your magic to the forefront, feeling it prickle against your $skin. "Have you ever been burned before?" He asks, and you shake your head. "Alright then. Imagine a roaring flame, like in a fireplace, or in a dark pit. Imagine the flames licking around your hands, singing your flesh. Imagine how that pain must feel." You don't like where this is going, but you try it anyway. It's almost as if you can feel the flames around your fingers, but you can't feel any sort of pain. "Now...picture that those flames are yours. Command them to bend to your will. Force them to obey you," Zexir's voice dips as he speaks to you, guiding you. "Feel their anger, their need to spread. Picture it." [[Continue.->Zexir1DMagic4]]]"The command the flames, you must become the flames. Empathise with their desperate need to light all they touch. Feel their rage as they spread across the landscape, torching tree and house alike." You wince a little as you feel a light burning sensation on your hand, running up your arm towards your elbow. It stings, just a little bit, but you endure it. "Good. Now, much like with the orb of light, will it into a ball in the palm of your hand. Command it to condense, so it may be thrown and spread anew." You concentrate. It's almost like the flames whisper to you, begging for air, begging for things to burn. You clench your fist for a moment before unfurling your fingers, feeling a mass falling into your palm. You open your eyes pre-emptively, sure that you'd manage to do what was necessary. You stand in alarm at the sight around you. Flames lick at your feet and around your legs, kissing your $skin. It spreads out in a circle around you, but it doesn't move that far, stopping in almost a perfect circle. The flames run along your left arm, and sitting in your palm is a heavy orb of condensed fire. It feels spongy when you squeeze it. "Very good. You can let go of it now." Zexir nods to you, and you look down at the flames. You silently ask them to leave with your mind and they vanish, receeding into your skin. "That was impressive, though you held too much enthusiasm," Zexir remarked. "Which is why they grew a little out of control. It's a good thing I put up a barrier to keep it in, else you might have burnt down my room." He chuckled and shook his head, and you breathe a sigh, pleased that you learnt something new. As always, you aim to test and refine the magic so that you can use it on command. (set:$Spells to it + (a:"Volition Fireball")) "Was there anything else, child?" [[Ask if he can teach you about the world.->Zexir1DWorld]] [[Ask if he can teach you how to fight.->Zexir1DFight]] (if:$Spells contains "Volition Light")[[[Ask if he can teach you more magic.->Zexir1DMagic3]]](else:)[[[Ask if he can teach you Magic.->Zexir1DMagic]]] [[Ask if he can teach you about the Isle.->Zexir1DIsle]] [[Take your leave.->IoDHub]](enchant: ?u, (text-style:'underline')) |u>[Volition Fireball] A fireball that you can create using the natural magic called Volition. By imagining the rage of the flames, you can condense it into your hand and throw it, showering an enemy with burning fire. You have to be careful using it-- you're fairly certain the flames keep talking to you.<br/>"Hang...out?" Zexir looks momentarily bewildered, before a wicked chuckle crosses his lips. (if:$Spells contains "Volition Fireball")[(if:(history:) contains "Zexir1N21")[A smile spreads across Zexir's maw as a sudden light illuminates the room, casting an eerie shadow across the wall. The large Dragon spreads his legs out and rolls onto his side, watching you with lidded eyes. "Back for more, are you?" He teases, making you uncomfortable squirm on the spot as you feel him undressing you with his eyes. You tentatively step closer as he cocks a leg up, giving you a wonderful show of his slit and the hidden tailhole between the crease of his tail and thick thighs. He doesn't say a word to you, because you know what he wants. You tentatively gulp. [[Reach out and touch his slit.->ZexirSS1]] [[Quickly take your leave.->Zexir1N21]]](else:)["Forgive me, Magister, but if you were seeking to court me, then you must be a little more subtle than that." He rolls on his side, splaying his legs out as he rests his head against the floor, blinking over at you. You quickly feel the colour rise to your cheeks and do your best to insist that it wasn't what you meant. "You can't go back on it now," He chuckled, sounding half-asleep. "You Magisters, always so coy...come over here." You glance anxiously over to Kami, who just tilts his head and sits back on his haunches, indifferent as to whether you obey or not. You slowly step over towards the large Dragon, and he doesn't move an inch as you get up near his body. It's hard to see in the dark, but he soon changes that. An orb appears above the two of you, revealing his underbelly. "Like what you see, Magister? Do you enjoy the sight of an adult Dragon's most vulnerable spot, the sight of their slit?" He whispered quietly, though it sounded rather loud in your ears as the illuminating light revealing the lengthy slit in front of him, almost as long as your arm. You dread of think what it housed. [[Reach out the touch it.->ZexirSS1]] [[Quickly take your leave.->Zexir1N21]]]](else:)["Don't be so silly, $name. It's the middle of the night. He flicks his tail and you hear the click of a lock behind you as the door swings open, allowing you leave. Zexir grunts and lowers his head. "You may go. I'd ask that you don't try to find me in the middle of the night again, but you Nomads are fickle..." He chuckled and opened his mouth in a stellar yawn, smacking his lips. You hear his heavy, rhythmic breathing and quickly deduce that he's gone back to sleep. You step out of the room and shut the door behind you, and feel a stab of pain as Kami nips your leg. You glare down at him and he snorts, his tail flicking around. |Kami>["You shouldn't be so rude, master."] You grumble and take your leave. He's right, but you feel bad for being told off. [[Return.->CouncilChamber]]]Zexir audibly grumbles at your excuse, but he seems to buy it. "Trying to find your room? You should ask one of the volunteers next time." He settles his head down and heaves a heavy, tired sigh. You hear the click of a lock behind you as the door swings open, allowing you to leave. "Goodnight, $name." He speaks rather sleepily, and you quickly realise that he's fallen asleep again already. You quietly slip out the door and close it behind you, wiping your forehead. You initially wanted to talk to him, but now you just feel bad. You take your leave with Kami following along behind you, giving you a scolding look. [[Return.->CouncilChamber]]You reach out nervously, touching your hands to the smooth line of his slit. The scales look as if they tuck inwards, giving the entrance a strangely womanly vibe, of sorts. You imagine that whatever's hidden beneath is both monstrous and enough to make any Dragon lover drool, though you can't help but think of it in a lewd way. You imagine how tight it might be, given his size. Slowly, you push your hand forwards and you feel the slit's muscles tigthen against your digits, though not nearly as much as you expected. You press your fingers deeper and, before you know it, your whole hand is practically inside of him. You can feel the warmth of his insides, the velvety softness of his flesh against your fingertips and your $skin. Zexir lets out an audible rumble of approval from your touch. "Magister," He speaks in a hushed whisper. "Use your magic. Just let it seep our through your fingers..." You're not sure why he's asking that specifically, but you do your best. You force the magic to the forefront, as you've learnt to do-- albeit somewhat-- and you let it dribble from your fingers as nothing but formless energy. Immediately, you feel Zexir's innards clamp down harder than before and the Dragon lets out a quiet hiss of delight, tail thumping against the ground. It doesn't take long for you to feel a sudden mass against your fingers, pushing and forcing its way out and moving your hand to the side. The tip pokes from the slit and its already sizeable. You place your free hand over the top of it and it twitches under your touch, growing to life remarkably rapidly. [[Continue.->ZexirSS2]]This is a little too much for you, so you quickly request that you take your leave. Zexir lets out a sigh. "Nomads, always a little fickle..." He muttered, but he didn't argue with you. The door clicked behind you and swung open, letting you leave. You silently bow your head at Zexir as you turn and bolt to the door, Kami quickly following behind you. Your heart pounds in your chest as you leave, making your way down the corridor. You cast your gaze back once, seeing the orb of light flicker and fade as Zexir goes back to sleep. You should potentially think twice before doing that again. [[Continue.->CouncilChamber]]It's probably not wise, but you decide to visit Zexir at this time of the night. You step into the council chambers and note a number of willing and able volunteers nearby, but they don't notice you in the dark, even with their torches. You move past them towards the end of the hall and turn right, hoping that it's the right way to go. Your journey leads you towards a small door at the end of a hallway, and Kami moves in beside you, keeping himself low to the ground. You approach the door and listen intently, keeping your breathing slow and quiet. Beyond the door, you hear quiet noises, little groans and whimpers. Curiously, you twist and handle and peek inside. You're immediately blinded, briefly, by an orb of light hovering above the room, illuminating the figures beneath it. You don't have to squint to see what's going on. Across the other side of the room is Zexir what appears to be one of his younger students, if you had to guess. Zexir is laid flat on his back, his legs high in the air and spread-eagled with the young Dragon between them. From where you're standing, you're given a clear heads-on fire to the student's gyrating hips, his thick tail and puckering asshole, coupled with his heavy swinging balls. From what you can tell, he's breeding Zexir-- and in his slit, no less. The two of them are far too enthralled in their mating to notice you, and you are admittedly too curious to stop watching. The air is filled with the heavy sounds of their beathing and groaning, coupled with the thick, wet sounds of the young student's hips pounding back and forth. The younger Dragon is barely a fifth of Zexir's body length, so you can't imagine he's really stimualting Zexir much. Despite that, both of them seem to be enjoying themselves. [[Continue.->ZexirAltSS2]]The more the cock grows, the more it frightens you, somewhat. It simply continues to get larger and larger, catching you by surprise. You don't know much about Dragon anatomy, but you were at least aware that Zexir was quite huge, physically speaking. You should have known what you were getting into. You withdraw your hand from his slit and take a step back to admire the length of his member, which now appears to be at full mast, aching for attention. Zexirs body itself is roughly 10 metres in length, if you had to guess. Judging by that alone, you'd guess that his cock is at least 2 metres long, which makes it about 78 inches, give or take. There mere thought of that mades you shudder, yet there it is. "I'm aware it's a little...//big//," Zexir assures you. "But you shant do anything with it you don't want to." You step a little closer and run your hands over the length. The sides of it bear thick ridges that drip and bulge, almost as if it bears ris, whilst the underside is slender and long. The base swells from a sizeably thick knot and the end is wedged and angular. You slide your hand up towards the top and point out, aloud, that you don't think your hands are going to be enough to satisfy him. "A little magic goes a long way, Magister. Just push your magic into my...well, I'm sure you understand." Zexir looked almost a little bashful about saying such lewd things, but you think you get the gist of what he means. You stroke your hands up and down the length and it twitches idly from your touch. You begin to envision pushing your magic through into his member and you feel it tingle at your fingertips, your digits massaging the muscle. Zexir immediately shudder and warbles his approval. You must be doing something right. [[Continue.->ZexirSS3]]It doesn't feel like you're doing much to please him, but he writhes and groans against your magical touch. The energy that surges through your fingers must be stimulating him somehow. "Ooh yes..." Zexir groans and you cast your gaze over his member towards the tip, watching in surprise as fluids ooze from the end of his cock, pooling down onto the cushion beneath him. He doesn't seem to mind about the mess; you imagine that if you were in such exquisite pleasure, you wouldn't care either. The more you stroke, the more he seems to enjoy it, his breathing low and heavy. The fluids are readily oozing with a greater intensity, and you curiously run your hand over the end, scooping up some of the cum for yourself. It pools in your hand, seeping between your fingers, and you bring it to the cock, slicking up your movements. Faster and faster you stroke, until the Dragon begins to grunt and snort. You can only assume he's getting close, so you make a point to bring your hand towards his knot, giving it a few affectionate strokes. He freezes and lets out a long, slow hiss, and you feel the cock violently throb underneath your hands. A thick jet of cum suddenly shoots from the end of Zexir's member and you turn, watching in alarm as it arcs of Kami's head, who was sitting nearby. Your canine companion bolts to the other side of the room as the seed splatters across the floor. Zexir seems to come to his senses rather quickly and a barrier suddenly curls around his member. The cum shoots against an invisible wall and pools around his bed. [[Continue.->ZexirSS4]]Spurt after spurt erupts from his rigid member, coating the inner bubble with seed, with you inside. Thankfully, the majority of it avoids you and you keep near the base of the member to avoid any stray arcs that happen to bounce off of the Dragon's magical barrier. He spends a great deal of time emptying himself, to the point where you're curious exactly how long its been since he last did this. Or, maybe, he's just that potent. Like... outside the walls?&quot; He looks nervous. &quot;I haven&#x27;t left Scarhaven in years, kid.&quot; But you take his hand and he doesn&#x27;t pull away. The two of you, with Kami trotting happily behind, walk out through the eastern gate and along a narrow path through the fields. Hans squints in the sunlight, his stubby legs working hard to keep up. You lead him to a clearing beside a small stream, where wildflowers dot the bank. The grass is soft and the air smells like earth and growth. Hans stands there, looking around, and something changes in his expression. &quot;Frederick and I used to come here,&quot; he says quietly. &quot;When we were kids. We&#x27;d catch frogs in that stream. He&#x27;d throw them at me and I&#x27;d cry.&quot; He laughs, and his eyes are wet. He sits down in the grass and pulls you down beside him. Kami rolls in the wildflowers, legs in the air, tongue lolling. Hans reaches into his pocket and pulls out a small block of wood and a whittling knife. Without a word, he begins carving. You watch his thick, careful fingers shape the wood-- a new figure taking form. Your shape. Your ears, your $hair, your face. He works in silence for a long time, the stream babbling beside you. When he&#x27;s done, he places the tiny carved figure of you in your palm. &quot;There. Now you&#x27;ve got one too.&quot; You lean against him, and he puts his arm around you. The sun is warm. Kami is rolling in flowers. Everything, for just this moment, is perfect.(set:$LoveMeter to it + 50) [[Head back.->HansRHub]]Hans has been acting strange all day. He keeps dropping his hammer and glancing at you when he thinks you&#x27;re not looking. Finally, he puts down his tools and walks over to you, wiping his hands nervously on his apron. &quot;I, uh... I made you something. And it took me a long time, so don&#x27;t you dare laugh.&quot; He reaches behind the anvil and pulls out a leather-wrapped object. He places it in your hands with the care of someone handling a newborn. You unwrap it. It&#x27;s a sheath for the Magister&#x27;s Dirk-- hand-forged, perfectly proportioned, with intricate scrollwork along the sides. Your name is engraved on the leather strap in small, precise letters. The metalwork is flawless. This isn&#x27;t just a sheath. It&#x27;s a masterpiece. &quot;Every hit of the hammer,&quot; Hans says quietly, his voice cracking. &quot;I was thinking of you.&quot; He takes a shaky breath. &quot;I love you, $name. More than anything. More than the forge, more than this stupid town, more than...&quot; He gestures vaguely at everything. &quot;...all of it.&quot; You kiss him. He melts into you. His arms wrap around your waist and he holds on like he&#x27;ll never let go. Kami watches from the doorway, his tail wagging slowly. Even he looks pleased.(set:$inv to it + (a:&quot;Master-forged Dirk Sheath&quot;)) [[Return->HansRHub]]You make your way to the barracks and find Frederick in his office. He looks up from a map spread across his desk and nods when you mention the bounty. &quot;Good. I was hoping someone would take that on.&quot; He leans back and crosses his arms. &quot;Bandits set up in the forest west of here after we cleared the animals. Opportunists. They&#x27;ve been hitting supply caravans between Scarhaven and the capital.&quot; He taps a point on the map. &quot;Their camp is somewhere in this area. My patrols have tracked them this far, but the forest is thick and they keep moving. You, however...&quot; He looks at Kami. &quot;You have certain advantages.&quot; |Kami>[&quot;I can track their scent, Master. If we get close enough.&quot;] Frederick nods. &quot;Be careful, $name. These aren&#x27;t common thugs. Some of them are ex-military. Deserters.&quot; He hands you a rough sketch of the forest paths and you head out. [[Continue.->SQ3Travel]]The forest west of Scarhaven is dense and overgrown. The path narrows quickly, and soon you&#x27;re pushing through branches and stepping over roots. Kami moves ahead, nose to the ground, tail stiff. You find the first sign within an hour: an overturned cart, half-hidden in the undergrowth. Broken crates spill their contents across the forest floor-- grain, cloth, a shattered bottle of wine staining the leaves dark red. Further on, bootprints in the mud. Fresh. |Kami>[&quot;Several of them, Master. At least six. They went this way.&quot;] You follow Kami deeper into the forest. The canopy thickens overhead, blocking out the sun. You can hear birds, insects, the rustle of small animals. You reach out with your green mist, gently sensing the creatures around you. A lookout bird sits on a branch overhead, trained to screech at intruders. You calm it with a tendril of magic, and it ruffles its feathers and settles. |Kami>[&quot;Well done, Master. We are close.&quot;] [[Continue.->SQ3Camp]]The bandit camp sits in a shallow ravine, hidden by the terrain and the thick canopy. Crude tents fashioned from stolen canvas. A fire pit, still smoking. Crates of stolen goods stacked haphazardly. Six or seven figures move about-- some sharpening weapons, some sleeping, one keeping watch from a raised platform. They look ragged. Thin. Their clothes are patched and worn. These aren&#x27;t wealthy criminals. They&#x27;re survivors. At the centre of the camp sits a larger tent, its flap closed. That&#x27;s where the leader will be. |Kami>[&quot;How shall we approach, Master?&quot;] You consider your options. [[Walk in openly. Show no fear.->SQ3Leader]] [[Use your powers to subdue the camp first.->SQ3PowerEntry]]You reach out with the green mist, extending your senses. The forest is full of animals-- wolves, foxes, birds-- and they respond to your presence. You call to them silently. Within minutes, the treeline around the camp begins to rustle. Wolves emerge from the shadows, eyes gleaming. Foxes sit at the perimeter. Birds settle on every branch. The bandits notice, and their hands go to their weapons. &quot;What the fuck--&quot; &quot;Stand down.&quot; Your voice is calm. The green mist crackles at your fingertips. Every animal in the clearing turns to face the bandits, and not one of them looks friendly. The bandits freeze. Weapons lower. The lookout nearly falls from his platform. You walk into the camp. Nobody moves. [[Continue.->SQ3Leader]]The leader emerges from the central tent. He&#x27;s a scarred, grizzled Wolf-- older than you expected, with grey streaking through his brown fur and a jagged scar across his muzzle. His eyes are sharp, calculating, but tired. So tired. &quot;A kid?&quot; He looks you up and down, then at Kami, then at the animals surrounding his camp (if the power entry was used). His jaw tightens. &quot;Roth,&quot; he says, extending a hand. You&#x27;re not sure if it&#x27;s a greeting or a surrender. &quot;I&#x27;m the one you&#x27;re looking for.&quot; He sits down on a crate and gestures for you to do the same. He looks... exhausted. &quot;I fought in the war, boy. Four years. When it was over, they threw us out. No pension. No thanks. No home to go back to.&quot; He gestures at his men. &quot;These aren&#x27;t criminals. They&#x27;re soldiers who got left behind.&quot; A lean, wiry fox with amber fur and sharp eyes leans against a tree nearby, watching you with an intensity that makes your skin prickle. That must be his lieutenant. [[Capture him by force.->SQ3Fight]] [[Negotiate his surrender.->SQ3Talk]] [[Ask about his lieutenant.->SQ3Deal]]You didn&#x27;t come here to listen to excuses. You reach out with the green mist, and every animal in the surrounding forest responds. Wolves snarl. The camp is surrounded. Roth sees it happening. He doesn&#x27;t fight. He drops his weapon and puts his hands up. &quot;You&#x27;ve got your father&#x27;s gift, boy. The green mist...&quot; He stares at you with something between fear and recognition. &quot;I&#x27;ve seen it before. A long time ago.&quot; You don&#x27;t ask what he means. You tie his hands and march him and his men out of the forest. |Kami>[&quot;He mentioned the green mist, Master. He has seen a Magister before.&quot;] Something about that doesn&#x27;t sit right with you. [[Continue.->SQ3Return]]You tell Roth that you don&#x27;t want to fight. He looks surprised. &quot;Then what do you want?&quot; You tell him to turn himself in to Frederick. Frederick is a fair man. He won&#x27;t execute surrendered soldiers. You promise his men will get work-- the barracks always needs hands, and Scarhaven needs builders. Roth is quiet for a long time. He looks at his men. The wiry fox-- his lieutenant-- catches his eye and gives a barely perceptible nod. &quot;And my men? What happens to them?&quot; You give your word. Roth studies your face, then slowly stands. He unbuckles his sword belt and holds it out to you. &quot;I&#x27;m trusting a kid. God help me.&quot; [[Continue.->SQ3Return]]Your eyes drift to the fox leaning against the tree. He&#x27;s lean, wiry, with amber fur that catches the dappled light. Sharp cheekbones. A thin scar across his lip that pulls his mouth into a permanent half-smirk. He notices you looking. &quot;That&#x27;s Cade,&quot; Roth says. &quot;My lieutenant. Best scout I&#x27;ve got.&quot; Cade pushes off the tree and saunters closer. He&#x27;s taller than you, but not by much. He circles you once, like a predator sizing up prey. &quot;So you&#x27;re the one they sent? Cute.&quot; His voice is low, teasing. His tail flicks behind him. He leans close to your ear. &quot;I can convince the captain to surrender. But it&#x27;ll cost you.&quot; His hand brushes your hip, deliberately. &quot;My tent. Five minutes.&quot; [[Follow Cade.->SQ3DealSS]] [[Refuse. Find another way.->SQ3Talk]]Cade&#x27;s tent is small and dark, lit by a single candle. Stolen furs cover the ground. [[Get dressed and follow.->SQ3Return]]Roth and his men march out of the forest with you. Some are bound, some walk freely-- depending on which path you took. Cade walks at the rear, occasionally catching your eye with that half-smirk. Frederick is waiting at the barracks. He takes one look at the procession and nods approvingly. &quot;Good work, $name.&quot; He takes Roth aside. You hear low, clipped conversation-- soldier to soldier. Frederick is firm but fair, exactly as you promised. Roth&#x27;s men are assigned bunks and work detail. Nobody is executed. Frederick returns to you and holds out a pouch of gold. &quot;200, as promised.&quot; He pauses. &quot;The Strange Man... nothing new. But the border patrols have been uneasy. Something is coming. I can feel it.&quot; Cade lingers at the barracks entrance. He catches your eye, winks, and disappears inside.(set:$SQ3D to 1)(set:$coin to $coin + 200) [[Return.->ScarInn]]The Von Aldersant estate sits an hour&#x27;s walk east of Scarhaven, down a narrow path overgrown with weeds. Iron gates loom at the entrance, rusted but sturdy. Beyond them, an imposing manor of dark stone rises above overgrown gardens. Dead rose bushes claw at the walls. Kami growls low in his throat, the fur on his neck bristling. |Kami>[&quot;I do not like this place, Master. Something is wrong here. I can smell... fear. Old fear.&quot;] You push through the gates and make your way up the path. The front door opens before you reach it. A young mouse boy stands in the doorway-- small, thin, wearing a servant&#x27;s uniform a size too large. A collar around his neck has a small bell that jingles when he moves. He flinches visibly when he sees you raise your hand to wave. &quot;You... you must be the adventurer,&quot; he says quietly. His name badge reads //Pell//. &quot;The master is expecting you. Follow me, please.&quot; He leads you inside. Kami stays close to your heel. [[Continue.->SQ4Manor]]The interior of the manor is opulent but wrong. Marble floors, velvet curtains, oil paintings of children lining the hallway-- but too many locks on the doors. Bars on some of the windows. Pell walks ahead with his head down, the bell on his collar jingling with each step. You notice scuff marks on his wrists. Old ones. Portraits stare down at you from the walls. Children of various species, all painted with the same flat, hollow expression. Not smiling. Not frowning. Just... present. Owned. Pell leads you down a long corridor and stops outside a heavy oak door. He knocks twice. &quot;Your guest, sir.&quot; A voice from within: &quot;Send them in.&quot; Pell opens the door and steps aside, not meeting your eyes. [[Enter the study.->SQ4Yven]] (if:$pk is >=1)[[[Explore the manor first.->SQ4Basement]]](else:)[<!--Do Nothing-->]Yven Von Aldersant sits behind a massive desk of dark wood. He is an elderly Lizard-- greying scales the colour of dried mud, yellow eyes that follow you with cold precision. His fingers are steepled beneath his chin. &quot;Ah. The adventurer.&quot; His voice is silk over gravel. He looks you up and down, lingering. &quot;I require someone of... smaller stature. Adults are too conspicuous for what I need.&quot; He explains: servants have been disappearing from his estate. He suspects bandits in the caves east of the property. He wants you to investigate and bring them back. &quot;They are //my// property,&quot; he says. Not //employees//. Not //staff//. Property. The word sits in the air like a stone. 300 gold if you return them. He doesn&#x27;t say &quot;rescue.&quot; He says &quot;bring them back.&quot; Kami&#x27;s claws click on the marble. His growl is barely audible. |Kami>[&quot;I do not trust this man, Master.&quot;] [[Accept and head to the caves.->SQ4Travel]] (if:(history:) contains &quot;SQ4Basement&quot;)[<!--Already explored-->](else:)[(if:$pk is >=1)[[[Explore the manor before leaving.->SQ4Basement]]]]Pell tries to steer you toward the exit. &quot;You should go now, sir. The master doesn&#x27;t like guests wandering.&quot; But something draws you deeper into the manor. A door at the end of the corridor, heavy oak reinforced with iron bands. Locked. Your Dirk hums at your hip-- you can feel it resonating. Magister magic was used here. The lock is enchanted. You press your hand to the lock and push with the green mist. The enchantment resists, then cracks like ice under pressure. The door swings open to reveal stone steps descending into darkness. Pell&#x27;s face drains of colour. &quot;Sir, please... you shouldn&#x27;t go down there.&quot; [[Descend.->SQ4BasementIn]] [[Leave. Investigate the caves instead.->SQ4Travel]]The basement is cold and damp. Stone walls slick with moisture. Your eyes adjust to the dim light from a single torch guttering on the wall. What you find makes your stomach turn. Cages. Small ones, built for small bodies. Iron bars bolted into the stone floor. Some have threadbare blankets and straw pallets. Others are bare. Chains hang from the walls, manacles sized for thin wrists. A rack near the far wall holds a row of collars-- identical to the one around Pell&#x27;s neck. Two of the cages are occupied. A young fox, maybe your age, with dull orange fur matted with grime. He presses himself against the back of the cage when he sees you, eyes wide. Beside him, in the next cage, a smaller rabbit with cream-coloured fur, hugging his knees to his chest. Both wear collars. Both have marks on their wrists. The fox speaks first, his voice barely a whisper. &quot;Are you here to take us to him? Please... not again.&quot; Kami snarls. The sound echoes off the stone walls, raw and furious. |Kami>[&quot;Master, this man is... I cannot express my revulsion. This is evil.&quot;] [[Free them now.->SQ4Intervene]] [[Leave them. You need more information.->SQ4Travel]] [[Hide and see what happens.->SQ4BasementWatch]]You pull Kami into the shadows behind a stone column, pressing a hand to his muzzle to silence his growling. He obeys, but his body is rigid with anger, trembling against your leg. You wait. Footsteps echo from a side passage. A door opens and a figure enters-- a burly Boar in a leather vest, thick arms scarred from old fights. The overseer. He doesn&#x27;t notice you in the dark. He walks to one of the cages. The rabbit. He unlocks it with a key from his belt, reaches in, and drags the boy out by his collar. The rabbit doesn&#x27;t fight. His eyes are vacant, staring at nothing. He&#x27;s been through this before. The overseer bends him over a wooden bench near the wall. The rabbit&#x27;s small body goes limp against the wood. The overseer tugs the boy&#x27;s trousers down without a word and unbuckles his own belt. What follows is rough, mechanical, dispassionate. The overseer uses the rabbit with the bored efficiency of someone performing a chore. The rabbit stares at the wall. His small body rocks with each thrust. Despite everything, despite the emptiness in his eyes, his body responds-- his small member stiffens against the bench, his muscles clenching involuntarily. The overstimulation draws reluctant physical reactions that he clearly cannot control. You feel Kami pressing harder against your leg, shaking with rage. The boy stumbles, catches himself on the bars, and crawls inside without being told. His small member is still stiff, wet at the tip, his body shaking. The overseer buckles his belt and turns toward the stairs. His eyes sweep the shadows and land directly on you. &quot;Who the fuck--&quot; He crosses the distance in two strides and grabs you by the front of your tunic. He&#x27;s strong-- far stronger than you. Your feet leave the ground. Kami lunges. The overseer&#x27;s boot connects with Kami&#x27;s ribs with a sickening thud. Kami yelps-- a sharp, pained sound-- and crumples, his legs buckling. He tries to rise, but his front paw gives out and he collapses, wheezing. The overseer pins you against the stone wall, one meaty hand around your wrists, the other fumbling with your clothes. You can smell stale sweat and cheap ale on his breath. &quot;Another little pet for the master, eh?&quot; He chuckles. He yanks your shorts down and forces you around, pressing your face against the cold stone. You feel him pushing between your legs from behind, thick and blunt and insistent. He approaches the dazed Kami. The wolf tries to scramble up but the jackal pushes him down, pressing a knee into his back. Kami whines, his claws scraping uselessly on the stone floor. The jackal lifts Kami&#x27;s hindquarters and mounts him from behind. Kami&#x27;s ears flatten completely. He won&#x27;t meet your eyes. You pull your clothes back on with shaking hands. Your body aches. So does your pride. |Kami>[&quot;...Master.&quot;] His voice is barely a whisper. He limps toward you and presses his nose against your hand. He doesn&#x27;t know what to say. Neither do you. The fox in the cage is watching you through the bars. His eyes are not hollow anymore. They&#x27;re desperate. &quot;Please,&quot; he whispers. &quot;Please get us out of here.&quot; [[Free them.->SQ4Intervene]] [[Leave. Come back with Frederick.->SQ4Travel]]The green mist surges from your hands. You&#x27;re not gentle about it. The power floods outward and every animal within the estate responds-- the horses in the stable, the rats in the walls, the birds on the roof. They answer you. If the overseer is present, his muscles lock rigid. He can&#x27;t move. Can&#x27;t speak. His eyes widen with terror as you hold him in place with nothing but your will. You break the cage locks with your hands, the enchantments crumbling under your magic. The captives stumble out-- the fox, the rabbit, and anyone else held below. They cling to you, small hands gripping your clothes, shaking. Kami stands at your side, bruised but resolute. He nuzzles the youngest captive gently. |Kami>[&quot;You are safe now. I give you my word.&quot;] [[Confront Yven.->SQ4Confront]]You head east into the forest. Kami moves ahead, nose to the ground, though he&#x27;s moving more stiffly than usual. He doesn&#x27;t complain. The caves are a 20-minute walk through dense undergrowth. You find them easily-- a series of openings in a low cliff face, partially concealed by hanging moss. The entrance is guarded by animals: wolves, a large stag, a brown bear dozing at the mouth. They respond to your green mist, but there&#x27;s confusion in them. You can feel the residual signature of //another// Magister&#x27;s command layered over their instincts. |Kami>[&quot;Another Magister was here, Master. I can smell it. The green residue... it is old, but unmistakable.&quot;] You calm the guardians and enter. [[Continue.->SQ4CaveKami]]Inside the cave, you find evidence of habitation. Crude bedding. Drawings on the walls in charcoal-- houses, trees, smiling faces. A child&#x27;s journal, half-filled in shaky handwriting. //&quot;The green man says we&#x27;re safe here. He feeds us with the animals. The wolves keep us warm at night. I miss the manor sometimes but I don&#x27;t miss the master.&quot;// The Hooded Mage was here. He //rescued// these people. Used his Beastmaster powers to carry them away and left animal guardians to protect them. As you explore deeper, the stag from the entrance follows you inside. Its nostrils flare. It&#x27;s not looking at you-- it&#x27;s looking at Kami. The curse pheromones. Your presence amplifies them. What you both are. He stops circling. Stands still. His ears flatten and his tail tucks. The stag mounts. Its weight drives Kami&#x27;s front end down, his chin hitting the stone. [[Continue.->SQ4CaveDeep]]Deeper in the cave, you find more green residue on the walls. Claw marks-- massive ones, far too large for any animal you know. Kami sniffs them. |Kami>[&quot;Dragon claws, Master. A very large one.&quot;] Korrath. The Dark Dragon. He was here too. The escaped servants are gone-- moved further away. The Hooded Mage anticipated that Yven would send someone looking. He planned ahead. You stand in the empty cave and think. The Hooded Mage commanded the Dark Dragon at the mountain. He was terrifying. Powerful. Dangerous. But here... he was //protecting// people. Using the same powers you have to shield captives from a monster. What separates you from him? The question hangs in the air like the green mist still lingering on your fingers. |Kami>[&quot;Master... the other Magister. He is not what I expected.&quot;] Neither is Yven. [[Return to the manor.->SQ4Pell]]You return to the manor. Pell meets you at the gate, his bell jingling as he runs to catch you. His eyes are wide. &quot;Did you find them? Please... please don&#x27;t bring them back.&quot; His voice cracks. He grabs your sleeve and his small hands are shaking. &quot;The master... he hurts us. He&#x27;s always hurt us. The green man-- the one who took the others-- he tried to take me too. But I was too scared to leave. Too scared of what the master would do if he found out.&quot; Tears stream down his mousy cheeks. Kami nuzzles his hand gently. |Kami>[&quot;He is just a boy, Master. This place is evil.&quot;] [[Comfort Pell.->SQ4PellSS]] [[Tell Pell to come with you.->SQ4Choice]]You pull Pell into the garden shed beside the gate. It&#x27;s small, dusty, filled with rusted tools. But it&#x27;s private. Kami sits outside, watching the path. Pell is shaking. You hold him against your chest and feel his heartbeat hammering through his thin frame. He clings to your tunic, face buried in the fabric. &quot;Nobody ever listens,&quot; he whispers. &quot;Nobody ever cares.&quot; You tell him you care. His grip tightens. What happens next is tender and slow. Pell is experienced-- Yven has used him, you realise with a sickening clarity-- but this is the first time someone is //gentle// with him. He doesn&#x27;t know what to do with kindness. When you touch him softly, he flinches. When you don&#x27;t pull away, he melts. He&#x27;s small. Mouse-thin. Trembling hands that fumble with buttons. You let him set the pace, and he goes slowly, hesitatingly, as if waiting for the pain that doesn&#x27;t come. He finishes quietly-- barely a sound, just a full-body shudder and a soft exhale against your neck. His small hands grip yours, tight, as if letting go means losing the only kindness he&#x27;s ever known. [[Continue.->SQ4PellSS2]]&quot;Nobody has ever been gentle with me before.&quot; His voice cracks. He&#x27;s still holding your hand. You brush a tear from his cheek with your thumb, and he leans into the touch like a plant leaning toward sunlight. You sit together in the dusty shed for a long time. He doesn&#x27;t let go of your hand. |Kami>[&quot;Master.&quot;] Kami&#x27;s voice comes from outside the shed, low and solemn. [&quot;We must make a decision about the Reptile.&quot;] He&#x27;s right. Time to face Yven. [[Continue.->SQ4Choice]]You stand outside the manor. Pell is behind you, wringing his hands. Kami sits at your side, bruised but resolute. The question is simple. What do you do about Yven Von Aldersant? [[Tell Yven the caves are empty. Take the gold. Leave.->SQ4Lie]] [[Confront Yven. Free everyone.->SQ4Confront]] [[Report Yven to Frederick.->SQ4Frederick]]You walk into Yven&#x27;s study and tell him the caves were empty. His yellow eyes narrow, but he can&#x27;t prove otherwise. He slides a pouch of gold across the desk. &quot;Disappointing. But you have my thanks.&quot; 300 gold. It sits heavy in your hand. You walk away from the manor with heavy pockets and a heavier conscience. The iron gates close behind you with a clang. Through the bars, you can see Pell watching from the doorway. His bell jingles as the door closes. |Kami>[&quot;...Master. Are we truly leaving them?&quot;] His voice is quiet. Not accusing. Just... sad. You don&#x27;t answer.(set:$SQ4D to 1)(set:$coin to $coin + 300) [[Return.->ScarInn]]You walk into Yven&#x27;s study and the green mist is already crackling at your fingertips. Every animal on the estate responds to your presence-- horses stamping, birds circling, even the rats in the walls freezing in their tracks. They are yours. &quot;You are not a good man, Von Aldersant.&quot; Yven&#x27;s face goes cold. &quot;You overstep, child.&quot; &quot;I&#x27;ve seen the basement.&quot; The room goes very still. Yven&#x27;s jaw works. His hand drifts toward a bell on his desk-- to summon the overseer-- but Kami plants his front paws on the desk and bares his fangs, inches from the Lizard&#x27;s face. Yven freezes. The captives are freed. The collars are removed. Pell walks out of the manor gates with you, blinking in the sunlight as if seeing it for the first time. You don&#x27;t get paid. But Pell is free, and the fox and the rabbit cling to Kami&#x27;s fur as you walk back to Scarhaven.(set:$SQ4D to 1)(set:$PellFreed to true) [[Return.->SQ4End]]You go straight to Frederick. You tell him everything. The basement. The cages. The collars. The overseer. Frederick&#x27;s expression doesn&#x27;t change as you speak, but you can see the muscle in his jaw tightening with every word. When you finish, he stands. Slowly. Deliberately. &quot;That reptile has been under my nose this entire time.&quot; He calls for soldiers. Within the hour, a squad marches on the Von Aldersant estate. Yven is dragged from his study in chains, sputtering about his rights and his lineage. Nobody listens. The captives are freed. The overseer is arrested. Frederick hands you a pouch of gold from the barracks coffers. &quot;200. It&#x27;s all I can spare, but you&#x27;ve earned it ten times over.&quot; Pell stands beside you, blinking in the sun. For the first time, you see him smile.(set:$SQ4D to 1)(set:$PellFreed to true)(set:$coin to $coin + 200) [[Continue.->SQ4End]](if:$PellFreed is true)[Pell walks with you back to Scarhaven. He doesn&#x27;t say much, but his small hand finds yours as you walk, and he doesn&#x27;t let go until you reach the inn. Wes takes one look at the boy and nods. &quot;I could use another hand around here. You any good at carrying trays, kid?&quot; Pell nods so fast his bell jingles. Wes reaches over and unclips the collar, tossing it into the fire. &quot;You won&#x27;t be needing that.&quot; Pell touches his bare neck and tears streak down his face. But he&#x27;s smiling.](else:)[You return to Scarhaven alone. The gold is heavy in your pocket. Kami walks beside you in silence.] The quest is done. But the questions linger. The &quot;green man&quot; who rescued Yven&#x27;s captives was the Hooded Mage-- the same figure who commanded the Dark Dragon on the mountain. He used the same powers you have. The same green mist. The same control over animals. He was protecting people. |Kami>[&quot;Master... the other Magister. Perhaps he is not the enemy we thought.&quot;] Or perhaps he is. But the line between you and him grows thinner with every passing day. [[Return.->ScarInn]]You kneel beside Rowan and press a cloth to the gash on his head. He winces but holds still. In the moonlight, you can see him more clearly: lean, young-- maybe 18 or 19-- with short antlers just beginning to branch. Handsome, despite the blood. &quot;Thank you,&quot; he breathes. &quot;I thought I was done for. The animals... they weren&#x27;t normal. Their eyes were glowing. Green.&quot; |Kami>[&quot;The Magister&#x27;s influence, Master. It reaches further than we thought.&quot;] Rowan looks at Kami with surprise, then back at you. &quot;A talking wolf. Huh. Seahome is gonna be pissed they missed this.&quot; You bandage his wound as best you can. He&#x27;s grateful-- almost desperately so. His hand finds yours and squeezes. &quot;I owe you my life, kid. Let me... show my appreciation.&quot; His eyes are warm, earnest, a little glazed with pain and something else. [[Accept.->ScarOtherNSS]] [[Decline. Tell him to rest.->ScarOtherNEnd]](set:$RowanMet to true)Rowan nods, understanding. He leans against the wall and closes his eyes. &quot;If you&#x27;re ever in Seahome, look for me. Name&#x27;s Rowan. I won&#x27;t forget this.&quot; He settles in to rest. You head back to the centre of town. [[Head back.->ScarHub]]In the shadow of the house, with the moon overhead and the town sleeping, Rowan pulls you close. His grip is strong despite his wound. His breath is hot against your neck. It&#x27;s quick, standing, urgent-- the gratitude of someone who thought he was going to die. His back against the wall, your body pressed against his. He lifts you against him-- he&#x27;s strong for his build-- and pushes into you with a shaky exhale. The stretch is smooth, his member long and curved, hitting deep. He thrusts with a desperate, uneven rhythm, his antlers scraping the stone behind him. Kami sits nearby, watching with those blue eyes. His tail thumps once. You knock softly. A pause, then: &quot;Come in.&quot; The office is different at night. Candlelight casts warm shadows across the map-covered walls. Frederick sits behind his desk, his cape draped over the back of his chair, his collar undone, the top buttons of his uniform open. Pren is asleep on the desk, curled into a small blue-red ball, snoring faintly. Frederick looks up. His expression softens when he sees you. &quot;Can&#x27;t sleep either, huh?&quot; He gestures to the chair across from him. &quot;Pull up a seat. I could use the company.&quot; He looks tired. The bags under his eyes are darker than you&#x27;ve seen them. Maps are spread across every surface, covered in pins and notes in his neat handwriting. [[Ask about the investigation.->FrederickNc1]] [[Ask about him personally.->FrederickNc2]] [[&quot;You look tense.&quot;->FrederickNSS]] [[Leave.->ScarBarracks]]Frederick leans forward, elbows on the desk, and rubs his temples. &quot;Between you and me... I&#x27;m scared, $name.&quot; He&#x27;s never said that before. The candlelight catches the worry lines around his eyes. &quot;The sightings are getting more frequent. The Strange Man-- or whatever he is-- has been seen near the roads between here and Seahome. Animals acting strangely. Green residue on the trees. It&#x27;s like the forest all over again.&quot; He pushes a map toward you. Pins mark a pattern: a line of sightings heading northeast, toward Seahome and The Bridge. &quot;I don&#x27;t have the men to send a proper patrol. And the Spire...&quot; He shakes his head. &quot;The Spire doesn&#x27;t care about a frontier village.&quot; He looks at you with something vulnerable in his expression. &quot;I&#x27;m glad you&#x27;re here.&quot; [[Ask about him personally.->FrederickNc2]] [[&quot;You look tense.&quot;->FrederickNSS]] [[Leave.->ScarBarracks]]Frederick leans back and stares at the ceiling. At night, without the cape and the formal posture, he looks younger. Smaller. &quot;Hans and I... he&#x27;s like a little brother. I&#x27;d die for that idiot.&quot; A faint smile crosses his lips. &quot;When his parents died, I was the only one who came. I sat with him in the workshop all night. Didn&#x27;t know what to say. Just... sat there.&quot; He scratches behind one grey-furred ear. &quot;The uniform keeps people at a distance, you know? Nobody wants to get close to the captain. They see the rank, not the person.&quot; He looks at you. &quot;You&#x27;re one of the few who doesn&#x27;t seem to care about the rank.&quot; [[Ask about the investigation.->FrederickNc1]] [[&quot;You look tense.&quot;->FrederickNSS]] [[Leave.->ScarBarracks]]You tell Frederick he looks tense. He lets out a short, humourless laugh. &quot;Tense. That&#x27;s one word for it.&quot; You walk around the desk and put your hands on his shoulders. He stiffens-- then slowly, reluctantly, relaxes. His muscles are like knotted rope beneath his uniform. &quot;We shouldn&#x27;t...&quot; He begins. &quot;I&#x27;m your superior. This is...&quot; But he doesn&#x27;t pull away. Your fingers work the knots in his shoulders and he groans quietly, his head dropping forward. His hands grip the edge of the desk. Your hands slide lower. Down his chest, past the open buttons of his uniform. His breath catches. You can feel his heartbeat under your palm-- rapid, despite his composed exterior. He turns in the chair and looks up at you. His yellow-green eyes are conflicted. Duty and desire, pulling in opposite directions. &quot;$name...&quot; (if:$KamiExped is >=1)[[[Tell him Kami is going to do more than watch.->FrederickNSSKami]]](else:)[<!--Do Nothing-->] [[Kiss him.->FrederickNSS2]] [[Pull back. He lifts you onto the desk. His hands are precise, military, efficient-- unbuttoning, unfastening, removing obstacles. He pulls out and buttons his uniform immediately. The professional mask slides back into place like it was never gone. &quot;This absolutely did not happen. And when you reach the door, he clears his throat. &quot;...Thank you.&quot;(set:$DN to $DNO)(set:$DNO to $DNO2)(set:$DNO2 to $DN) [[Leave.->ScarBarracks]]You whistle softly. Kami, who was waiting outside the office, pads in through the door. Frederick&#x27;s eyes widen. &quot;Is he going to watch?&quot; You tell Frederick that Kami is going to do more than watch. The captain&#x27;s composure cracks for the first time-- his mouth opens, closes, opens again. He looks from you, to Kami, back to you. &quot;...I&#x27;m a captain in the King&#x27;s guard. I should not be doing this.&quot; But he doesn&#x27;t say no. You guide Frederick to lean over the desk. His tail rises instinctively as you position Kami behind him. The wolf mounts, forepaws locking around the feline&#x27;s lean waist, hips already thrusting, searching. Kami finds his mark and pushes in. Frederick&#x27;s composure shatters-- his claws dig into the desk, scattering maps, and a sound escapes his throat that is decidedly //not// military. The captain lets out a strangled sound and his own feline cock erupts beneath him, barbed member pulsing against the underside of the desk. You finish in his mouth moments later, and he swallows without comment. When Kami pulls out and pads back to the door, Frederick straightens his uniform with trembling hands. His face is scarlet beneath his fur. &quot;This //absolutely// did not happen. //None// of it.&quot;(set:$DN to $DNO)(set:$DNO to $DNO2)(set:$DNO2 to $DN) [[Leave.->ScarBarracks]]The hooded figure watches you approach. As you draw closer, they lower their hood to reveal a sleek, dark-furred feline with sharp amber eyes and a thin golden chain around their neck. Their robes are finely made-- silk, embroidered with patterns you don&#x27;t recognise. &quot;Ah. A visitor.&quot; Their voice carries an accent-- clipped vowels, rolling consonants. &quot;I am a merchant from Adplagam. The eastern continent.&quot; They study you with interest. &quot;You have an... interesting aura, child. In Adplagam, your kind are revered. And feared.&quot; They know what you are. Your hand drifts to the Dirk at your hip. &quot;Relax. I am not your enemy. I trade in goods, not secrets.&quot; They smile thinly. &quot;Though I //do// hear interesting things, from time to time.&quot; They offer a single item for sale: Adplagam Incense. &quot;Burns with a purple flame. Calms the mind. 20 gold.&quot; (if:$coin is >=20)[(link:&quot;Purchase Adplagam Incense. (20 gold)&quot;)[(set:$coin to $coin - 20)(set:$inv to it + (a:&quot;Adplagam Incense&quot;))You purchase the incense. It smells of sandalwood and something sharper, exotic. The merchant nods approvingly. &quot;Burn it when the world is too loud. It will help.&quot; [[Return.->ScarInn]]]](else:)[You can&#x27;t afford it. The merchant shrugs.] &quot;If you travel east... be careful. The bridge is not what it seems.&quot; They pull their hood back up. The conversation is over. [[Return.->ScarInn]]Raln drops from his perch, landing in front of you with a thud that shakes the ground. Up close, he&#x27;s even more imposing-- wingless, but built like a fortress of muscle and scale. Mottled red and brown, jagged horns, and eyes that hold zero patience. &quot;First lesson. Don&#x27;t die.&quot; He swipes at you. Not full force-- you can tell he&#x27;s holding back-- but fast enough to knock you sideways if you don&#x27;t move. You dodge, barely. He swipes again. You dodge again, less barely. &quot;Good. You can move. That&#x27;s more than most Nomads.&quot; Kami enters the arena and Raln&#x27;s eyes flick to the wolf. He nods approvingly. &quot;Your beast fights alongside you. That&#x27;s rare. Most Magisters treat their animals like tools. You treat yours like a partner.&quot; The session lasts an hour. By the end, you&#x27;re bruised, scraped, and your muscles are screaming. But you blocked three out of ten strikes. &quot;You&#x27;re not entirely hopeless.&quot; High praise, from Raln.(set:$RalnTrain to 1) [[Return.->IoDHub]]Raln tosses you a wooden practice sword. Dense hardwood, weighted like the real thing. &quot;Hold it like //this//, not like you&#x27;re stirring soup.&quot; He adjusts your grip, his massive clawed hand surprisingly gentle. Then he attacks-- slow, methodical, each strike a lesson. Block. Parry. Riposte. His voice a constant bark of correction. &quot;Better. Again.&quot; Kami lunges at practice dummies alongside you. Raln watches with interest. &quot;Your beast learns faster than you do.&quot; By the end, your arms are jelly. Raln examines the practice blade. You left marks on it. &quot;Not bad. For a Nomad.&quot; Almost a smile.(set:$RalnTrain to 2) [[Return.->IoDHub]]No warmup. Raln attacks immediately. &quot;Integrate. Your powers and your blade are one. Show me.&quot; You reach out with the green mist while fighting. A hawk dives at Raln&#x27;s face. He bats it aside, but you land a strike on his forearm. Raln stares at the mark. Then at you. Then at the hawk. &quot;That&#x27;s... not something I&#x27;ve seen before.&quot; A grin splits his scarred muzzle. &quot;You might survive out there after all, Magister.&quot; He invites you to sit on the arena wall. First time he&#x27;s ever asked you to stay.(set:$RalnTrain to 3) [[Talk to Raln.->RalnChat]] [[Return.->IoDHub]]Raln sits on the arena wall, massive legs dangling. The whole island spreads below. &quot;What do you want to know, little one?&quot; [[&quot;Why are you wingless?&quot;->RalnChat1]] [[&quot;What do you think of Zexir?&quot;->RalnChat2]] [[&quot;Tell me about Valia.&quot;->RalnChat3]] [[&quot;What do you fight for?&quot;->RalnChat4]] [[Leave.->IoDHub]]Raln is quiet for a long moment. &quot;Born that way. Genetic. My mother was horrified. My father was... disappointed.&quot; He flexes one massive arm. &quot;Couldn&#x27;t fly. So I fought. Got bigger, stronger, //meaner// than any flying Dragon. If I can&#x27;t soar above my enemies, I&#x27;ll crush them beneath my feet.&quot; His tail swishes. &quot;I don&#x27;t need wings. Wings are a crutch.&quot; Then, softer: &quot;Though I&#x27;d be lying if I said I never wondered what it feels like.&quot; [[Ask something else.->RalnChat]]&quot;Zexir?&quot; Raln snorts. &quot;Bleeding heart. Talks too much. Trusts too easily.&quot; Pause. &quot;But he&#x27;s kept this island alive for 200 years. And when he fights... you don&#x27;t want to be on the other side.&quot; Something beyond respect in his voice. Admiration. Maybe love, in the way soldiers love generals. &quot;Don&#x27;t tell him I said that. His head is big enough.&quot; [[Ask something else.->RalnChat]]&quot;Valia keeps the records. Every dragon who&#x27;s ever lived here. Every birth, every death, every egg.&quot; He looks out over the island. &quot;She&#x27;s older than Zexir. Oldest dragon alive. Doesn&#x27;t fight, doesn&#x27;t lead. Just watches. Writes. Remembers.&quot; &quot;She&#x27;s complicated. Meet her yourself. The archive is off the council chamber, on the left. Tell her Raln sent you. She&#x27;ll complain, but she&#x27;ll talk.&quot; [[Ask something else.->RalnChat]]Raln stares at the ocean. Wind ruffles the crest of rough scales along his spine. &quot;Them.&quot; He gestures at the island below-- town, caves, hatchlings in the plaza. &quot;Every Dragon. Every Nomad. Every hatchling who doesn&#x27;t know what war looks like.&quot; His claws dig into stone. &quot;I fight so they don&#x27;t have to. That&#x27;s all there is to it.&quot; He glances at you. &quot;I think you understand that. You fight for your wolf the same way.&quot; [[Ask something else.->RalnChat]]The arena is empty at night. Moonlight filters through the open roof. You find Raln alone with a massive bowl of mead. He looks up. &quot;Can&#x27;t sleep.&quot; You settle against his side-- his scales radiate heat. He&#x27;s been drinking. His usual edge is blunted. &quot;You&#x27;re not afraid of me. Most Nomads flinch when I move too fast.&quot; His massive hand reaches down, clawed fingers touching your chin, tilting your face up. The size difference is overwhelming. &quot;I could hurt you,&quot; he says quietly. For training.&quot; His excuse to see you again.(set:$DN to $DNO)(set:$DNO to $DNO2)(set:$DNO2 to $DN) [[Return.->IoDHub]]Green mist. Kami slumps at the edge of the arena. Glassy-eyed. Gone. Zexir is there, his massive turquoise form draped across the stone floor. He lifts his head when you enter. &quot;Ah, child. You took the quest.&quot; His voice rumbles. &quot;A dragon controlled by a Magister. This is unprecedented. I need to understand how it happened.&quot; He describes the Dark Dragon from the mountain-- the one who nearly killed you. The one the Hooded Mage commanded. &quot;Investigate the eastern caves. Beyond the residential area. That creature must have rested somewhere on this island before it was turned. Find what you can and report to Valia in the archive. She keeps the records.&quot; [[Head to the eastern caves.->IoDQ1Search]]The eastern caves are deeper and darker than the residential ones. You use Volition Light to illuminate the passages. Kami&#x27;s nose works overtime, tracking old scents through the damp stone. After an hour of searching, you find it: a massive chamber, its ceiling lost in darkness. The walls are scored with claw marks-- enormous, deep, the stone itself cracked from the force. Shed scales litter the floor, black and purple, each one as large as your hand. And on the walls: green residue. Faint, old, but unmistakable. |Kami>[&quot;Magister magic, Master. The same signature as the forest. And the mountain.&quot;] Wall carvings in dragon script line one section-- crude, old, scratched into the stone with claws. You can&#x27;t read them. [[Bring findings to Valia.->IoDQ1Valia]]You show the scales and describe the carvings to Valia in the archive. She examines the scales through her spectacles, turning them in her slender claws. &quot;These scales belong to Korrath.&quot; Her voice is measured, precise. &quot;He vanished 12 years ago. A young adult. Rebellious. Left the island against orders.&quot; She pulls a scroll from the shelf and unrolls it, revealing a detailed lineage chart. &quot;Something found him out there. Something... changed him. The green residue confirms it. A Magister bound him.&quot; She gives you access to her archive for further research. [[Research Magister-Dragon bonds.->IoDQ1Archive]]The archive is vast. You spend hours reading ancient scrolls about the relationship between Magisters and Dragons. Most of it is dry academic history, but one passage stops you cold: //&quot;When a Magister&#x27;s power corrupts, the bond with beasts becomes chains. The animal loses its will, its identity. It becomes an extension of the Magister-- a weapon, a tool, a slave. The Magister who does this does not merely control. They //consume//.&quot;// |Kami>[&quot;Master... this is what the Hooded Mage has done to Korrath. He has consumed him.&quot;] You bring your findings to Zexir. [[Report to Zexir.->IoDQ1End]]Zexir is disturbed. His massive head sways slowly. &quot;Korrath... I remember him. Headstrong. Proud. He wanted to see the world beyond this island.&quot; The ancient dragon closes his eyes. &quot;And now he is a slave. A weapon wielded by a corrupted Magister.&quot; He opens his eyes and fixes them on you. &quot;You have done well, child. The isle is in your debt.&quot; (if:$inv contains &quot;Zexir's Amulet&quot;)[(set:$coin to $coin + 200)He hands you a pouch of gold. 200, counted precisely.](else:)[(set:$inv to it + (a:&quot;Zexir's Amulet&quot;))He reaches beneath a cushion and retrieves an amulet-- midnight black cabochon in silver finery. &quot;Take this. It will allow you to travel between here and Orbath.&quot;] (set:$IoDQ1D to 1) [[Return.->IoDHub]]The posting is from Erel. You find him at his clinic, pacing, his red and white feathers ruffled with worry. &quot;Oh, $name! Thank goodness. The hatchlings in the eastern caves-- they&#x27;ve gone feral. Won&#x27;t respond to their parents. Snapping, biting, eyes... strange.&quot; He wrings his feathered hands. &quot;I&#x27;ve never seen anything like it. But your powers... you can communicate with animals in ways I cannot. Will you help?&quot; Kami nuzzles Erel&#x27;s hand, and the avian manages a weak smile. [[Head to the hatchling den.->IoDQ2Cave]]The den is in the eastern caves, a different branch than the Dark Dragon&#x27;s chamber. Warmer. Sulfurous. The sound of small, angry snarling echoes off the walls. Four hatchlings, each no bigger than a large cat, huddle in the back of the cave. Their scales are various shades of green and bronze, their eyes-- normally amber or gold-- tinged with a sickly green glow. |Kami>[&quot;Master... that green. It is the same as your power. But... wrong. Twisted.&quot;] Residual Magister magic. The Hooded Mage&#x27;s influence lingered in these caves and corrupted the hatchlings. They snarl at you, tiny jaws snapping. [[Use your powers to calm them.->IoDQ2Calm]]You reach out with the green mist. The hatchlings resist-- snarling, snapping at the tendrils of magic. The corrupted energy fights back, pushing against your will. It&#x27;s like trying to untangle a knot made of smoke. You push harder. Sweat beads on your forehead. The mist thickens, wrapping around each hatchling, sinking into the corrupted magic and //burning// it away. The green glow in their eyes flickers, dims, and finally fades. One by one, the hatchlings stop snarling. Their eyes return to normal-- amber, curious, confused. The smallest one chirps and totters toward you, bumping its head against your shin. You did it. The corruption is gone. |Kami>[&quot;Your power grows, Master. You purged the other Magister&#x27;s influence.&quot;] [[Return to Erel.->IoDQ2Erel]]Erel meets you at the cave entrance, his medical kit clutched to his chest. He examines the hatchlings with practiced precision-- checking their eyes, their scales, their responses. His concern melts into relief. &quot;They&#x27;re clean. Not just professional respect-- something warmer, more curious. &quot;You&#x27;re quite remarkable, you know that?&quot; He adjusts his spectacles. &quot;Your powers work on all animals. Dragons are animals too, at a biological level. The implications for veterinary medicine are... extraordinary.&quot; The dragon parents arrive, swooping down and nuzzling their hatchlings. The chirping and cooing fills the cave. [[Walk back with Erel.->IoDQ2ErelSS]] [[Head back alone.->IoDQ2End]]You walk back to his clinic together. The path is quiet, the ocean breeze warm. Floor-to-ceiling scrolls line the walls, parchment rolls in honeycomb shelves, ancient tomes stacked on reading desks. Dust motes drift in the light. The air smells of old paper and beeswax. Valia is here-- a slender dragon, smaller than Zexir or Raln, with deep blue scales and a ridge of silver running along her spine. Delicate, almost fragile-looking for a dragon. And perched on her snout: a pair of wire-rimmed spectacles. She looks up from a scroll and regards you over the spectacle rims. &quot;Ah. The Magister.&quot; Her voice is dry, precise, like the rustle of old paper. &quot;Zexir told me about you. Raln told me about you. Even Hran told me about you. I suppose I was the only one left.&quot; She sets down her quill. &quot;I am Valia. I keep the records. And you have questions. Everyone always does.&quot; [[Continue.->ValiaChat]]Valia settles onto a reading cushion, her long tail curling around her feet. She adjusts her spectacles and peers at you expectantly. &quot;Ask, then. I do have work to do.&quot; [[Ask about the island&#x27;s history.->ValiaChatc1]] [[Ask about Magisters.->ValiaChatc2]] [[Ask about herself.->ValiaChatc3]] (if:$IoDQ1D is 1)[[[Ask about the Dark Dragon (Korrath).->ValiaChatc4]]](else:)[<!--Do Nothing-->] [[Leave.->IoDHub]]&quot;The Isle was founded 200 years ago by dragons fleeing the war. This island was the compromise.&quot; She looks out the window toward the ocean. &quot;We have stayed hidden because the world is still not kind to dragons. But we cannot hide forever. The Hooded Mage proved that when he took Korrath.&quot; [[Ask something else.->ValiaChat]]&quot;Magisters and Dragons have a long history. Not all of it good.&quot; She pulls a scroll from a high shelf without getting up-- her tail reaches it. &quot;In the old days, Magisters served as mediators between Dragons and Nomads. They could speak to both. Understand both. They kept the peace.&quot; Her eyes fix on you. &quot;The last Magister who visited this island left under suspicious circumstances. That was decades ago. Before your time. Before mine, even.&quot; &quot;I don&#x27;t trust easily, child. But Zexir vouches for you, and his judgement has kept us alive this long.&quot; [[Ask something else.->ValiaChat]]Valia&#x27;s expression flickers-- surprise, maybe. Nobody asks about her. &quot;I am the oldest dragon on this island. Older than Zexir by... well. A lady doesn&#x27;t tell.&quot; A dry smile. &quot;I chose records over warfare. I have seen too much death to add to it. I would rather preserve what we have.&quot; She taps her spectacles. &quot;And my role is &#x27;unspecified&#x27; because I refused to let Raln put &#x27;Librarian&#x27; on the official record. She pulls the lineage chart from earlier. &quot;He was headstrong. Restless. Wanted to see the world beyond the island. And now he is a slave, controlled by a Magister who should never have had that power.&quot; She looks at you over her spectacles. &quot;Your kind can command. But command without consent is slavery. The bottom-- who you now see clearly-- is slimmer, younger, with matching scales but a more delicate build. Note: This is an Adult interactive story. You must be over the age of 18 to play.

Current Version: 0.41 - - Click here to view the patch notes.


A big thank you to LupusDaemon, Huzz, Endelphia, Chopper, SpazzleDusky, Riggles Wiggles, Jdrednaz, Commander Wilhelm and Shorty for playtesting this version, and a big thank you to everyone for supporting it! <3

If you enjoyed this game and wish to support its development, you can donate directly to my Ko-fi page.

General
  • Relationship hubs have been added.
    • Relationships are available for Wyatt, Hans, and Misfit.
    • For now, only Misfit is fully fleshed out. Sorry.
  • A main quest has been added. Find it at Scarhaven.
  • RPG Statistics for Kami have been added.
    • You can find detailed statistic at any Inn or resting place.
  • Added a new hub town: The Isle of Dragons.
    • Added 6 new sex scenes and 4 new notable characters.
    • Added 2 new spells to be learned.
    • You can access this new area through a special quest in Scarhaven.
  • A scene for the playtesting contest winner has been added.
    • Find it at Holstein's Hub.
  • Added a sidebar for your inventory.
    • Known Issue: Clicking too many inventory items will have it roll off the screen. To circumvent this, go out and back into your inventory.
  • You now have a Spell book to view your learned spells.
  • Multiple Save Slots are now available. (1->3)
  • Added a secret sex scene. Good luck finding it!
  • Added a Heart Ring item to view your curent relationship status.

Bug Fixes
    • Fixed an issue where Kami's text was too bolded on his equipment passage.
    • Fixed an issue where Hans would continually introduce himself.
    • Fixed an issue where Dillan kept introducing himself.
    • Fixed several issues that users encountered.
    • Fixed several spelling errors.






If you encounter an error, click here to print the passage name.
